Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: import fetchMock from \\\"fetch-mock-jest\\\";\\n18: \\n19: import * as languageHandler from \\\"../../src/languageHandler\\\";\\n20: import en from \\\"../../src/i18n/strings/en_EN.json\\\";\\n21: import de from \\\"../../src/i18n/strings/de_DE.json\\\";\\n22: \\n23: const lv = {\\n24:     \\\"Save\\\": \\\"Saglab\u0101t\\\",\\n25:     \\\"Uploading %(filename)s and %(count)s others|one\\\": \\\"\u041a\u0430\u0447\u0432\u0430\u043d\u0435 \u043d\u0430 %(filename)s \u0438 %(count)s \u0434\u0440\u0443\u0433\\\",\\n26: };\\n27: \\n28: // Fake languages.json containing references to en_EN, de_DE and lv\\n29: // en_EN.json\\n30: // de_DE.json\\n31: // lv.json - mock version with few translations, used to test fallback translation\\n32: \\n33: function weblateToCounterpart(inTrs: object): object {\\n34:     const outTrs = {};\\n35: \\n36:     for (const key of Object.keys(inTrs)) {\\n37:         const keyParts = key.split('|', 2);\\n38:         if (keyParts.length === 2) {\\n39:             let obj = outTrs[keyParts[0]];\\n40:             if (obj === undefined) {\\n41:                 obj = outTrs[keyParts[0]] = {};\\n42:             } else if (typeof obj === \\\"string\\\") {\\n43:                 // This is a transitional edge case if a string went from singular to pluralised and both still remain\\n44:                 // in the translation json file. Use the singular translation as `other` and merge pluralisation atop.\\n45:                 obj = outTrs[keyParts[0]] = {\\n46:                     \\\"other\\\": inTrs[key],\\n47:                 };\\n48:                 console.warn(\\\"Found entry in i18n file in both singular and pluralised form\\\", keyParts[0]);\\n49:             }\\n50:             obj[keyParts[1]] = inTrs[key];\\n51:         } else {\\n52:             outTrs[key] = inTrs[key];\\n53:         }\\n54:     }\\n55: \\n56:     return outTrs;\\n57: }\\n58: \\n59: fetchMock\\n60:     .get(\\\"/i18n/languages.json\\\", {\\n61:         \\\"en\\\": {\\n62:             \\\"fileName\\\": \\\"en_EN.json\\\",\\n63:             \\\"label\\\": \\\"English\\\",\\n64:         },\\n65:         \\\"de\\\": {\\n66:             \\\"fileName\\\": \\\"de_DE.json\\\",\\n67:             \\\"label\\\": \\\"German\\\",\\n68:         },\\n69:         \\\"lv\\\": {\\n70:             \\\"fileName\\\": \\\"lv.json\\\",\\n71:             \\\"label\\\": \\\"Latvian\\\",\\n72:         },\\n73:     })\\n74:     .get(\\\"end:en_EN.json\\\", weblateToCounterpart(en))\\n75:     .get(\\\"end:de_DE.json\\\", weblateToCounterpart(de))\\n76:     .get(\\\"end:lv.json\\\", weblateToCounterpart(lv));\\n77: \\n78: languageHandler.setLanguage('en');\\n79: languageHandler.setMissingEntryGenerator(key => key.split(\\\"|\\\", 2)[1]);\\n80: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/test/setup/setupLanguage.ts\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: import fetchMock from \"fetch-mock-jest\";\n18: \n19: import * as languageHandler from \"../../src/languageHandler\";\n20: import en from \"../../src/i18n/strings/en_EN.json\";\n21: import de from \"../../src/i18n/strings/de_DE.json\";\n22: \n23: const lv = {\n24:     \"Save\": \"Saglab\u0101t\",\n25:     \"Uploading %(filename)s and %(count)s others|one\": \"\u041a\u0430\u0447\u0432\u0430\u043d\u0435 \u043d\u0430 %(filename)s \u0438 %(count)s \u0434\u0440\u0443\u0433\",\n26: };\n27: \n28: // Fake languages.json containing references to en_EN, de_DE and lv\n29: // en_EN.json\n30: // de_DE.json\n31: // lv.json - mock version with few translations, used to test fallback translation\n32: \n33: function weblateToCounterpart(inTrs: object): object {\n34:     const outTrs = {};\n35: \n36:     for (const key of Object.keys(inTrs)) {\n37:         const keyParts = key.split('|', 2);\n38:         if (keyParts.length === 2) {\n39:             let obj = outTrs[keyParts[0]];\n40:             if (obj === undefined) {\n41:                 obj = outTrs[keyParts[0]] = {};\n42:             } else if (typeof obj === \"string\") {\n43:                 // This is a transitional edge case if a string went from singular to pluralised and both still remain\n44:                 // in the translation json file. Use the singular translation as `other` and merge pluralisation atop.\n45:                 obj = outTrs[keyParts[0]] = {\n46:                     \"other\": inTrs[key],\n47:                 };\n48:                 console.warn(\"Found entry in i18n file in both singular and pluralised form\", keyParts[0]);\n49:             }\n50:             obj[keyParts[1]] = inTrs[key];\n51:         } else {\n52:             outTrs[key] = inTrs[key];\n53:         }\n54:     }\n55: \n56:     return outTrs;\n57: }\n58: \n59: fetchMock\n60:     .get(\"/i18n/languages.json\", {\n61:         \"en\": {\n62:             \"fileName\": \"en_EN.json\",\n63:             \"label\": \"English\",\n64:         },\n65:         \"de\": {\n66:             \"fileName\": \"de_DE.json\",\n67:             \"label\": \"German\",\n68:         },\n69:         \"lv\": {\n70:             \"fileName\": \"lv.json\",\n71:             \"label\": \"Latvian\",\n72:         },\n73:     })\n74:     .get(\"end:en_EN.json\", weblateToCounterpart(en))\n75:     .get(\"end:de_DE.json\", weblateToCounterpart(de))\n76:     .get(\"end:lv.json\", weblateToCounterpart(lv));\n77: \n78: languageHandler.setLanguage('en');\n79: languageHandler.setMissingEntryGenerator(key => key.split(\"|\", 2)[1]);\n80: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/test/setup/setupLanguage.ts"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n7: \\n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n9: \\n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: import EventEmitter from \\\"events\\\";\\n18: import { MethodLikeKeys, mocked, MockedObject, PropertyLikeKeys } from \\\"jest-mock\\\";\\n19: import { Feature, ServerSupport } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/feature\\\";\\n20: import { MatrixClient, User } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\\\";\\n21: \\n22: import { MatrixClientPeg } from \\\"../../src/MatrixClientPeg\\\";\\n23: \\n24: /**\\n25:  * Mocked generic class with a real EventEmitter.\\n26:  * Useful for mocks which need event emitters.\\n27:  */\\n28: export class MockEventEmitter<T> extends EventEmitter {\\n29:     /**\\n30:      * Construct a new event emitter with additional properties/functions. The event emitter functions\\n31:      * like .emit and .on will be real.\\n32:      * @param mockProperties An object with the mock property or function implementations. 'getters'\\n33:      * are correctly cloned to this event emitter.\\n34:      */\\n35:     constructor(mockProperties: Partial<Record<MethodLikeKeys<T>|PropertyLikeKeys<T>, unknown>> = {}) {\\n36:         super();\\n37:         // We must use defineProperties and not assign as the former clones getters correctly,\\n38:         // whereas the latter invokes the getter and sets the return value permanently on the\\n39:         // destination object.\\n40:         Object.defineProperties(this, Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptors(mockProperties));\\n41:     }\\n42: }\\n43: \\n44: /**\\n45:  * Mock client with real event emitter\\n46:  * useful for testing code that listens\\n47:  * to MatrixClient events\\n48:  */\\n49: export class MockClientWithEventEmitter extends EventEmitter {\\n50:     constructor(mockProperties: Partial<Record<MethodLikeKeys<MatrixClient>, unknown>> = {}) {\\n51:         super();\\n52: \\n53:         Object.assign(this, mockProperties);\\n54:     }\\n55: }\\n56: \\n57: /**\\n58:  * - make a mock client\\n59:  * - cast the type to mocked(MatrixClient)\\n60:  * - spy on MatrixClientPeg.get to return the mock\\n61:  * eg\\n62:  * ```\\n63:  * const mockClient = getMockClientWithEventEmitter({\\n64:         getUserId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(aliceId),\\n65:     });\\n66:  * ```\\n67:  */\\n68: export const getMockClientWithEventEmitter = (\\n69:     mockProperties: Partial<Record<MethodLikeKeys<MatrixClient>, unknown>>,\\n70: ): MockedObject<MatrixClient> => {\\n71:     const mock = mocked(new MockClientWithEventEmitter(mockProperties) as unknown as MatrixClient);\\n72: \\n73:     jest.spyOn(MatrixClientPeg, 'get').mockReturnValue(mock);\\n74: \\n75:     // @ts-ignore simplified test stub\\n76:     mock.canSupport = new Map();\\n77:     Object.keys(Feature).forEach(feature => {\\n78:         mock.canSupport.set(feature as Feature, ServerSupport.Stable);\\n79:     });\\n80:     return mock;\\n81: };\\n82: \\n83: export const unmockClientPeg = () => jest.spyOn(MatrixClientPeg, 'get').mockRestore();\\n84: \\n85: /**\\n86:  * Returns basic mocked client methods related to the current user\\n87:  * ```\\n88:  * const mockClient = getMockClientWithEventEmitter({\\n89:         ...mockClientMethodsUser('@mytestuser:domain'),\\n90:     });\\n91:  * ```\\n92:  */\\n93: export const mockClientMethodsUser = (userId = '@alice:domain') => ({\\n94:     getUserId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(userId),\\n95:     getUser: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(new User(userId)),\\n96:     isGuest: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n97:     mxcUrlToHttp: jest.fn().mockReturnValue('mock-mxcUrlToHttp'),\\n98:     credentials: { userId },\\n99:     getThreePids: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ threepids: [] }),\\n100:     getAccessToken: jest.fn(),\\n101:     getDeviceId: jest.fn(),\\n102:     getAccountData: jest.fn(),\\n103: });\\n104: \\n105: /**\\n106:  * Returns basic mocked client methods related to rendering events\\n107:  * ```\\n108:  * const mockClient = getMockClientWithEventEmitter({\\n109:         ...mockClientMethodsUser('@mytestuser:domain'),\\n110:     });\\n111:  * ```\\n112:  */\\n113: export const mockClientMethodsEvents = () => ({\\n114:     decryptEventIfNeeded: jest.fn(),\\n115:     getPushActionsForEvent: jest.fn(),\\n116: });\\n117: \\n118: /**\\n119:  * Returns basic mocked client methods related to server support\\n120:  */\\n121: export const mockClientMethodsServer = (): Partial<Record<MethodLikeKeys<MatrixClient>, unknown>> => ({\\n122:     doesServerSupportSeparateAddAndBind: jest.fn(),\\n123:     getIdentityServerUrl: jest.fn(),\\n124:     getHomeserverUrl: jest.fn(),\\n125:     getCapabilities: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({}),\\n126:     getClientWellKnown: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({}),\\n127:     doesServerSupportUnstableFeature: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(false),\\n128:     getVersions: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n129:     isFallbackICEServerAllowed: jest.fn(),\\n130: });\\n131: \\n132: export const mockClientMethodsDevice = (\\n133:     deviceId = 'test-device-id',\\n134: ): Partial<Record<MethodLikeKeys<MatrixClient>, unknown>> => ({\\n135:     getDeviceId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(deviceId),\\n136:     getDeviceEd25519Key: jest.fn(),\\n137:     getDevices: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ devices: [] }),\\n138: });\\n139: \\n140: export const mockClientMethodsCrypto = (): Partial<Record<\\n141:     MethodLikeKeys<MatrixClient> & PropertyLikeKeys<MatrixClient>, unknown>\\n142: > => ({\\n143:     isCryptoEnabled: jest.fn(),\\n144:     isSecretStorageReady: jest.fn(),\\n145:     isCrossSigningReady: jest.fn(),\\n146:     isKeyBackupKeyStored: jest.fn(),\\n147:     getCrossSigningCacheCallbacks: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({ getCrossSigningKeyCache: jest.fn() }),\\n148:     getStoredCrossSigningForUser: jest.fn(),\\n149:     checkKeyBackup: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({}),\\n150:     crypto: {\\n151:         getSessionBackupPrivateKey: jest.fn(),\\n152:         secretStorage: { hasKey: jest.fn() },\\n153:         crossSigningInfo: {\\n154:             getId: jest.fn(),\\n155:             isStoredInSecretStorage: jest.fn(),\\n156:         },\\n157:     },\\n158: });\\n159: \\n160:     "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: import EventEmitter from \"events\";\n18: import { MethodLikeKeys, mocked, MockedObject, PropertyLikeKeys } from \"jest-mock\";\n19: import { Feature, ServerSupport } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/feature\";\n20: import { MatrixClient, User } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix\";\n21: \n22: import { MatrixClientPeg } from \"../../src/MatrixClientPeg\";\n23: \n24: /**\n25:  * Mocked generic class with a real EventEmitter.\n26:  * Useful for mocks which need event emitters.\n27:  */\n28: export class MockEventEmitter<T> extends EventEmitter {\n29:     /**\n30:      * Construct a new event emitter with additional properties/functions. 'getters'\n33:      * are correctly cloned to this event emitter.\n34:      */\n35:     constructor(mockProperties: Partial<Record<MethodLikeKeys<T>|PropertyLikeKeys<T>, unknown>> = {}) {\n36: event emitter\n46:  * useful for testing code that listens\n47:  * to MatrixClient events\n48:  */\n49: export class MockClientWithEventEmitter extends EventEmitter {\n50: as MatrixClient);\n72: \n73:     jest.spyOn(MatrixClientPeg, 'get').mockReturnValue(mock);\n74: \n75:     // @ts-ignore simplified test stub\n76:     mock.canSupport = new Map();\n77: = () => jest.spyOn(MatrixClientPeg, 'get').mockRestore();\n84: \n85: /**\n86:  * Returns basic mocked client methods related to the current user\n87:  * ```\n88:  * const mockClient = getMockClientWithEventEmitter({\n89:         ...mockClientMethodsUser('@mytestuser:domain'),\n90:     });\n91:  * ```\n92:  */\n93: export const mockClientMethodsUser = (userId = '@alice:domain') => ({\n94:     getUserId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(userId),\n95:     getUser: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(new User(userId)),\n96:     isGuest: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\n97:     mxcUrlToHttp: jest.fn().mockReturnValue('mock-mxcUrlToHttp'),\n98:     credentials: { userId },\n99:     getThreePids: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ threepids: [] }),\n100:     getAccessToken: jest.fn(),\n101: jest.fn(),\n124:     getHomeserverUrl: jest.fn(),\n125:     getCapabilities: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({}),\n126:     getClientWellKnown: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({}),\n127:   "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\\n3: \\n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n5: you may not use this file in writing, software\\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n14: limitations under the License.\\n15: */\\n16: \\n17: import EventEmitter from \\\"events\\\";\\n18: import { mocked, MockedObject } from 'jest-mock';\\n19: import { MatrixEvent } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event\\\";\\n20: import { JoinRule } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/@types/partials';\\n21: import {\\n22:     Room,\\n23:     User,\\n24:     IContent,\\n25:     IEvent,\\n26:     RoomMember,\\n27:     MatrixClient,\\n28:     EventTimeline,\\n29:     RoomState,\\n30:     EventType,\\n31:     IEventRelation,\\n32:     IUnsigned,\\n33:     IPusher,\\n34:     RoomType,\\n35:     KNOWN_SAFE_ROOM_VERSION,\\n36: } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix';\\n37: import { normalize } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/utils\\\";\\n38: import { ReEmitter } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/ReEmitter\\\";\\n39: import { MediaHandler } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/webrtc/mediaHandler\\\";\\n40: import { Feature, ServerSupport } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/feature\\\";\\n41: \\n42: import { MatrixClientPeg as peg } from '../../src/MatrixClientPeg';\\n43: import { makeType } from \\\"../../src/utils/TypeUtils\\\";\\n44: import { ValidatedServerConfig } from \\\"../../src/utils/ValidatedServerConfig\\\";\\n45: import { EnhancedMap } from \\\"../../src/utils/maps\\\";\\n46: import { AsyncStoreWithClient } from \\\"../../src/stores/AsyncStoreWithClient\\\";\\n47: import MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler from \\\"../../src/settings/handlers/MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler\\\";\\n48: \\n49: /**\\n50:  * Stub out the MatrixClient, and configure the MatrixClientPeg object to\\n51:  * return it when get() is called.\\n52:  *\\n53:  * TODO: once the components are updated to get their MatrixClients from\\n54:  * the react context, we can get rid of this and just inject a test client\\n55:  * via the context instead.\\n56:  */\\n57: export function stubClient(): MatrixClient {\\n58:     const client = createTestClient();\\n59: \\n60:     // stub out the methods in MatrixClientPeg\\n61:     //\\n62:     // 'sandbox.restore()' doesn't work correctly on inherited methods,\\n63:     // so we do this for each method\\n64:     jest.spyOn(peg, 'get');\\n65:     jest.spyOn(peg, 'unset');\\n66:     jest.spyOn(peg, 'replaceUsingCreds');\\n67:     // MatrixClientPeg.get() is called a /lot/, so implement it with our own\\n68:     // fast stub function rather than a sinon stub\\n69:     peg.get = function() { return client; };\\n70:     MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler.matrixClient = client;\\n71:     return client;\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: /**\\n75:  * Create a stubbed-out MatrixClient\\n76:  *\\n77:  * @returns {object} MatrixClient stub\\n78:  */\\n79: export function createTestClient(): MatrixClient {\\n80:     const eventEmitter = new EventEmitter();\\n81:     let txnId = 1;\\n82: \\n83:     const client = {\\n84:         getHomeserverUrl: jest.fn(),\\n85:         getIdentityServerUrl: jest.fn(),\\n86:         getDomain: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\\\"matrix.org\\\"),\\n87:         getUserId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\\\"@userId:matrix.org\\\"),\\n88:         getUser: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({ on: jest.fn() }),\\n89:         getDeviceId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\\\"ABCDEFGHI\\\"),\\n90:         deviceId: \\\"ABCDEFGHI\\\",\\n91:         getDevices: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ devices: [{ device_id: \\\"ABCDEFGHI\\\" }] }),\\n92:         credentials: { userId: \\\"@userId:matrix.org\\\" },\\n93: \\n94:         store: {\\n95:             getPendingEvents: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue([]),\\n96:             setPendingEvents: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n97:             storeRoom: jest.fn(),\\n98:             removeRoom: jest.fn(),\\n99:         },\\n100: \\n101:         crypto: {\\n102:             deviceList: {\\n103:                 downloadKeys: jest.fn(),\\n104:             },\\n105:         },\\n106: \\n107:         getPushActionsForEvent: jest.fn(),\\n108:         getRoom: jest.fn().mockImplementation(roomId => mkStubRoom(roomId, \\\"My room\\\", client)),\\n109:         getRooms: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n110:         getVisibleRooms: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n111:         loginFlows: jest.fn(),\\n112:         on: eventEmitter.on.bind(eventEmitter),\\n113:         off: eventEmitter.off.bind(eventEmitter),\\n114:         removeListener: eventEmitter.removeListener.bind(eventEmitter),\\n115:         emit: eventEmitter.emit.bind(eventEmitter),\\n116:         isRoomEncrypted: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n117:         peekInRoom: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(mkStubRoom(undefined, undefined, undefined)),\\n118:         stopPeeking: jest.fn(),\\n119: \\n120:         paginateEventTimeline: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n121:         sendReadReceipt: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n122:         getRoomIdForAlias: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n123:         getRoomDirectoryVisibility: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n124:         getProfileInfo: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n125:         getThirdpartyProtocols: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n126:         getClientWellKnown: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(null),\\n127:         supportsVoip: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n128:         getTurnServers: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n129:         getTurnServersExpiry: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(2 ^ 32),\\n130:         getThirdpartyUser: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue([]),\\n131:         getAccountData: (type) => {\\n132:             return mkEvent({\\n133:                 user: undefined,\\n134:                 room: undefined,\\n135:                 type,\\n136:                 event: true,\\n137:                 content: {},\\n138:             });\\n139:         },\\n140:         mxcUrlToHttp: (mxc) => `http://this.is.a.url/${mxc.substring(6)}`,\\n141:         setAccountData: jest.fn(),\\n142:         setRoomAccountData: jest.fn(),\\n143:         setRoomTopic: jest.fn(),\\n144:         setRoomReadMarkers: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n145:         sendTyping: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n146:         sendMessage: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n147:         sendStateEvent: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n148:         getSyncState: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\\\"SYNCING\\\"),\\n149:         generateClientSecret: () => \\\"t35tcl1Ent5ECr3T\\\",\\n150:         isGuest: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n151:         getRoomHierarchy: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({\\n152:             rooms: [],\\n153:         }),\\n154:         createRoom: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ room_id: \\\"!1:example.org\\\" }),\\n155:         setPowerLevel: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n156:         pushRules: {},\\n157:         decryptEventIfNeeded: () => Promise.resolve(),\\n158:         isUserIgnored: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n159:         getCapabilities: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n160:         supportsExperimentalThreads: () => false,\\n161:         getRoomUpgradeHistory: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n162:         getOpenIdToken: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n163:         registerWithIdentityServer: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n164:         getIdentityAccount: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\\n165:         getTerms: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ policies: [] }),\\n166:         doesServerSupportUnstableFeature: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n167:         isVersionSupported: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n168:         getPushRules: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n169:         getPushers: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ pushers: [] }),\\n170:         getThreePids: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ threepids: [] }),\\n171:         setPusher: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n172:         setPushRuleEnabled: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n173:         setPushRuleActions: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n174:         relations: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({\\n175:             events: [],\\n176:         }),\\n177:         isCryptoEnabled: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n178:         hasLazyLoadMembersEnabled: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n179:         isInitialSyncComplete: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n180:         downloadKeys: jest.fn(),\\n181:         fetchRoomEvent: jest.fn(),\\n182:         makeTxnId: jest.fn().mockImplementation(() => `t${txnId++}`),\\n183:         sendToDevice: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n184:         queueToDevice: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n185:         encryptAndSendToDevices: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\\n186:         cancelPendingEvent: jest.fn(),\\n187: \\n188:         getMediaHandler: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({\\n189:             setVideoInput: jest.fn(),\\n190:             setAudioInput: jest.fn(),\\n191:             setAudioSettings: jest.fn(),\\n192:         } as unknown as MediaHandler),\\n193:         uploadContent: jest.fn(),\\n194:         getEventMapper: () => (opts) => new MatrixEvent(opts),\\n195:         leaveRoomChain: jest.fn(roomId => ({ [roomId]: null })),\\n196:     } as unknown as MatrixClient;\\n197: \\n198:     client.reEmitter = new ReEmitter(client);\\n199: \\n200:     client.canSupport = new Map();\\n201:     Object.keys(Feature).forEach(feature => {\\n202:         client.canSupport.set(feature as Feature, ServerSupport.Stable);\\n203:     });\\n204: \\n205:     Object.defineProperty(client, \\\"pollingTurnServers\\\", {\\n206:         configurable: true,\\n207:         get: () => true,\\n208:     });\\n209:     return client;\\n210: }\\n211: \\n212: type MakeEventPassThruProps = {\\n213:     user: User[\\\"userId\\\"];\\n214:     relatesTo?: IEventRelation;\\n215:     event?: boolean;\\n216:     ts?: number;\\n217:     skey?: string;\\n218: };\\n219: type MakeEventProps = MakeEventPassThruProps & {\\n220:     type: string;\\n221:     redacts?: string;\\n222:     content: IContent;\\n223:     room?: Room[\\\"roomId\\\"]; // to-device messages are roomless\\n224:     // eslint-disable-next-line camelcase\\n225:     prev_content?: IContent;\\n226:     unsigned?: IUnsigned;\\n227: };\\n228: \\n229: export const mkRoomCreateEvent = (userId: string, roomId: string): MatrixEvent => {\\n230:     return mkEvent({\\n231:         event: true,\\n232:         type: EventType.RoomCreate,\\n233:         content: {\\n234:             creator: userId,\\n235:             room_version: KNOWN_SAFE_ROOM_VERSION,\\n236:         },\\n237:         skey: \\\"\\\",\\n238:         user: userId,\\n239:         room: roomId,\\n240:     });\\n241: };\\n242: \\n243: /**\\n244:  * Create an Event.\\n245:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the event.\\n246:  * @param {string} opts.type The event.type\\n247:  * @param {string} opts.room The event.room_id\\n248:  * @param {string} opts.user The event.user_id\\n249:  * @param {string=} opts.skey Optional. The state key (auto inserts empty string)\\n250:  * @param {number=} opts.ts   Optional. Timestamp for the event\\n251:  * @param {Object} opts.content The event.content\\n252:  * @param {boolean} opts.event True to make a MatrixEvent.\\n253:  * @param {unsigned=} opts.unsigned\\n254:  * @return {Object} a JSON object representing this event.\\n255:  */\\n256: export function mkEvent(opts: MakeEventProps): MatrixEvent {\\n257:     if (!opts.type || !opts.content) {\\n258:         throw new Error(\\\"Missing .type or .content =>\\\" + JSON.stringify(opts));\\n259:     }\\n260:     const event: Partial<IEvent> = {\\n261:         type: opts.type,\\n262:         room_id: opts.room,\\n263:         sender: opts.user,\\n264:         content: opts.content,\\n265:         prev_content: opts.prev_content,\\n266:         event_id: \\\"$\\\" + Math.random() + \\\"-\\\" + Math.random(),\\n267:         origin_server_ts: opts.ts ?? 0,\\n268:         unsigned: opts.unsigned,\\n269:         redacts: opts.redacts,\\n270:     };\\n271:     if (opts.skey !== undefined) {\\n272:         event.state_key = opts.skey;\\n273:     } else if ([\\n274:         \\\"m.room.name\\\", \\\"m.room.topic\\\", \\\"m.room.create\\\", \\\"m.room.join_rules\\\",\\n275:         \\\"m.room.power_levels\\\", \\\"m.room.topic\\\", \\\"m.room.history_visibility\\\",\\n276:         \\\"m.room.encryption\\\", \\\"m.room.member\\\", \\\"com.example.state\\\",\\n277:         \\\"m.room.guest_access\\\", \\\"m.room.tombstone\\\",\\n278:     ].indexOf(opts.type) !== -1) {\\n279:         event.state_key = \\\"\\\";\\n280:     }\\n281: \\n282:     const mxEvent = opts.event ? new MatrixEvent(event) : event as unknown as MatrixEvent;\\n283:     if (!mxEvent.sender && opts.user && opts.room) {\\n284:         mxEvent.sender = {\\n285:             userId: opts.user,\\n286:             membership: \\\"join\\\",\\n287:             name: opts.user,\\n288:             rawDisplayName: opts.user,\\n289:             roomId: opts.room,\\n290:             getAvatarUrl: () => {},\\n291:             getMxcAvatarUrl: () => {},\\n292:         } as unknown as RoomMember;\\n293:     }\\n294:     return mxEvent;\\n295: }\\n296: \\n297: /**\\n298:  * Create an m.presence event.\\n299:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the presence.\\n300:  * @return {Object|MatrixEvent} The event\\n301:  */\\n302: export function mkPresence(opts) {\\n303:     if (!opts.user) {\\n304:         throw new Error(\\\"Missing user\\\");\\n305:     }\\n306:     const event = {\\n307:         event_id: \\\"$\\\" + Math.random() + \\\"-\\\" + Math.random(),\\n308:         type: \\\"m.presence\\\",\\n309:         sender: opts.user,\\n310:         content: {\\n311:             avatar_url: opts.url,\\n312:             displayname: opts.name,\\n313:             last_active_ago: opts.ago,\\n314:             presence: opts.presence || \\\"offline\\\",\\n315:         },\\n316:     };\\n317:     return opts.event ? new MatrixEvent(event) : event;\\n318: }\\n319: \\n320: /**\\n321:  * Create an m.room.member event.\\n322:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the membership.\\n323:  * @param {string} opts.room The room ID for the event.\\n324:  * @param {string} opts.mship The content.membership for the event.\\n325:  * @param {string} opts.prevMship The prev_content.membership for the event.\\n326:  * @param {number=} opts.ts   Optional. Timestamp for the event\\n327:  * @param {string} opts.user The user ID for the event.\\n328:  * @param {RoomMember} opts.target The target of the event.\\n329:  * @param {string=} opts.skey The other user ID for the event if applicable\\n330:  * e.g. for invites/bans.\\n331:  * @param {string} opts.name The content.displayname for the event.\\n332:  * @param {string=} opts.url The content.avatar_url for the event.\\n333:  * @param {boolean} opts.event True to make a MatrixEvent.\\n334:  * @return {Object|MatrixEvent} The event\\n335:  */\\n336: export function mkMembership(opts: MakeEventPassThruProps & {\\n337:     room: Room[\\\"roomId\\\"];\\n338:     mship: string;\\n339:     prevMship?: string;\\n340:     name?: string;\\n341:     url?: string;\\n342:     skey?: string;\\n343:     target?: RoomMember;\\n344: }): MatrixEvent {\\n345:     const event: MakeEventProps = {\\n346:         ...opts,\\n347:         type: \\\"m.room.member\\\",\\n348:         content: {\\n349:             membership: opts.mship,\\n350:         },\\n351:     };\\n352:     if (!opts.skey) {\\n353:         event.skey = opts.user;\\n354:     }\\n355:     if (!opts.mship) {\\n356:         throw new Error(\\\"Missing .mship => \\\" + JSON.stringify(opts));\\n357:     }\\n358: \\n359:     if (opts.prevMship) {\\n360:         event.prev_content = { membership: opts.prevMship };\\n361:     }\\n362:     if (opts.name) { event.content.displayname = opts.name; }\\n363:     if (opts.url) { event.content.avatar_url = opts.url; }\\n364:     const e = mkEvent(event);\\n365:     if (opts.target) {\\n366:         e.target = opts.target;\\n367:     }\\n368:     return e;\\n369: }\\n370: \\n371: export function mkRoomMember(roomId: string, userId: string, membership = \\\"join\\\"): RoomMember {\\n372:     return {\\n373:         userId,\\n374:         membership,\\n375:         name: userId,\\n376:         rawDisplayName: userId,\\n377:         roomId,\\n378:         events: {},\\n379:         getAvatarUrl: () => {},\\n380:         getMxcAvatarUrl: () => {},\\n381:         getDMInviter: () => {},\\n382:     } as unknown as RoomMember;\\n383: }\\n384: \\n385: export type MessageEventProps = MakeEventPassThruProps & {\\n386:     room: Room[\\\"roomId\\\"];\\n387:     relatesTo?: IEventRelation;\\n388:     msg?: string;\\n389: };\\n390: \\n391: /**\\n392:  * Create an m.room.message event.\\n393:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the message\\n394:  * @param {string} opts.room The room ID for the event.\\n395:  * @param {string} opts.user The user ID for the event.\\n396:  * @param {number} opts.ts The timestamp for the event.\\n397:  * @param {boolean} opts.event True to make a MatrixEvent.\\n398:  * @param {string=} opts.msg Optional. The content.body for the event.\\n399:  * @return {Object|MatrixEvent} The event\\n400:  */\\n401: export function mkMessage({ msg, relatesTo, ...opts }: MakeEventPassThruProps & {\\n402:     room: Room[\\\"roomId\\\"];\\n403:     msg?: string;\\n404: }): MatrixEvent {\\n405:     if (!opts.room || !opts.user) {\\n406:         throw new Error(\\\"Missing .room or .user from options\\\");\\n407:     }\\n408:     const message = msg ?? \\\"Random->\\\" + Math.random();\\n409:     const event: MakeEventProps = {\\n410:         ...opts,\\n411:         type: \\\"m.room.message\\\",\\n412:         content: {\\n413:             msgtype: \\\"m.text\\\",\\n414:             body: message,\\n415:             ['m.relates_to']: relatesTo,\\n416:         },\\n417:     };\\n418: \\n419:     return mkEvent(event);\\n420: }\\n421: \\n422: export function mkStubRoom(roomId: string = null, name: string, client: MatrixClient): Room {\\n423:     const stubTimeline = { getEvents: () => [] } as unknown as EventTimeline;\\n424:     return {\\n425:         roomId,\\n426:         getReceiptsForEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n427:         getMember: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({\\n428:             userId: '@member:domain.bla',\\n429:             name: 'Member',\\n430:             rawDisplayName: 'Member',\\n431:             roomId: roomId,\\n432:             getAvatarUrl: () => 'mxc://avatar.url/image.png',\\n433:             getMxcAvatarUrl: () => 'mxc://avatar.url/image.png',\\n434:         }),\\n435:         getMembersWithMembership: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n436:         getJoinedMembers: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n437:         getJoinedMemberCount: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(1),\\n438:         getInvitedAndJoinedMemberCount: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(1),\\n439:         setUnreadNotificationCount: jest.fn(),\\n440:         getMembers: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n441:         getPendingEvents: () => [],\\n442:         getLiveTimeline: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(stubTimeline),\\n443:         getUnfilteredTimelineSet: jest.fn(),\\n444:         findEventById: () => null,\\n445:         getAccountData: () => null,\\n446:         hasMembershipState: () => null,\\n447:         getVersion: () => '1',\\n448:         shouldUpgradeToVersion: () => null,\\n449:         getMyMembership: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\\\"join\\\"),\\n450:         maySendMessage: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n451:         currentState: {\\n452:             getStateEvents: jest.fn(),\\n453:             getMember: jest.fn(),\\n454:             mayClientSendStateEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n455:             maySendStateEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n456:             maySendRedactionForEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n457:             maySendEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\\n458:             members: {},\\n459:             getJoinRule: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(JoinRule.Invite),\\n460:             on: jest.fn(),\\n461:             off: jest.fn(),\\n462:         } as unknown as RoomState,\\n463:         tags: {},\\n464:         setBlacklistUnverifiedDevices: jest.fn(),\\n465:         on: jest.fn(),\\n466:         off: jest.fn(),\\n467:         removeListener: jest.fn(),\\n468:         getDMInviter: jest.fn(),\\n469:         name,\\n470:         normalizedName: normalize(name || \\\"\\\"),\\n471:         getAvatarUrl: () => 'mxc://avatar.url/room.png',\\n472:         getMxcAvatarUrl: () => 'mxc://avatar.url/room.png',\\n473:         isSpaceRoom: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n474:         getType: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(undefined),\\n475:         isElementVideoRoom: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\\n476:         getUnreadNotificationCount: jest.fn(() => 0),\\n477:         getEventReadUpTo: jest.fn(() => null),\\n478:         getCanonicalAlias: jest.fn(),\\n479:         getAltAliases: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n480:         timeline: [],\\n481:         getJoinRule: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\\\"invite\\\"),\\n482:         loadMembersIfNeeded: jest.fn(),\\n483:         client,\\n484:         myUserId: client?.getUserId(),\\n485:         canInvite: jest.fn(),\\n486:         getThreads: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\\n487:         eventShouldLiveIn: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({}),\\n488:         createThreadsTimelineSets: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(new Promise(() => {})),\\n489:         fetchRoomThreads: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(new Promise(() => {})),\\n490:     } as unknown as Room;\\n491: }\\n492: \\n493: export function mkServerConfig(hsUrl, isUrl) {\\n494:     return makeType(ValidatedServerConfig, {\\n495:         hsUrl,\\n496:         hsName: \\\"TEST_ENVIRONMENT\\\",\\n497:         hsNameIsDifferent: false, // yes, we lie\\n498:         isUrl,\\n499:     });\\n500: }\\n501: \\n502: // These methods make some use of some private methods on the AsyncStoreWithClient to simplify getting into a consistent\\n503: // ready state without needing to wire up a dispatcher and pretend to be a js-sdk client.\\n504: \\n505: export const setupAsyncStoreWithClient = async <T = unknown>(store: AsyncStoreWithClient<T>, client: MatrixClient) => {\\n506:     // @ts-ignore\\n507:     store.readyStore.useUnitTestClient(client);\\n508:     // @ts-ignore\\n509:     await store.onReady();\\n510: };\\n511: \\n512: export const resetAsyncStoreWithClient = async <T = unknown>(store: AsyncStoreWithClient<T>) => {\\n513:     // @ts-ignore\\n514:     await store.onNotReady();\\n515: };\\n516: \\n517: export const mockStateEventImplementation = (events: MatrixEvent[]) => {\\n518:     const stateMap = new EnhancedMap<string, Map<string, MatrixEvent>>();\\n519:     events.forEach(event => {\\n520:         stateMap.getOrCreate(event.getType(), new Map()).set(event.getStateKey(), event);\\n521:     });\\n522: \\n523:     // recreate the overloading in RoomState\\n524:     function getStateEvents(eventType: EventType | string): MatrixEvent[];\\n525:     function getStateEvents(eventType: EventType | string, stateKey: string): MatrixEvent;\\n526:     function getStateEvents(eventType: EventType | string, stateKey?: string) {\\n527:         if (stateKey || stateKey === \\\"\\\") {\\n528:             return stateMap.get(eventType)?.get(stateKey) || null;\\n529:         }\\n530:         return Array.from(stateMap.get(eventType)?.values() || []);\\n531:     }\\n532:     return getStateEvents;\\n533: };\\n534: \\n535: export const mkRoom = (\\n536:     client: MatrixClient,\\n537:     roomId: string,\\n538:     rooms?: ReturnType<typeof mkStubRoom>[],\\n539: ): MockedObject<Room> => {\\n540:     const room = mocked(mkStubRoom(roomId, roomId, client));\\n541:     mocked(room.currentState).getStateEvents.mockImplementation(mockStateEventImplementation([]));\\n542:     rooms?.push(room);\\n543:     return room;\\n544: };\\n545: \\n546: /**\\n547:  * Upserts given events into room.currentState\\n548:  * @param room\\n549:  * @param events\\n550:  */\\n551: export const upsertRoomStateEvents = (room: Room, events: MatrixEvent[]): void => {\\n552:     const eventsMap = events.reduce((acc, event) => {\\n553:         const eventType = event.getType();\\n554:         if (!acc.has(eventType)) {\\n555:             acc.set(eventType, new Map());\\n556:         }\\n557:         acc.get(eventType).set(event.getStateKey(), event);\\n558:         return acc;\\n559:     }, room.currentState.events || new Map<string, Map<string, MatrixEvent>>());\\n560: \\n561:     room.currentState.events = eventsMap;\\n562: };\\n563: \\n564: export const mkSpace = (\\n565:     client: MatrixClient,\\n566:     spaceId: string,\\n567:     rooms?: ReturnType<typeof mkStubRoom>[],\\n568:     children: string[] = [],\\n569: ): MockedObject<Room> => {\\n570:     const space = mocked(mkRoom(client, spaceId, rooms));\\n571:     space.isSpaceRoom.mockReturnValue(true);\\n572:     space.getType.mockReturnValue(RoomType.Space);\\n573:     mocked(space.currentState).getStateEvents.mockImplementation(mockStateEventImplementation(children.map(roomId =>\\n574:         mkEvent({\\n575:             event: true,\\n576:             type: EventType.SpaceChild,\\n577:             room: spaceId,\\n578:             user: \\\"@user:server\\\",\\n579:             skey: roomId,\\n580:             content: { via: [] },\\n581:             ts: Date.now(),\\n582:         }),\\n583:     )));\\n584:     return space;\\n585: };\\n586: \\n587: export const mkRoomMemberJoinEvent = (user: string, room: string): MatrixEvent => {\\n588:     return mkEvent({\\n589:         event: true,\\n590:         type: EventType.RoomMember,\\n591:         content: {\\n592:             membership: \\\"join\\\",\\n593:         },\\n594:         skey: user,\\n595:         user,\\n596:         room,\\n597:     });\\n598: };\\n599: \\n600: export const mkPusher = (extra: Partial<IPusher> = {}): IPusher => ({\\n601:     app_display_name: \\\"app\\\",\\n602:     app_id: \\\"123\\\",\\n603:     data: {},\\n604:     device_display_name: \\\"name\\\",\\n605:     kind: \\\"http\\\",\\n606:     lang: \\\"en\\\",\\n607:     pushkey: \\\"pushpush\\\",\\n608:     ...extra,\\n609: });\\n610: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/test/test-utils/test-utils.ts\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n3: \n4: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n5: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n6: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n7: \n8:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n9: \n10: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n11: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n12: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n13: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n14: limitations under the License.\n15: */\n16: \n17: import EventEmitter from \"events\";\n18: import { mocked, MockedObject } from 'jest-mock';\n19: import { MatrixEvent } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event\";\n20: import { JoinRule } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/@types/partials';\n21: import {\n22:     Room,\n23:     User,\n24:     IContent,\n25:     IEvent,\n26:     RoomMember,\n27:     MatrixClient,\n28:     EventTimeline,\n29:     RoomState,\n30:     EventType,\n31:     IEventRelation,\n32:     IUnsigned,\n33:     IPusher,\n34:     RoomType,\n35:     KNOWN_SAFE_ROOM_VERSION,\n36: } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix';\n37: import { normalize } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/utils\";\n38: import { ReEmitter } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/ReEmitter\";\n39: import { MediaHandler } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/webrtc/mediaHandler\";\n40: import { Feature, ServerSupport } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/feature\";\n41: \n42: import { MatrixClientPeg as peg } from '../../src/MatrixClientPeg';\n43: import { makeType } from \"../../src/utils/TypeUtils\";\n44: import { ValidatedServerConfig } from \"../../src/utils/ValidatedServerConfig\";\n45: import { EnhancedMap } from \"../../src/utils/maps\";\n46: import { AsyncStoreWithClient } from \"../../src/stores/AsyncStoreWithClient\";\n47: import MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler from \"../../src/settings/handlers/MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler\";\n48: \n49: /**\n50:  * Stub out the MatrixClient, and configure the MatrixClientPeg object to\n51:  * return it when get() is called.\n52:  *\n53:  * TODO: once the components are updated to get their MatrixClients from\n54:  * the react context, we can get rid of this and just inject a test client\n55:  * via the context instead.\n56:  */\n57: export function stubClient(): MatrixClient {\n58:     const client = createTestClient();\n59: \n60:     // stub out the methods in MatrixClientPeg\n61:     //\n62:     // 'sandbox.restore()' doesn't work correctly on inherited methods,\n63:     // so we do this for each method\n64:     jest.spyOn(peg, 'get');\n65:     jest.spyOn(peg, 'unset');\n66:     jest.spyOn(peg, 'replaceUsingCreds');\n67:     // MatrixClientPeg.get() is called a /lot/, so implement it with our own\n68:     // fast stub function rather than a sinon stub\n69:     peg.get = function() { return client; };\n70:     MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler.matrixClient = client;\n71:     return client;\n72: }\n73: \n74: /**\n75:  * Create a stubbed-out MatrixClient\n76:  *\n77:  * @returns {object} MatrixClient stub\n78:  */\n79: export function createTestClient(): MatrixClient {\n80:     const eventEmitter = new EventEmitter();\n81:     let txnId = 1;\n82: \n83:     const client = {\n84:         getHomeserverUrl: jest.fn(),\n85:         getIdentityServerUrl: jest.fn(),\n86:         getDomain: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\"matrix.org\"),\n87:         getUserId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\"@userId:matrix.org\"),\n88:         getUser: jest.fn().mockReturnValue({ on: jest.fn() }),\n89:         getDeviceId: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\"ABCDEFGHI\"),\n90:         deviceId: \"ABCDEFGHI\",\n91:         getDevices: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ devices: [{ device_id: \"ABCDEFGHI\" }] }),\n92:         credentials: { userId: \"@userId:matrix.org\" },\n93: \n94:         store: {\n95:             getPendingEvents: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue([]),\n96:             setPendingEvents: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n97:             storeRoom: jest.fn(),\n98:             removeRoom: jest.fn(),\n99:         },\n100: \n101:         crypto: {\n102:             deviceList: {\n103:                 downloadKeys: jest.fn(),\n104:             },\n105:         },\n106: \n107:         getPushActionsForEvent: jest.fn(),\n108:         getRoom: jest.fn().mockImplementation(roomId => mkStubRoom(roomId, \"My room\", client)),\n109:         getRooms: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n110:         getVisibleRooms: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n111:         loginFlows: jest.fn(),\n112:         on: eventEmitter.on.bind(eventEmitter),\n113:         off: eventEmitter.off.bind(eventEmitter),\n114:         removeListener: eventEmitter.removeListener.bind(eventEmitter),\n115:         emit: eventEmitter.emit.bind(eventEmitter),\n116:         isRoomEncrypted: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\n117:         peekInRoom: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(mkStubRoom(undefined, undefined, undefined)),\n118:         stopPeeking: jest.fn(),\n119: \n120:         paginateEventTimeline: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n121:         sendReadReceipt: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n122:         getRoomIdForAlias: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n123:         getRoomDirectoryVisibility: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n124:         getProfileInfo: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n125:         getThirdpartyProtocols: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n126:         getClientWellKnown: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(null),\n127:         supportsVoip: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\n128:         getTurnServers: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n129:         getTurnServersExpiry: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(2 ^ 32),\n130:         getThirdpartyUser: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue([]),\n131:         getAccountData: (type) => {\n132:             return mkEvent({\n133:                 user: undefined,\n134:                 room: undefined,\n135:                 type,\n136:                 event: true,\n137:                 content: {},\n138:             });\n139:         },\n140:         mxcUrlToHttp: (mxc) => `http://this.is.a.url/${mxc.substring(6)}`,\n141:         setAccountData: jest.fn(),\n142:         setRoomAccountData: jest.fn(),\n143:         setRoomTopic: jest.fn(),\n144:         setRoomReadMarkers: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n145:         sendTyping: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n146:         sendMessage: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({}),\n147:         sendStateEvent: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue(undefined),\n148:         getSyncState: return client;\n210: }\n211: \n212: type MakeEventPassThruProps = {\n213:     user: User[\"userId\"];\n214:     relatesTo?: IEventRelation;\n215:     event?: boolean;\n216:     ts?: number;\n217:     skey?: string;\n218: };\n219: type MakeEventProps = MakeEventPassThruProps & {\n220:     type: string;\n221:     redacts?: string;\n222:     content: IContent;\n223:     room?: Room[\"roomId\"]; // to-device messages are roomless\n224:     // eslint-disable-next-line camelcase\n225:     prev_content?: IContent;\n226:     unsigned?: IUnsigned;\n227: };\n228: \n229: export const mkRoomCreateEvent = (userId: string, roomId: string): MatrixEvent => {\n230:     return mkEvent({\n231:         event: true,\n232:         type: EventType.RoomCreate,\n233:         content: {\n234:             creator: userId,\n235:             room_version: KNOWN_SAFE_ROOM_VERSION,\n236:         },\n237:         skey: \"\",\n238:         user: userId,\n239:         room: roomId,\n240:     });\n241: };\n242: \n243: /**\n244:  * Create an Event.\n245:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the event.\n246:  * @param {string} opts.type The event.type\n247:  * @param {string} opts.room The event.room_id\n248:  * @param {string} opts.user The event.user_id\n249:  * @param {string=} opts.skey Optional. The state key (auto inserts empty string)\n250:  * @param {number=} opts.ts   Optional. Timestamp for the event\n251:  * @param {Object} opts.content The event.content\n252:  * @param {boolean} opts.event True to make a MatrixEvent.\n253:  * @param {unsigned=} opts.unsigned\n254:  * @return {Object} a JSON object representing this event.\n255:  */\n256: export function mkEvent(opts: MakeEventProps): MatrixEvent {\n257:     if (!opts.type || !opts.content) {\n258:         throw new Error(\"Missing .type or .content =>\" + JSON.stringify(opts));\n259:     }\n260:     const event: Partial<IEvent> = {\n261:         type: opts.type,\n262:         room_id: opts.room,\n263:         sender: opts.user,\n264:         content: opts.content,\n265:         prev_content: opts.prev_content,\n266:         event_id: \"$\" + Math.random() + \"-\" + Math.random(),\n267:         origin_server_ts: opts.ts ?? 0,\n268:         unsigned: opts.unsigned,\n269:         redacts: opts.redacts,\n270:     };\n271:     if (opts.skey !== undefined) {\n272:         event.state_key = opts.skey;\n273:     } else if ([\n274:         \"m.room.name\", \"m.room.topic\", \"m.room.create\", \"m.room.join_rules\",\n275:         \"m.room.power_levels\", \"m.room.topic\", \"m.room.history_visibility\",\n276:         \"m.room.encryption\", \"m.room.member\", \"com.example.state\",\n277:         \"m.room.guest_access\", \"m.room.tombstone\",\n278:     ].indexOf(opts.type) !== -1) {\n279:         event.state_key = \"\";\n280:     }\n281: \n282:     const mxEvent = opts.event ? new MatrixEvent(event) : event as unknown as MatrixEvent;\n283:     if (!mxEvent.sender && opts.user && opts.room) {\n284:         mxEvent.sender = {\n285:             userId: opts.user,\n286:             membership: \"join\",\n287:             name: opts.user,\n288:             rawDisplayName: opts.user,\n289:             roomId: opts.room,\n290:             getAvatarUrl: () => {},\n291:             getMxcAvatarUrl: () => {},\n292:         } as unknown as RoomMember;\n293:     }\n294:     return mxEvent;\n295: }\n296: \n297: /**\n298:  * Create an m.presence event.\n299:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the presence.\n300:  * @return {Object|MatrixEvent} The event\n301:  */\n302: export function mkPresence(opts) {\n303:     if (!opts.user) {\n304:         throw new Error(\"Missing user\");\n305:     }\n306:     const event = {\n307:         event_id: \"$\" + Math.random() + \"-\" + Math.random(),\n308:         type: \"m.presence\",\n309:         sender: opts.user,\n310:         content: {\n311:             avatar_url: opts.url,\n312:             displayname: opts.name,\n313:             last_active_ago: opts.ago,\n314:             presence: opts.presence || \"offline\",\n315:         },\n316:     };\n317:     return opts.event ? new MatrixEvent(event) : event;\n318: }\n319: \n320: /**\n321:  * Create an m.room.member event.\n322:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the membership.\n323:  * @param {string} opts.room The room ID for the event.\n324:  * @param {string} opts.mship The content.membership for the event.\n325:  * @param {string} opts.prevMship The prev_content.membership for the event.\n326:  * @param {number=} opts.ts   Optional. Timestamp for the event\n327:  * @param {string} opts.user The user ID for the event.\n328:  * @param {RoomMember} opts.target The target of the event.\n329:  * @param {string=} opts.skey The other user ID for the event if applicable\n330:  * e.g. for invites/bans.\n331:  * @param {string} opts.name The content.displayname for the event.\n332:  * @param {string=} opts.url The content.avatar_url for the event.\n333:  * @param {boolean} opts.event True to make a MatrixEvent.\n334:  * @return {Object|MatrixEvent} The event\n335:  */\n336: export function mkMembership(opts: MakeEventPassThruProps & {\n337:     room: Room[\"roomId\"];\n338:     mship: string;\n339:     prevMship?: string;\n340:     name?: string;\n341:     url?: string;\n342:     skey?: string;\n343:     target?: RoomMember;\n344: }): MatrixEvent {\n345:     const event: MakeEventProps = {\n346:         ...opts,\n347:         type: \"m.room.member\",\n348:         content: {\n349:             membership: opts.mship,\n350:         },\n351:     };\n352:     if (!opts.skey) {\n353:         event.skey = opts.user;\n354:     }\n355:     if (!opts.mship) {\n356:         throw new Error(\"Missing .mship => \" + JSON.stringify(opts));\n357:     }\n358: \n359:     if (opts.prevMship) {\n360:         event.prev_content = { membership: opts.prevMship };\n361:     }\n362:     if (opts.name) { event.content.displayname = opts.name; }\n363:     if (opts.url) { event.content.avatar_url = opts.url; }\n364:     const e = mkEvent(event);\n365:     if (opts.target) {\n366:         e.target = opts.target;\n367:     }\n368:     return e;\n369: }\n370: \n371: export function mkRoomMember(roomId: string, userId: string, membership = \"join\"): RoomMember {\n372:     return {\n373:         userId,\n374:         membership,\n375:         name: userId,\n376:         rawDisplayName: userId,\n377:         roomId,\n378:         events: {},\n379:         getAvatarUrl: () => {},\n380:         getMxcAvatarUrl: () => {},\n381:         getDMInviter: () => {},\n382:     } as unknown as RoomMember;\n383: }\n384: \n385: export type MessageEventProps = MakeEventPassThruProps & {\n386:     room: Room[\"roomId\"];\n387:     relatesTo?: IEventRelation;\n388:     msg?: string;\n389: };\n390: \n391: /**\n392:  * Create an m.room.message event.\n393:  * @param {Object} opts Values for the message\n394:  * @param {string} opts.room The room ID for the event.\n395:  * @param {string} opts.user The user ID for the event.\n396:  * @param {number} opts.ts The timestamp for the event.\n397:  * @param {boolean} opts.event True to make a MatrixEvent.\n398:  * @param {string=} opts.msg Optional. The content.body for the event.\n399:  * @return {Object|MatrixEvent} The event\n400:  */\n401: export function mkMessage({ msg, relatesTo, ...opts }: MakeEventPassThruProps & {\n402:     room: Room[\"roomId\"];\n403:     msg?: string;\n404: }): MatrixEvent {\n405:     if (!opts.room || !opts.user) {\n406:         throw new Error(\"Missing .room or .user from options\");\n407:     }\n408:     const message = msg ?? \"Random->\" + Math.random();\n409:     const event: MakeEventProps = {\n410:         ...opts,\n411:         type: \"m.room.message\",\n412:         content: {\n413:             msgtype: \"m.text\",\n414:             body: message,\n415:             ['m.relates_to']: relatesTo,\n416:         },\n417:     };\n418: \n419:     return mkEvent(event);\n420: }\n421: \n422: export function mkStubRoom(roomId: string = null, name: string, client: MatrixClient): Room {\n423:     const stubTimeline = { getEvents: () => [] } as unknown as EventTimeline;\n424:     return {\n425: => 'mxc://avatar.url/image.png',\n434:         }),\n435:         getMembersWithMembership: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n436:         getJoinedMembers: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n437:         getJoinedMemberCount: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(1),\n438:         getInvitedAndJoinedMemberCount: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(1),\n439:         setUnreadNotificationCount: jest.fn(),\n440:         getMembers: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n441:         getPendingEvents: () => [],\n442:         getLiveTimeline: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(stubTimeline),\n443:         getUnfilteredTimelineSet: jest.fn(),\n444:         findEventById: () => null,\n445:         getAccountData: () => null,\n446:         hasMembershipState: () => null,\n447:         getVersion: () => '1',\n448: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\n456:             maySendRedactionForEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\n457:             maySendEvent: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(true),\n458:             members: {},\n459:             getJoinRule: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(JoinRule.Invite),\n460:             on: jest.fn(),\n461:             off: jest.fn(),\n462:         } as unknown as RoomState,\n463:         tags: {},\n464:         setBlacklistUnverifiedDevices: jest.fn(),\n465:         on: jest.fn(),\n466:         off: jest.fn(),\n467:         removeListener: jest.fn(),\n468:         getDMInviter: jest.fn(),\n469:         name,\n470:         normalizedName: normalize(name || \"\"),\n471:         getAvatarUrl: () => 'mxc://avatar.url/room.png',\n472:         getMxcAvatarUrl: () => 'mxc://avatar.url/room.png',\n473:         isSpaceRoom: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\n474:         getType: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(undefined),\n475:         isElementVideoRoom: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(false),\n476:         getUnreadNotificationCount: jest.fn(() => 0),\n477:         getEventReadUpTo: jest.fn(() => null),\n478:         getCanonicalAlias: jest.fn(),\n479:         getAltAliases: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n480:         timeline: [],\n481:         getJoinRule: jest.fn().mockReturnValue(\"invite\"),\n482:         loadMembersIfNeeded: jest.fn(),\n483:         client,\n484:         myUserId: client?.getUserId(),\n485:         canInvite: jest.fn(),\n486:         getThreads: jest.fn().mockReturnValue([]),\n487:         eventShouldLiveIn: {})),\n490:     } as unknown as Room;\n491: }\n492: \n493: export function mkServerConfig(hsUrl, isUrl) {\n494:     return makeType(ValidatedServerConfig, {\n495:         hsUrl,\n496:         hsName: \"TEST_ENVIRONMENT\",\n497:         hsNameIsDifferent: false, // yes, we lie\n498:         isUrl,\n499:     });\n500: }\n501: \n502: // These methods make some use of some private methods on the AsyncStoreWithClient to simplify getting into a consistent\n503: // ready state without needing to wire up a dispatcher and pretend to be a js-sdk client.\n504: \n505: export const setupAsyncStoreWithClient = async <T = unknown>(store: AsyncStoreWithClient<T>, client: MatrixClient) => {\n506:     // @ts-ignore\n507:     store.readyStore.useUnitTestClient(client);\n508:     // @ts-ignore\n509:     await store.onReady();\n510: };\n511: \n512: export const resetAsyncStoreWithClient = async <T = unknown>(store: AsyncStoreWithClient<T>) => {\n513:     // @ts-ignore\n514:     await store.onNotReady();\n515: };\n516: \n517: export const mockStateEventImplementation = (events: MatrixEvent[]) => {\n518:     const stateMap = new EnhancedMap<string, Map<string, MatrixEvent>>();\n519:     events.forEach(event => {\n520:         stateMap.getOrCreate(event.getType(), new Map()).set(event.getStateKey(), event);\n521:     });\n522: \n523:     // recreate the overloading in RoomState\n524:     function getStateEvents(eventType: EventType | string): MatrixEvent[];\n525:     function getStateEvents(eventType: EventType | string, stateKey: string): MatrixEvent;\n526:     function getStateEvents(eventType: EventType | string, stateKey?: string) {\n527:         if (stateKey || stateKey === \"\") {\n528:             return stateMap.get(eventType)?.get(stateKey) || null;\n529:         }\n530:         return Array.from(stateMap.get(eventType)?.values() || []);\n531:     }\n532:     return getStateEvents;\n533: };\n534: \n535: export const mkRoom = (\n536:     client: MatrixClient,\n537:     roomId: string,\n538:     rooms?: ReturnType<typeof mkStubRoom>[],\n539: ): MockedObject<Room> => {\n540:     const room = mocked(mkStubRoom(roomId, roomId, client));\n541:     mocked(room.currentState).getStateEvents.mockImplementation(mockStateEventImplementation([]));\n542:     rooms?.push(room);\n543:     return room;\n544: };\n545: \n546: /**\n547:  * Upserts given events into room.currentState\n548:  * @param room\n549:  * @param events\n550:  */\n551: export const upsertRoomStateEvents = (room: Room, events: MatrixEvent[]): void => {\n552:     const eventsMap = events.reduce((acc, event) => {\n553:         const eventType = event.getType();\n554:         if (!acc.has(eventType)) {\n555:             acc.set(eventType, new Map());\n556:         }\n557:         acc.get(eventType).set(event.getStateKey(), event);\n558:         return acc;\n559:     }, room.currentState.events || new Map<string, Map<string, MatrixEvent>>());\n560: \n561:     room.currentState.events = eventsMap;\n562: };\n563: \n564: export const mkSpace = (\n565:     client: MatrixClient,\n566:     spaceId: string,\n567:     rooms?: ReturnType<typeof mkStubRoom>[],\n568:     children: string[] = [],\n569: ): MockedObject<Room> => {\n570:     const space = mocked(mkRoom(client, spaceId, rooms));\n571:     space.isSpaceRoom.mockReturnValue(true);\n572:     space.getType.mockReturnValue(RoomType.Space);\n573:     mocked(space.currentState).getStateEvents.mockImplementation(mockStateEventImplementation(children.map(roomId =>\n574:         mkEvent({\n575:             event: true,\n576:             type: EventType.SpaceChild,\n577:             room: spaceId,\n578:             user: \"@user:server\",\n579:             skey: roomId,\n580:             content: { via: [] },\n581:             ts: Date.now(),\n582:         }),\n583:     )));\n584:     return space;\n585: };\n586: \n587: export const mkRoomMemberJoinEvent = (user: string, room: string): MatrixEvent => {\n588:     return mkEvent({\n589:         event: true,\n590:         type: EventType.RoomMember,\n591:         content: {\n592:             membership: \"join\",\n593:         },\n594:         skey: user,\n595:         user,\n596:         room,\n597:     });\n598: };\n599: \n600: export const mkPusher = (extra: Partial<IPusher> = {}): IPusher => ({\n601:     app_display_name: \"app\",\n602:     app_id: \"123\",\n603:     data: {},\n604:     device_display_name: \"name\",\n605:     kind: \"http\",\n606:     lang: \"en\",\n607:     pushkey: \"pushpush\",\n608:     ...extra,\n609: });\n610: ",

159. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "src/languageHandler.tsx",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2017 MTRNord and Cooperative EITA\\n3: Copyright 2017 Vector Creations Ltd.\\n4: Copyright 2019 Michael Telatynski <7t3chguy@gmail.com>\\n5: Copyright 2019 - 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\\n6: \\n7: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n8: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n9: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n10: \\n11:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n12: \\n13: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n14: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n15: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n16: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n17: limitations under the License.\\n18: */\\n19: \\n20: import counterpart from 'counterpart';\\n21: import React from 'react';\\n22: import { logger } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\\\";\\n23: import { Optional } from \\\"matrix-events-sdk\\\";\\n24: \\n25: import SettingsStore from \\\"./settings/SettingsStore\\\";\\n26: import PlatformPeg from \\\"./PlatformPeg\\\";\\n27: import { SettingLevel } from \\\"./settings/SettingLevel\\\";\\n28: import { retry } from \\\"./utils/promise\\\";\\n29: import SdkConfig from \\\"./SdkConfig\\\";\\n30: import { ModuleRunner } from \\\"./modules/ModuleRunner\\\";\\n31: \\n32: // @ts-ignore - $webapp is a webpack resolve alias pointing to the output directory, see webpack config\\n33: import webpackLangJsonUrl from \\\"$webapp/i18n/languages.json\\\";\\n34: \\n35: const i18nFolder = 'i18n/';\\n36: \\n37: // Control whether to also return original, untranslated strings\\n38: // Useful for debugging and testing\\n39: const ANNOTATE_STRINGS = false;\\n40: \\n41: // We use english strings as keys, some of which contain full stops\\n42: counterpart.setSeparator('|');\\n43: \\n44: // see `translateWithFallback` for an explanation of fallback handling\\n45: const FALLBACK_LOCALE = 'en';\\n46: counterpart.setFallbackLocale(FALLBACK_LOCALE);\\n47: \\n48: export interface ITranslatableError extends Error {\\n49:     translatedMessage: string;\\n50: }\\n51: \\n52: /**\\n53:  * Helper function to create an error which has an English message\\n54:  * with a translatedMessage property for use by the consumer.\\n55:  * @param {string} message Message to translate.\\n56:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\\n57:  * @returns {Error} The constructed error.\\n58:  */\\n59: export function newTranslatableError(message: string, variables?: IVariables): ITranslatableError {\\n60:     const error = new Error(message) as ITranslatableError;\\n61:     error.translatedMessage = _t(message, variables);\\n62:     return error;\\n63: }\\n64: \\n65: export function getUserLanguage(): string {\\n66:     const language = SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"language\\\", null, /*excludeDefault:*/true);\\n67:     if (language) {\\n68:         return language;\\n69:     } else {\\n70:         return normalizeLanguageKey(getLanguageFromBrowser());\\n71:     }\\n72: }\\n73: \\n74: // Function which only purpose is to mark that a string is translatable\\n75: // Does not actually do anything. It's helpful for automatic extraction of translatable strings\\n76: export function _td(s: string): string { // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\\n77:     return s;\\n78: }\\n79: \\n80: /**\\n81:  * to improve screen reader experience translations that are not in the main page language\\n82:  * eg a translation that fell back to english from another language\\n83:  * should be wrapped with an appropriate `lang='en'` attribute\\n84:  * counterpart's `translate` doesn't expose a way to determine if the resulting translation\\n85:  * is in the target locale or a fallback locale\\n86:  * for this reason, force fallbackLocale === locale in the first call to translate\\n87:  * and fallback 'manually' so we can mark fallback strings appropriately\\n88:  * */\\n89: const translateWithFallback = (text: string, options?: object): { translated?: string, isFallback?: boolean } => {\\n90:     const translated = counterpart.translate(text, { ...options, fallbackLocale: counterpart.getLocale() });\\n91:     if (!translated || translated.startsWith(\\\"missing translation:\\\")) {\\n92:         const fallbackTranslated = counterpart.translate(text, { ...options, locale: FALLBACK_LOCALE });\\n93:         if ((!fallbackTranslated || fallbackTranslated.startsWith(\\\"missing translation:\\\"))\\n94:             && process.env.NODE_ENV !== \\\"development\\\") {\\n95:             // Even the translation via FALLBACK_LOCALE failed; this can happen if\\n96:             //\\n97:             // 1. The string isn't in the translations dictionary, usually because you're in develop\\n98:             // and haven't run yarn i18n\\n99:             // 2. Loading the translation resources over the network failed, which can happen due to\\n100:             // to network or if the client tried to load a translation that's been removed from the\\n101:             // server.\\n102:             //\\n103:             // At this point, its the lesser evil to show the untranslated text, which\\n104:             // will be in English, so the user can still make out *something*, rather than an opaque\\n105:             // \\\"missing translation\\\" error.\\n106:             //\\n107:             // Don't do this in develop so people remember to run yarn i18n.\\n108:             return { translated: text, isFallback: true };\\n109:         }\\n110:         return { translated: fallbackTranslated, isFallback: true };\\n111:     }\\n112:     return { translated };\\n113: };\\n114: \\n115: // Wrapper for counterpart's translation function so that it handles nulls and undefineds properly\\n116: // Takes the same arguments as counterpart.translate()\\n117: function safeCounterpartTranslate(text: string, variables?: object) {\\n118:     // Don't do substitutions in counterpart. We handle it ourselves so we can replace with React components\\n119:     // However, still pass the variables to counterpart so that it can choose the correct plural if count is given\\n120:     // It is enough to pass the count variable, but in the future counterpart might make use of other information too\\n121:     const options = { ...variables, interpolate: false };\\n122: \\n123:     // Horrible hack to avoid https://github.com/vector-im/element-web/issues/4191\\n124:     // The interpolation library that counterpart uses does not support undefined/null\\n125:     // values and instead will throw an error. This is a problem since everywhere else\\n126:     // in JS land passing undefined/null will simply stringify instead, and when converting\\n127:     // valid ES6 template strings to i18n strings it's extremely easy to pass undefined/null\\n128:     // if there are no existing null guards. To avoid this making the app completely inoperable,\\n129:     // we'll check all the values for undefined/null and stringify them here.\\n130:     if (options && typeof options === 'object') {\\n131:         Object.keys(options).forEach((k) => {\\n132:             if (options[k] === undefined) {\\n133:                 logger.warn(\\\"safeCounterpartTranslate called with undefined interpolation name: \\\" + k);\\n134:                 options[k] = 'undefined';\\n135:             }\\n136:             if (options[k] === null) {\\n137:                 logger.warn(\\\"safeCounterpartTranslate called with null interpolation name: \\\" + k);\\n138:                 options[k] = 'null';\\n139:             }\\n140:         });\\n141:     }\\n142:     return translateWithFallback(text, options);\\n143: }\\n144: \\n145: type SubstitutionValue = number | string | React.ReactNode | ((sub: string) => React.ReactNode);\\n146: \\n147: export interface IVariables {\\n148:     count?: number;\\n149:     [key: string]: SubstitutionValue;\\n150: }\\n151: \\n152: export type Tags = Record<string, SubstitutionValue>;\\n153: \\n154: export type TranslatedString = string | React.ReactNode;\\n155: \\n156: // For development/testing purposes it is useful to also output the original string\\n157: // Don't do that for release versions\\n158: const annotateStrings = (result: TranslatedString, translationKey: string): TranslatedString => {\\n159:     if (!ANNOTATE_STRINGS) {\\n160:         return result;\\n161:     }\\n162: \\n163:     if (typeof result === 'string') {\\n164:         return `@@${translationKey}##${result}@@`;\\n165:     } else {\\n166:         return <span className='translated-string' data-orig-string={translationKey}>{ result }</span>;\\n167:     }\\n168: };\\n169: \\n170: /*\\n171:  * Translates text and optionally also replaces XML-ish elements in the text with e.g. React components\\n172:  * @param {string} text The untranslated text, e.g \\\"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\\\".\\n173:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\\n174:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\\n175:  *\\n176:  * In both variables and tags, the values to substitute with can be either simple strings, React components,\\n177:  * or functions that return the value to use in the substitution (e.g. return a React component). In case of\\n178:  * a tag replacement, the function receives as the argument the text inside the element corresponding to the tag.\\n179:  *\\n180:  * Use tag substitutions if you need to translate text between tags (e.g. \\\"<a>Click here!</a>\\\"), otherwise\\n181:  * you will end up with literal \\\"<a>\\\" in your output, rather than HTML. Note that you can also use variable\\n182:  * substitution to insert React components, but you can't use it to translate text between tags.\\n183:  *\\n184:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\\n185:  */\\n186: // eslint-next-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\\n187: export function _t(text: string, variables?: IVariables): string;\\n188: export function _t(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): React.ReactNode;\\n189: export function _t(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): TranslatedString {\\n190:     // The translation returns text so there's no XSS vector here (no unsafe HTML, no code execution)\\n191:     const { translated } = safeCounterpartTranslate(text, variables);\\n192:     const substituted = substitute(translated, variables, tags);\\n193: \\n194:     return annotateStrings(substituted, text);\\n195: }\\n196: \\n197: /*\\n198:  * Wraps normal _t function and adds atttribution for translations that used a fallback locale\\n199:  * Wraps translations that fell back from active locale to fallback locale with a `<span lang=<fallback locale>>`\\n200:  * @param {string} text The untranslated text, e.g \\\"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\\\".\\n201:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\\n202:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\\n203:  *\\n204:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions\\n205:  * or translation used a fallback locale, otherwise a string\\n206:  */\\n207: // eslint-next-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\\n208: export function _tDom(text: string, variables?: IVariables): TranslatedString;\\n209: export function _tDom(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): React.ReactNode;\\n210: export function _tDom(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): TranslatedString {\\n211:     // The translation returns text so there's no XSS vector here (no unsafe HTML, no code execution)\\n212:     const { translated, isFallback } = safeCounterpartTranslate(text, variables);\\n213:     const substituted = substitute(translated, variables, tags);\\n214: \\n215:     // wrap en fallback translation with lang attribute for screen readers\\n216:     const result = isFallback ? <span lang='en'>{ substituted }</span> : substituted;\\n217: \\n218:     return annotateStrings(result, text);\\n219: }\\n220: \\n221: /**\\n222:  * Sanitizes unsafe text for the sanitizer, ensuring references to variables will not be considered\\n223:  * replaceable by the translation functions.\\n224:  * @param {string} text The text to sanitize.\\n225:  * @returns {string} The sanitized text.\\n226:  */\\n227: export function sanitizeForTranslation(text: string): string {\\n228:     // Add a non-breaking space so the regex doesn't trigger when translating.\\n229:     return text.replace(/%\\\\(([^)]*)\\\\)/g, '%\\\\xa0($1)');\\n230: }\\n231: \\n232: /*\\n233:  * Similar to _t(), except only does substitutions, and no translation\\n234:  * @param {string} text The text, e.g \\\"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\\\".\\n235:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\\n236:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\\n237:  *\\n238:  * The values to substitute with can be either simple strings, or functions that return the value to use in\\n239:  * the substitution (e.g. return a React component).
