In case of a tag replacement, the function receives as\\n240:  * the argument the text inside the element corresponding to the tag.\\n241:  *\\n242:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\\n243:  */\\n244: export function substitute(text: string, variables?: IVariables): string;\\n245: export function substitute(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): string;\\n246: export function substitute(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\\n247:     let result: React.ReactNode | string = text;\\n248: \\n249:     if (variables !== undefined) {\\n250:         const regexpMapping: IVariables = {};\\n251:         for (const variable in variables) {\\n252:             regexpMapping[`%\\\\\\\\(${variable}\\\\\\\\)s`] = variables[variable];\\n253:         }\\n254:         result = replaceByRegexes(result as string, regexpMapping);\\n255:     }\\n256: \\n257:     if (tags !== undefined) {\\n258:         const regexpMapping: Tags = {};\\n259:         for (const tag in tags) {\\n260:             regexpMapping[`(<${tag}>(.*?)<\\\\\\\\/${tag}>|<${tag}>|<${tag}\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\/>)`] = tags[tag];\\n261:         }\\n262:         result = replaceByRegexes(result as string, regexpMapping);\\n263:     }\\n264: \\n265:     return result;\\n266: }\\n267: \\n268: /*\\n269:  * Replace parts of a text using regular expressions\\n270:  * @param {string} text The text on which to perform substitutions\\n271:  * @param {object} mapping A mapping from regular expressions in string form to replacement string or a\\n272:  * function which will receive as the argument the capture groups defined in the regexp. E.g.\\n273:  * { 'Hello (.?) World': (sub) => sub.toUpperCase() }\\n274:  *\\n275:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\\n276:  */\\n277: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables): string;\\n278: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: Tags): React.ReactNode;\\n279: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables | Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\\n280:     // We initially store our output as an array of strings and objects (e.g. React components).\\n281:     // This will then be converted to a string or a <span> at the end\\n282:     const output = [text];\\n283: \\n284:     // If we insert any components we need to wrap the output in a span. React doesn't like just an array of components.\\n285:     let shouldWrapInSpan = false;\\n286: \\n287:     for (const regexpString in mapping) {\\n288:         // TODO: Cache regexps\\n289:         const regexp = new RegExp(regexpString, \\\"g\\\");\\n290: \\n291:         // Loop over what output we have so far and perform replacements\\n292:         // We look for matches: if we find one, we get three parts: everything before the match, the replaced part,\\n293:         // and everything after the match. Insert all three into the output. We need to do this because we can insert objects.\\n294:         // Otherwise there would be no need for the splitting and we could do simple replacement.\\n295:         let matchFoundSomewhere = false; // If we don't find a match anywhere we want to log it\\n296:         for (let outputIndex = 0; outputIndex < output.length; outputIndex++) {\\n297:             const inputText = output[outputIndex];\\n298:             if (typeof inputText !== 'string') { // We might have inserted objects earlier, don't try to replace them\\n299:                 continue;\\n300:             }\\n301: \\n302:             // process every match in the string\\n303:             // starting with the first\\n304:             let match = regexp.exec(inputText);\\n305: \\n306:             if (!match) continue;\\n307:             matchFoundSomewhere = true;\\n308: \\n309:             // The textual part before the first match\\n310:             const head = inputText.slice(0, match.index);\\n311: \\n312:             const parts = [];\\n313:             // keep track of prevMatch\\n314:             let prevMatch;\\n315:             while (match) {\\n316:                 // store prevMatch\\n317:                 prevMatch = match;\\n318:                 const capturedGroups = match.slice(2);\\n319: \\n320:                 let replaced;\\n321:                 // If substitution is a function, call it\\n322:                 if (mapping[regexpString] instanceof Function) {\\n323:                     replaced = ((mapping as Tags)[regexpString] as Function)(...capturedGroups);\\n324:                 } else {\\n325:                     replaced = mapping[regexpString];\\n326:                 }\\n327: \\n328:                 if (typeof replaced === 'object') {\\n329:                     shouldWrapInSpan = true;\\n330:                 }\\n331: \\n332:                 // Here we also need to check that it actually is a string before comparing against one\\n333:                 // The head and tail are always strings\\n334:                 if (typeof replaced !== 'string' || replaced !== '') {\\n335:                     parts.push(replaced);\\n336:                 }\\n337: \\n338:                 // try the next match\\n339:                 match = regexp.exec(inputText);\\n340: \\n341:                 // add the text between prevMatch and this one\\n342:                 // or the end of the string if prevMatch is the last match\\n343:                 let tail;\\n344:                 if (match) {\\n345:                     const startIndex = prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length;\\n346:                     tail = inputText.slice(startIndex, match.index);\\n347:                 } else {\\n348:                     tail = inputText.slice(prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length);\\n349:                 }\\n350:                 if (tail) {\\n351:                     parts.push(tail);\\n352:                 }\\n353:             }\\n354: \\n355:             // Insert in reverse order as splice does insert-before and this way we get the final order correct\\n356:             // remove the old element at the same time\\n357:             output.splice(outputIndex, 1, ...parts);\\n358: \\n359:             if (head !== '') { // Don't push empty nodes, they are of no use\\n360:                 output.splice(outputIndex, 0, head);\\n361:             }\\n362:         }\\n363:         if (!matchFoundSomewhere) { // The current regexp did not match anything in the input\\n364:             // Missing matches is entirely possible because you might choose to show some variables only in the case\\n365:             // of e.g. plurals. It's still a bit suspicious, and could be due to an error, so log it.\\n366:             // However, not showing count is so common that it's not worth logging. And other commonly unused variables\\n367:             // here, if there are any.\\n368:             if (regexpString !== '%\\\\\\\\(count\\\\\\\\)s') {\\n369:                 logger.log(`Could not find ${regexp} in ${text}`);\\n370:             }\\n371:         }\\n372:     }\\n373: \\n374:     if (shouldWrapInSpan) {\\n375:         return React.createElement('span', null, ...output);\\n376:     } else {\\n377:         return output.join('');\\n378:     }\\n379: }\\n380: \\n381: // Allow overriding the text displayed when no translation exists\\n382: // Currently only used in unit tests to avoid having to load\\n383: // the translations in element-web\\n384: export function setMissingEntryGenerator(f: (value: string) => void) {\\n385:     counterpart.setMissingEntryGenerator(f);\\n386: }\\n387: \\n388: type Languages = {\\n389:     [lang: string]: {\\n390:         fileName: string;\\n391:         label: string;\\n392:     };\\n393: };\\n394: \\n395: export function setLanguage(preferredLangs: string | string[]) {\\n396:     if (!Array.isArray(preferredLangs)) {\\n397:         preferredLangs = [preferredLangs];\\n398:     }\\n399: \\n400:     const plaf = PlatformPeg.get();\\n401:     if (plaf) {\\n402:         plaf.setLanguage(preferredLangs);\\n403:     }\\n404: \\n405:     let langToUse: string;\\n406:     let availLangs: Languages;\\n407:     return getLangsJson().then((result) => {\\n408:         availLangs = result;\\n409: \\n410:         for (let i = 0; i < preferredLangs.length; ++i) {\\n411:             if (availLangs.hasOwnProperty(preferredLangs[i])) {\\n412:                 langToUse = preferredLangs[i];\\n413:                 break;\\n414:             }\\n415:         }\\n416:         if (!langToUse) {\\n417:             // Fallback to en_EN if none is found\\n418:             langToUse = 'en';\\n419:             logger.error(\\\"Unable to find an appropriate language\\\");\\n420:         }\\n421: \\n422:         return getLanguageRetry(i18nFolder + availLangs[langToUse].fileName);\\n423:     }).then(async (langData) => {\\n424:         counterpart.registerTranslations(langToUse, langData);\\n425:         await registerCustomTranslations();\\n426:         counterpart.setLocale(langToUse);\\n427:         await SettingsStore.setValue(\\\"language\\\", null, SettingLevel.DEVICE, langToUse);\\n428:         // Adds a lot of noise to test runs, so disable logging there.\\n429:         if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== \\\"test\\\") {\\n430:             logger.log(\\\"set language to \\\" + langToUse);\\n431:         }\\n432: \\n433:         // Set 'en' as fallback language:\\n434:         if (langToUse !== \\\"en\\\") {\\n435:             return getLanguageRetry(i18nFolder + availLangs['en'].fileName);\\n436:         }\\n437:     }).then(async (langData) => {\\n438:         if (langData) counterpart.registerTranslations('en', langData);\\n439:         await registerCustomTranslations();\\n440:     });\\n441: }\\n442: \\n443: type Language = {\\n444:     value: string;\\n445:     label: string;\\n446: };\\n447: \\n448: export function getAllLanguagesFromJson(): Promise<Language[]> {\\n449:     return getLangsJson().then((langsObject) => {\\n450:         const langs: Language[] = [];\\n451:         for (const langKey in langsObject) {\\n452:             if (langsObject.hasOwnProperty(langKey)) {\\n453:                 langs.push({\\n454:                     'value': langKey,\\n455:                     'label': langsObject[langKey].label,\\n456:                 });\\n457:             }\\n458:         }\\n459:         return langs;\\n460:     });\\n461: }\\n462: \\n463: export function getLanguagesFromBrowser() {\\n464:     if (navigator.languages && navigator.languages.length) return navigator.languages;\\n465:     if (navigator.language) return [navigator.language];\\n466:     return [navigator.userLanguage || \\\"en\\\"];\\n467: }\\n468: \\n469: export function getLanguageFromBrowser() {\\n470:     return getLanguagesFromBrowser()[0];\\n471: }\\n472: \\n473: /**\\n474:  * Turns a language string, normalises it,\\n475:  * (see normalizeLanguageKey) into an array of language strings\\n476:  * with fallback to generic languages\\n477:  * (eg. 'pt-BR' => ['pt-br', 'pt'])\\n478:  *\\n479:  * @param {string} language The input language string\\n480:  * @return {string[]} List of normalised languages\\n481:  */\\n482: export function getNormalizedLanguageKeys(language: string) {\\n483:     const languageKeys: string[] = [];\\n484:     const normalizedLanguage = normalizeLanguageKey(language);\\n485:     const languageParts = normalizedLanguage.split('-');\\n486:     if (languageParts.length === 2 && languageParts[0] === languageParts[1]) {\\n487:         languageKeys.push(languageParts[0]);\\n488:     } else {\\n489:         languageKeys.push(normalizedLanguage);\\n490:         if (languageParts.length === 2) {\\n491:             languageKeys.push(languageParts[0]);\\n492:         }\\n493:     }\\n494:     return languageKeys;\\n495: }\\n496: \\n497: /**\\n498:  * Returns a language string with underscores replaced with\\n499:  * hyphens, and lowercased.\\n500:  *\\n501:  * @param {string} language The language string to be normalized\\n502:  * @returns {string} The normalized language string\\n503:  */\\n504: export function normalizeLanguageKey(language: string) {\\n505:     return language.toLowerCase().replace(\\\"_\\\", \\\"-\\\");\\n506: }\\n507: \\n508: export function getCurrentLanguage() {\\n509:     return counterpart.getLocale();\\n510: }\\n511: \\n512: /**\\n513:  * Given a list of language codes, pick the most appropriate one\\n514:  * given the current language (ie. getCurrentLanguage())\\n515:  * English is assumed to be a reasonable default.\\n516:  *\\n517:  * @param {string[]} langs List of language codes to pick from\\n518:  * @returns {string} The most appropriate language code from langs\\n519:  */\\n520: export function pickBestLanguage(langs: string[]): string {\\n521:     const currentLang = getCurrentLanguage();\\n522:     const normalisedLangs = langs.map(normalizeLanguageKey);\\n523: \\n524:     {\\n525:         // Best is an exact match\\n526:         const currentLangIndex = normalisedLangs.indexOf(currentLang);\\n527:         if (currentLangIndex > -1) return langs[currentLangIndex];\\n528:     }\\n529: \\n530:     {\\n531:         // Failing that, a different dialect of the same language\\n532:         const closeLangIndex = normalisedLangs.findIndex((l) => l.slice(0, 2) === currentLang.slice(0, 2));\\n533:         if (closeLangIndex > -1) return langs[closeLangIndex];\\n534:     }\\n535: \\n536:     {\\n537:         // Neither of those? Try an english variant.\\n538:         const enIndex = normalisedLangs.findIndex((l) => l.startsWith('en'));\\n539:         if (enIndex > -1) return langs[enIndex];\\n540:     }\\n541: \\n542:     // if nothing else, use the first\\n543:     return langs[0];\\n544: }\\n545: \\n546: async function getLangsJson(): Promise<Languages> {\\n547:     let url: string;\\n548:     if (typeof(webpackLangJsonUrl) === 'string') { // in Jest this 'url' isn't a URL, so just fall through\\n549:         url = webpackLangJsonUrl;\\n550:     } else {\\n551:         url = i18nFolder + 'languages.json';\\n552:     }\\n553: \\n554:     const res = await fetch(url, { method: \\\"GET\\\" });\\n555: \\n556:     if (!res.ok) {\\n557:         throw new Error(`Failed to load ${url}, got ${res.status}`);\\n558:     }\\n559: \\n560:     return res.json();\\n561: }\\n562: \\n563: interface ICounterpartTranslation {\\n564:     [key: string]: string | {\\n565:         [pluralisation: string]: string;\\n566:     };\\n567: }\\n568: \\n569: async function getLanguageRetry(langPath: string, num = 3): Promise<ICounterpartTranslation> {\\n570:     return retry(() => getLanguage(langPath), num, e => {\\n571:         logger.log(\\\"Failed to load i18n\\\", langPath);\\n572:         logger.error(e);\\n573:         return true; // always retry\\n574:     });\\n575: }\\n576: \\n577: async function getLanguage(langPath: string): Promise<ICounterpartTranslation> {\\n578:     const res = await fetch(langPath, { method: \\\"GET\\\" });\\n579: \\n580:     if (!res.ok) {\\n581:         throw new Error(`Failed to load ${langPath}, got ${res.status}`);\\n582:     }\\n583: \\n584:     return res.json();\\n585: }\\n586: \\n587: export interface ICustomTranslations {\\n588:     // Format is a map of english string to language to override\\n589:     [str: string]: {\\n590:         [lang: string]: string;\\n591:     };\\n592: }\\n593: \\n594: let cachedCustomTranslations: Optional<ICustomTranslations> = null;\\n595: let cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = 0; // zero to trigger expiration right away\\n596: \\n597: // This awkward class exists so the test runner can get at the function. It is\\n598: // not intended for practical or realistic usage.\\n599: export class CustomTranslationOptions {\\n600:     public static lookupFn: (url: string) => ICustomTranslations;\\n601: \\n602:     private constructor() {\\n603:         // static access for tests only\\n604:     }\\n605: }\\n606: \\n607: function doRegisterTranslations(customTranslations: ICustomTranslations) {\\n608:     // We convert the operator-friendly version into something counterpart can\\n609:     // consume.\\n610:     const langs: {\\n611:         // same structure, just flipped key order\\n612:         [lang: string]: {\\n613:             [str: string]: string;\\n614:         };\\n615:     } = {};\\n616:     for (const [str, translations] of Object.entries(customTranslations)) {\\n617:         for (const [lang, newStr] of Object.entries(translations)) {\\n618:             if (!langs[lang]) langs[lang] = {};\\n619:             langs[lang][str] = newStr;\\n620:         }\\n621:     }\\n622: \\n623:     // Finally, tell counterpart about our translations\\n624:     for (const [lang, translations] of Object.entries(langs)) {\\n625:         counterpart.registerTranslations(lang, translations);\\n626:     }\\n627: }\\n628: \\n629: /**\\n630:  * Any custom modules with translations to load are parsed first, followed by an\\n631:  * optionally defined translations file in the config. If no customization is made,\\n632:  * or the file can't be parsed, no action will be taken.\\n633:  *\\n634:  * This function should be called *after* registering other translations data to\\n635:  * ensure it overrides strings properly.\\n636:  */\\n637: export async function registerCustomTranslations() {\\n638:     const moduleTranslations = ModuleRunner.instance.allTranslations;\\n639:     doRegisterTranslations(moduleTranslations);\\n640: \\n641:     const lookupUrl = SdkConfig.get().custom_translations_url;\\n642:     if (!lookupUrl) return; // easy - nothing to do\\n643: \\n644:     try {\\n645:         let json: ICustomTranslations;\\n646:         if (Date.now() >= cachedCustomTranslationsExpire) {\\n647:             json = CustomTranslationOptions.lookupFn\\n648:                 ? CustomTranslationOptions.lookupFn(lookupUrl)\\n649:                 : (await (await fetch(lookupUrl)).json() as ICustomTranslations);\\n650:             cachedCustomTranslations = json;\\n651: \\n652:             // Set expiration to the future, but not too far. Just trying to avoid\\n653:             // repeated, successive, calls to the server rather than anything long-term.\\n654:             cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = Date.now() + (5 * 60 * 1000);\\n655:         } else {\\n656:             json = cachedCustomTranslations;\\n657:         }\\n658: \\n659:         // If the (potentially cached) json is invalid, don't use it.\\n660:         if (!json) return;\\n661: \\n662:         // Finally, register it.\\n663:         doRegisterTranslations(json);\\n664:     } catch (e) {\\n665:         // We consume all exceptions because it's considered non-fatal for custom\\n666:         // translations to break. Most failures will be during initial development\\n667:         // of the json file and not (hopefully) at runtime.\\n668:         logger.warn(\\\"Ignoring error while registering custom translations: \\\", e);\\n669: \\n670:         // Like above: trigger a cache of the json to avoid successive calls.\\n671:         cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = Date.now() + (5 * 60 * 1000);\\n672:     }\\n673: }\\n674: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/src/languageHandler.tsx\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2017 MTRNord and Cooperative EITA\n3: Copyright 2017 Vector Creations Ltd.\n4: Copyright 2019 Michael Telatynski <7t3chguy@gmail.com>\n5: Copyright 2019 - 2022 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n6: \n7: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n8: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n9: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n10: \n11:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n12: \n13: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n14: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n15: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n16: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n17: limitations under the License.\n18: */\n19: \n20: import counterpart from 'counterpart';\n21: import React from 'react';\n22: import { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\n23: import { Optional } from \"matrix-events-sdk\";\n24: \n25: import SettingsStore from \"./settings/SettingsStore\";\n26: import PlatformPeg from \"./PlatformPeg\";\n27: import { SettingLevel } from \"./settings/SettingLevel\";\n28: import { retry } from \"./utils/promise\";\n29: import SdkConfig from \"./SdkConfig\";\n30: import { ModuleRunner } from \"./modules/ModuleRunner\";\n31: \n32: // @ts-ignore - $webapp is a webpack resolve alias pointing to the output directory, see webpack config\n33: import webpackLangJsonUrl from \"$webapp/i18n/languages.json\";\n34: \n35: const i18nFolder = 'i18n/';\n36: \n37: // Control whether to also return original, untranslated strings\n38: // Useful for debugging and testing\n39: const ANNOTATE_STRINGS = false;\n40: \n41: // We use english strings as keys, some of which contain full stops\n42: counterpart.setSeparator('|');\n43: \n44: // see `translateWithFallback` for an explanation of fallback handling\n45: const FALLBACK_LOCALE = 'en';\n46: counterpart.setFallbackLocale(FALLBACK_LOCALE);\n47: \n48: export interface ITranslatableError extends Error {\n49:     translatedMessage: string;\n50: }\n51: \n52: /**\n53:  * Helper function to create an error which has an English message\n54:  * with a translatedMessage property for use by the consumer.\n55:  * @param {string} message Message to translate.\n56:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n57:  * @returns {Error} The constructed error.\n58:  */\n59: export function newTranslatableError(message: string, variables?: IVariables): ITranslatableError {\n60:     const error = new Error(message) as ITranslatableError;\n61:     error.translatedMessage = _t(message, variables);\n62:     return error;\n63: }\n64: \n65: export function getUserLanguage(): string {\n66:     const language = SettingsStore.getValue(\"language\", null, /*excludeDefault:*/true);\n67:     if (language) {\n68:         return language;\n69:     } else {\n70:         return normalizeLanguageKey(getLanguageFromBrowser());\n71:     }\n72: }\n73: \n74: // Function which only purpose is to mark that a string is translatable\n75: // Does not actually do anything. It's helpful for automatic extraction of translatable strings\n76: export function _td(s: string): string { // eslint-disable-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\n77:     return s;\n78: }\n79: \n80: /**\n81:  * to improve screen reader experience translations that are not in the main page language\n82:  * eg a translation that fell back to english from another language\n83:  * should be wrapped with an appropriate `lang='en'` attribute\n84:  * counterpart's `translate` doesn't expose a way to determine if the resulting translation\n85:  * is in the target locale or a fallback locale\n86:  * for this reason, force fallbackLocale === locale in the first call to translate\n87:  * and fallback 'manually' so we can mark fallback strings appropriately\n88:  * */\n89: const translateWithFallback = (text: string, options?: object): { translated?: string, isFallback?: boolean } => {\n90:     const translated = counterpart.translate(text, { ...options, fallbackLocale: counterpart.getLocale() });\n91:     if (!translated || translated.startsWith(\"missing translation:\")) {\n92:         const fallbackTranslated = counterpart.translate(text, { ...options, locale: FALLBACK_LOCALE });\n93:         if ((!fallbackTranslated || fallbackTranslated.startsWith(\"missing translation:\"))\n94:             && process.env.NODE_ENV !== \"development\") {\n95:             // Even the translation via FALLBACK_LOCALE failed; this can happen if\n96:             //\n97:             // 1. The string isn't in the translations dictionary, usually because you're in develop\n98:             // and haven't run yarn i18n\n99:             // 2. Loading the translation resources over the network failed, which can happen due to\n100:             // to network or if the client tried to load a translation that's been removed from the\n101:             // server.\n102:             //\n103:             // At this point, its the lesser evil to show the untranslated text, which\n104:             // will be in English, so the user can still make out *something*, rather than an opaque\n105:             // \"missing translation\" error.\n106:             //\n107:             // Don't do this in develop so people remember to run yarn i18n.\n108:             return { translated: text, isFallback: true };\n109:         }\n110:         return { translated: fallbackTranslated, isFallback: true };\n111:     }\n112:     return { translated };\n113: };\n114: \n115: // Wrapper for counterpart's translation function so that it handles nulls and undefineds properly\n116: // Takes the same arguments as counterpart.translate()\n117: function safeCounterpartTranslate(text: string, variables?: object) {\n118:     // Don't do substitutions in counterpart. We handle it ourselves so we can replace with React components\n119:     // However, still pass the variables to counterpart so that it can choose the correct plural if count is given\n120:     // It is enough to pass the count variable, but in the future counterpart might make use of other information too\n121:     const options = { ...variables, interpolate: false };\n122: \n123:     // Horrible hack to avoid https://github.com/vector-im/element-web/issues/4191\n124:     // The interpolation library that counterpart uses does not support undefined/null\n125:     // values and instead will throw an error. This is a problem since everywhere else\n126:     // in JS land passing undefined/null will simply stringify instead, and when converting\n127:     // valid ES6 template strings to i18n strings it's extremely easy to pass undefined/null\n128:     // if there are no existing null guards. To avoid this making the app completely inoperable,\n129:     // we'll check all the values for undefined/null and stringify them here.\n130:     if (options && typeof options === 'object') {\n131:         Object.keys(options).forEach((k) => {\n132:             if (options[k] === undefined) {\n133:                 logger.warn(\"safeCounterpartTranslate called with undefined interpolation name: \" + k);\n134:                 options[k] = 'undefined';\n135:             }\n136:             if (options[k] === null) {\n137:                 logger.warn(\"safeCounterpartTranslate called with null interpolation name: \" + k);\n138:                 options[k] = 'null';\n139:             }\n140:         });\n141:     }\n142:     return translateWithFallback(text, options);\n143: }\n144: \n145: type SubstitutionValue = number | string | React.ReactNode | ((sub: string) => React.ReactNode);\n146: \n147: export interface IVariables {\n148:     count?: number;\n149:     [key: string]: SubstitutionValue;\n150: }\n151: \n152: export type Tags = Record<string, SubstitutionValue>;\n153: \n154: export type TranslatedString = string | React.ReactNode;\n155: \n156: // For development/testing purposes it is useful to also output the original string\n157: // Don't do that for release versions\n158: const annotateStrings = (result: TranslatedString, translationKey: string): TranslatedString => {\n159:     if (!ANNOTATE_STRINGS) {\n160:         return result;\n161:     }\n162: \n163:     if (typeof result === 'string') {\n164:         return `@@${translationKey}##${result}@@`;\n165:     } else {\n166:         return <span className='translated-string' data-orig-string={translationKey}>{ result }</span>;\n167:     }\n168: };\n169: \n170: /*\n171:  * Translates text and optionally also replaces XML-ish elements in the text with e.g. React components\n172:  * @param {string} text The untranslated text, e.g \"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\".\n173:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n174:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\n175:  *\n176:  * In both variables and tags, the values to substitute with can be either simple strings, React components,\n177:  * or functions that return the value to use in the substitution (e.g. return a React component). In case of\n178:  * a tag replacement, the function receives as the argument the text inside the element corresponding to the tag.\n179:  *\n180:  * Use tag substitutions if you need to translate text between tags (e.g. \"<a>Click here!</a>\"), otherwise\n181:  * you will end up with literal \"<a>\" in your output, rather than HTML. Note that you can also use variable\n182:  * substitution to insert React components, but you can't use it to translate text between tags.\n183:  *\n184:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\n185:  */\n186: // eslint-next-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\n187: export function _t(text: string, variables?: IVariables): string;\n188: export function _t(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): React.ReactNode;\n189: export function _t(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): TranslatedString {\n190:     // The translation returns text so there's no XSS vector here (no unsafe HTML, no code execution)\n191:     const { translated } = safeCounterpartTranslate(text, variables);\n192:     const substituted = substitute(translated, variables, tags);\n193: \n194:     return annotateStrings(substituted, text);\n195: }\n196: \n197: /*\n198:  * Wraps normal _t function and adds atttribution for translations that used a fallback locale\n199:  * Wraps translations that fell back from active locale to fallback locale with a `<span lang=<fallback locale>>`\n200:  * @param {string} text The untranslated text, e.g \"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\".\n201:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n202:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\n203:  *\n204:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions\n205:  * or translation used a fallback locale, otherwise a string\n206:  */\n207: // eslint-next-line @typescript-eslint/naming-convention\n208: export function _tDom(text: string, variables?: IVariables): TranslatedString;\n209: export function _tDom(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): React.ReactNode;\n210: export function _tDom(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): TranslatedString {\n211:     // The translation returns text so there's no XSS vector here (no unsafe HTML, no code execution)\n212:     const { translated, isFallback } = safeCounterpartTranslate(text, variables);\n213:     const substituted = substitute(translated, variables, tags);\n214: \n215:     // wrap en fallback translation with lang attribute for screen readers\n216:     const result = isFallback ? <span lang='en'>{ substituted }</span> : substituted;\n217: \n218:     return annotateStrings(result, text);\n219: }\n220: \n221: /**\n222:  * Sanitizes unsafe text for the sanitizer, ensuring references to variables will not be considered\n223:  * replaceable by the translation functions.\n224:  * @param {string} text The text to sanitize.\n225:  * @returns {string} The sanitized text.\n226:  */\n227: export function sanitizeForTranslation(text: string): string {\n228:     // Add a non-breaking space so the regex doesn't trigger when translating.\n229:     return text.replace(/%\\(([^)]*)\\)/g, '%\\xa0($1)');\n230: }\n231: \n232: /*\n233:  * Similar to _t(), except only does substitutions, and no translation\n234:  * @param {string} text The text, e.g \"click <a>here</a> now to %(foo)s\".\n235:  * @param {object} variables Variable substitutions, e.g { foo: 'bar' }\n236:  * @param {object} tags Tag substitutions e.g. { 'a': (sub) => <a>{sub}</a> }\n237:  *\n238:  * The values to substitute with can be either simple strings, or functions that return the value to use in\n239:  * the substitution (e.g. return a React component). In case of a tag replacement, the function receives as\n240:  * the argument the text inside the element corresponding to the tag.\n241:  *\n242:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\n243:  */\n244: export function substitute(text: string, variables?: IVariables): string;\n245: export function substitute(text: string, variables: IVariables, tags: Tags): string;\n246: export function substitute(text: string, variables?: IVariables, tags?: Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\n247:     let result: React.ReactNode | string = text;\n248: \n249:     if (variables !== undefined) {\n250:         const regexpMapping: IVariables = {};\n251:         for (const variable in variables) {\n252:             regexpMapping[`%\\\\(${variable}\\\\)s`] = variables[variable];\n253:         }\n254:         result = replaceByRegexes(result as string, regexpMapping);\n255:     }\n256: \n257:     if (tags !== undefined) {\n258:         const regexpMapping: Tags = {};\n259:         for (const tag in tags) {\n260:             regexpMapping[`(<${tag}>(.*?)<\\\\/${tag}>|<${tag}>|<${tag}\\\\s*\\\\/>)`] = tags[tag];\n261:         }\n262:         result = replaceByRegexes(result as string, regexpMapping);\n263:     }\n264: \n265:     return result;\n266: }\n267: \n268: /*\n269:  * Replace parts of a text using regular expressions\n270:  * @param {string} text The text on which to perform substitutions\n271:  * @param {object} mapping A mapping from regular expressions in string form to replacement string or a\n272:  * function which will receive as the argument the capture groups defined in the regexp. E.g.\n273:  * { 'Hello (.?) World': (sub) => sub.toUpperCase() }\n274:  *\n275:  * @return a React <span> component if any non-strings were used in substitutions, otherwise a string\n276:  */\n277: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables): string;\n278: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: Tags): React.ReactNode;\n279: export function replaceByRegexes(text: string, mapping: IVariables | Tags): string | React.ReactNode {\n280:     // We initially store our output as an array of strings and objects (e.g. React components).\n281:     // This will then be converted to a string or a <span> at the end\n282:     const output = [text];\n283: \n284:     // If we insert any components we need to wrap the output in a span. React doesn't like just an array of components.\n285:     let shouldWrapInSpan = false;\n286: \n287:     for (const regexpString in mapping) {\n288:         // TODO: Cache regexps\n289:         const regexp = new RegExp(regexpString, \"g\");\n290: \n291:         // Loop over what output we have so far and perform replacements\n292:         // We look for matches: if we find one, we get three parts: everything before the match, the replaced part,\n293:         // and everything after the match. Insert all three into the output. We need to do this because we can insert objects.\n294:         // Otherwise there would be no need for the splitting and we could do simple replacement.\n295:         let matchFoundSomewhere = false; // If we don't find a match anywhere we want to log it\n296:         for (let outputIndex = 0; outputIndex < output.length; outputIndex++) {\n297:             const inputText = output[outputIndex];\n298:             if (typeof inputText !== 'string') { // We might have inserted objects earlier, don't try to replace them\n299:                 continue;\n300:             }\n301: \n302:             // process every match in the string\n303:             // starting with the first\n304:             let match = regexp.exec(inputText);\n305: \n306:             if (!match) continue;\n307:             matchFoundSomewhere = true;\n308: \n309:             // The textual part before the first match\n310:             const head = inputText.slice(0, match.index);\n311: \n312:             const parts = [];\n313:             // keep track of prevMatch\n314:             let prevMatch;\n315:             while (match) {\n316:                 // store prevMatch\n317:                 prevMatch = match;\n318:                 const capturedGroups = match.slice(2);\n319: \n320:                 let replaced;\n321:                 // If substitution is a function, call it\n322:                 if (mapping[regexpString] instanceof Function) {\n323:                     replaced = ((mapping as Tags)[regexpString] as Function)(...capturedGroups);\n324:                 } else {\n325:                     replaced = mapping[regexpString];\n326:                 }\n327: \n328:                 if (typeof replaced === 'object') {\n329:                     shouldWrapInSpan = true;\n330:                 }\n331: \n332:                 // Here we also need to check that it actually is a string before comparing against one\n333:                 // The head and tail are always strings\n334:                 if (typeof replaced !== 'string' || replaced !== '') {\n335:                     parts.push(replaced);\n336:                 }\n337: \n338:                 // try the next match\n339:                 match = regexp.exec(inputText);\n340: \n341:                 // add the text between prevMatch and this one\n342:                 // or the end of the string if prevMatch is the last match\n343:                 let tail;\n344:                 if (match) {\n345:                     const startIndex = prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length;\n346:                     tail = inputText.slice(startIndex, match.index);\n347:                 } else {\n348:                     tail = inputText.slice(prevMatch.index + prevMatch[0].length);\n349:                 }\n350:                 if (tail) {\n351:                     parts.push(tail);\n352:                 }\n353:             }\n354: \n355:             // Insert in reverse order as splice does insert-before and this way we get the final order correct\n356:             // remove the old element at the same time\n357:             output.splice(outputIndex, 1, ...parts);\n358: \n359:             if (head !== '') { // Don't push empty nodes, they are of no use\n360:                 output.splice(outputIndex, 0, head);\n361:             }\n362:         }\n363:         if (!matchFoundSomewhere) { // The current regexp did not match anything in the input\n364:             // Missing matches is entirely possible because you might choose to show some variables only in the case\n365:             // of e.g. plurals. It's still a bit suspicious, and could be due to an error, so log it.\n366:             // However, not showing count is so common that it's not worth logging. And other commonly unused variables\n367:             // here, if there are any.\n368:             if (regexpString !== '%\\\\(count\\\\)s') {\n369:                 logger.log(`Could not find ${regexp} in ${text}`);\n370:             }\n371:         }\n372:     }\n373: \n374:     if (shouldWrapInSpan) {\n375:         return React.createElement('span', null, ...output);\n376:     } else {\n377:         return output.join('');\n378:     }\n379: }\n380: \n381: // Allow overriding the text displayed when no translation exists\n382: // Currently only used in unit tests to avoid having to load\n383: // the translations in element-web\n384: export function setMissingEntryGenerator(f: (value: string) => void) {\n385:     counterpart.setMissingEntryGenerator(f);\n386: }\n387: \n388: type Languages = {\n389:     [lang: string]: {\n390:         fileName: string;\n391:         label: string;\n392:     };\n393: };\n394: \n395: export function setLanguage(preferredLangs: string | string[]) {\n396:     if (!Array.isArray(preferredLangs)) {\n397:         preferredLangs = [preferredLangs];\n398:     }\n399: \n400:     const plaf = PlatformPeg.get();\n401:     if (plaf) {\n402:         plaf.setLanguage(preferredLangs);\n403:     }\n404: \n405:     let langToUse: string;\n406:     let availLangs: Languages;\n407:     return getLangsJson().then((result) => {\n408:         availLangs = result;\n409: \n410:         for (let i = 0; i < preferredLangs.length; ++i) {\n411:             if (availLangs.hasOwnProperty(preferredLangs[i])) {\n412:                 langToUse = preferredLangs[i];\n413:                 break;\n414:             }\n415:         }\n416:         if (!langToUse) {\n417:             // Fallback to en_EN if none is found\n418:             langToUse = 'en';\n419:             logger.error(\"Unable to find an appropriate language\");\n420:         }\n421: \n422:         return getLanguageRetry(i18nFolder + availLangs[langToUse].fileName);\n423:     }).then(async (langData) => {\n424:         counterpart.registerTranslations(langToUse, langData);\n425:         await registerCustomTranslations();\n426:         counterpart.setLocale(langToUse);\n427:         await SettingsStore.setValue(\"language\", null, SettingLevel.DEVICE, langToUse);\n428:         // Adds a lot of noise to test runs, so disable logging there.\n429:         if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== \"test\") {\n430:             logger.log(\"set language to \" + langToUse);\n431:         }\n432: \n433:         // Set 'en' as fallback language:\n434:         if (langToUse !== \"en\") {\n435:             return getLanguageRetry(i18nFolder + availLangs['en'].fileName);\n436:         }\n437:     }).then(async (langData) => {\n438:         if (langData) counterpart.registerTranslations('en', langData);\n439:         await registerCustomTranslations();\n440:     });\n441: }\n442: \n443: type Language = {\n444:     value: string;\n445:     label: string;\n446: };\n447: \n448: export function getAllLanguagesFromJson(): Promise<Language[]> {\n449:     return getLangsJson().then((langsObject) => {\n450:         const langs: Language[] = [];\n451:         for (const langKey in langsObject) {\n452:             if (langsObject.hasOwnProperty(langKey)) {\n453:                 langs.push({\n454:                     'value': langKey,\n455:                     'label': langsObject[langKey].label,\n456:                 });\n457:             }\n458:         }\n459:         return langs;\n460:     });\n461: }\n462: \n463: export function getLanguagesFromBrowser() {\n464:     if (navigator.languages && navigator.languages.length) return navigator.languages;\n465:     if (navigator.language) return [navigator.language];\n466:     return [navigator.userLanguage || \"en\"];\n467: }\n468: \n469: export function getLanguageFromBrowser() {\n470:     return getLanguagesFromBrowser()[0];\n471: }\n472: \n473: /**\n474:  * Turns a language string, normalises it,\n475:  * (see normalizeLanguageKey) into an array of language strings\n476:  * with fallback to generic languages\n477:  * (eg. 'pt-BR' => ['pt-br', 'pt'])\n478:  *\n479:  * @param {string} language The input language string\n480:  * @return {string[]} List of normalised languages\n481:  */\n482: export function getNormalizedLanguageKeys(language: string) {\n483:     const languageKeys: string[] = [];\n484:     const normalizedLanguage = normalizeLanguageKey(language);\n485:     const languageParts = normalizedLanguage.split('-');\n486:     if (languageParts.length === 2 && languageParts[0] === languageParts[1]) {\n487:         languageKeys.push(languageParts[0]);\n488:     } else {\n489:         languageKeys.push(normalizedLanguage);\n490:         if (languageParts.length === 2) {\n491:             languageKeys.push(languageParts[0]);\n492:         }\n493:     }\n494:     return languageKeys;\n495: }\n496: \n497: /**\n498:  * Returns a language string with underscores replaced with\n499:  * hyphens, and lowercased.\n500:  *\n501:  * @param {string} language The language string to be normalized\n502:  * @returns {string} The normalized language string\n503:  */\n504: export function normalizeLanguageKey(language: string) {\n505:     return language.toLowerCase().replace(\"_\", \"-\");\n506: }\n507: \n508: export function getCurrentLanguage() {\n509:     return counterpart.getLocale();\n510: }\n511: \n512: /**\n513:  * Given a list of language codes, pick the most appropriate one\n514:  * given the current language (ie. getCurrentLanguage())\n515:  * English is assumed to be a reasonable default.\n516:  *\n517:  * @param {string[]} langs List of language codes to pick from\n518:  * @returns {string} The most appropriate language code from langs\n519:  */\n520: export function pickBestLanguage(langs: string[]): string {\n521:     const currentLang = getCurrentLanguage();\n522:     const normalisedLangs = langs.map(normalizeLanguageKey);\n523: \n524:     {\n525:         // Best is an exact match\n526:         const currentLangIndex = normalisedLangs.indexOf(currentLang);\n527:         if (currentLangIndex > -1) return langs[currentLangIndex];\n528:     }\n529: \n530:     {\n531:         // Failing that, a different dialect of the same language\n532:         const closeLangIndex = normalisedLangs.findIndex((l) => l.slice(0, 2) === currentLang.slice(0, 2));\n533:         if (closeLangIndex > -1) return langs[closeLangIndex];\n534:     }\n535: \n536:     {\n537:         // Neither of those? Try an english variant.\n538:         const enIndex = normalisedLangs.findIndex((l) => l.startsWith('en'));\n539:         if (enIndex > -1) return langs[enIndex];\n540:     }\n541: \n542:     // if nothing else, use the first\n543:     return langs[0];\n544: }\n545: \n546: async function getLangsJson(): Promise<Languages> {\n547:     let url: string;\n548:     if (typeof(webpackLangJsonUrl) === 'string') { // in Jest this 'url' isn't a URL, so just fall through\n549:         url = webpackLangJsonUrl;\n550:     } else {\n551:         url = i18nFolder + 'languages.json';\n552:     }\n553: \n554:     const res = await fetch(url, { method: \"GET\" });\n555: \n556:     if (!res.ok) {\n557:         throw new Error(`Failed to load ${url}, got ${res.status}`);\n558:     }\n559: \n560:     return res.json();\n561: }\n562: \n563: interface ICounterpartTranslation {\n564:     [key: string]: string | {\n565:         [pluralisation: string]: string;\n566:     };\n567: }\n568: \n569: async function getLanguageRetry(langPath: string, num = 3): Promise<ICounterpartTranslation> {\n570:     return retry(() => getLanguage(langPath), num, e => {\n571:         logger.log(\"Failed to load i18n\", langPath);\n572:         logger.error(e);\n573:         return true; // always retry\n574:     });\n575: }\n576: \n577: async function getLanguage(langPath: string): Promise<ICounterpartTranslation> {\n578:     const res = await fetch(langPath, { method: \"GET\" });\n579: \n580:     if (!res.ok) {\n581:         throw new Error(`Failed to load ${langPath}, got ${res.status}`);\n582:     }\n583: \n584:     return res.json();\n585: }\n586: \n587: export interface ICustomTranslations {\n588:     // Format is a map of english string to language to override\n589:     [str: string]: {\n590:         [lang: string]: string;\n591:     };\n592: }\n593: \n594: let cachedCustomTranslations: Optional<ICustomTranslations> = null;\n595: let cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = 0; // zero to trigger expiration right away\n596: \n597: // This awkward class exists so the test runner can get at the function. It is\n598: // not intended for practical or realistic usage.\n599: export class CustomTranslationOptions {\n600:     public static lookupFn: (url: string) => ICustomTranslations;\n601: \n602:     private constructor() {\n603:         // static access for tests only\n604:     }\n605: }\n606: \n607: function doRegisterTranslations(customTranslations: ICustomTranslations) {\n608:     // We convert the operator-friendly version into something counterpart can\n609:     // consume.\n610:     const langs: {\n611:         // same structure, just flipped key order\n612:         [lang: string]: {\n613:             [str: string]: string;\n614:         };\n615:     } = {};\n616:     for (const [str, translations] of Object.entries(customTranslations)) {\n617:         for (const [lang, newStr] of Object.entries(translations)) {\n618:             if (!langs[lang]) langs[lang] = {};\n619:             langs[lang][str] = newStr;\n620:         }\n621:     }\n622: \n623:     // Finally, tell counterpart about our translations\n624:     for (const [lang, translations] of Object.entries(langs)) {\n625:         counterpart.registerTranslations(lang, translations);\n626:     }\n627: }\n628: \n629: /**\n630:  * Any custom modules with translations to load are parsed first, followed by an\n631:  * optionally defined translations file in the config. If no customization is made,\n632:  * or the file can't be parsed, no action will be taken.\n633:  *\n634:  * This function should be called *after* registering other translations data to\n635:  * ensure it overrides strings properly.\n636:  */\n637: export async function registerCustomTranslations() {\n638:     const moduleTranslations = ModuleRunner.instance.allTranslations;\n639:     doRegisterTranslations(moduleTranslations);\n640: \n641:     const lookupUrl = SdkConfig.get().custom_translations_url;\n642:     if (!lookupUrl) return; // easy - nothing to do\n643: \n644:     try {\n645:         let json: ICustomTranslations;\n646:         if (Date.now() >= cachedCustomTranslationsExpire) {\n647:             json = CustomTranslationOptions.lookupFn\n648:                 ? CustomTranslationOptions.lookupFn(lookupUrl)\n649:                 : (await (await fetch(lookupUrl)).json() as ICustomTranslations);\n650:             cachedCustomTranslations = json;\n651: \n652:             // Set expiration to the future, but not too far. Just trying to avoid\n653:             // repeated, successive, calls to the server rather than anything long-term.\n654:             cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = Date.now() + (5 * 60 * 1000);\n655:         } else {\n656:             json = cachedCustomTranslations;\n657:         }\n658: \n659:         // If the (potentially cached) json is invalid, don't use it.\n660:         if (!json) return;\n661: \n662:         // Finally, register it.\n663:         doRegisterTranslations(json);\n664:     } catch (e) {\n665:         // We consume all exceptions because it's considered non-fatal for custom\n666:         // translations to break. Most failures will be during initial development\n667:         // of the json file and not (hopefully) at runtime.\n668:         logger.warn(\"Ignoring error while registering custom translations: \", e);\n669: \n670:         // Like above: trigger a cache of the json to avoid successive calls.\n671:         cachedCustomTranslationsExpire = Date.now() + (5 * 60 * 1000);\n672:     }\n673: }\n674: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/src/languageHandler.tsx"
  }
}
160. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "src/MatrixClientPeg.ts",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2015, 2016 OpenMarket Ltd\\n3: Copyright 2017 Vector Creations Ltd.\\n4: Copyright 2017, 2018, 2019 New Vector Ltd\\n5: Copyright 2019, 2020 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\\n6: \\n7: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n8: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n9: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n10: \\n11:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n12: \\n13: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n14: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n15: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n16: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n17: limitations under the License.\\n18: */\\n19: \\n20: import { ICreateClientOpts, PendingEventOrdering, RoomNameState, RoomNameType } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix';\\n21: import { IStartClientOpts, MatrixClient } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/client';\\n22: import { MemoryStore } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/store/memory';\\n23: import * as utils from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/utils';\\n24: import { EventTimeline } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event-timeline';\\n25: import { EventTimelineSet } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event-timeline-set';\\n26: import { verificationMethods } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/crypto';\\n27: import { SHOW_QR_CODE_METHOD } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/crypto/verification/QRCode\\\";\\n28: import { logger } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\\\";\\n29: \\n30: import createMatrixClient from './utils/createMatrixClient';\\n31: import SettingsStore from './settings/SettingsStore';\\n32: import MatrixActionCreators from './actions/MatrixActionCreators';\\n33: import Modal from './Modal';\\n34: import MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler from \\\"./settings/handlers/MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler\\\";\\n35: import * as StorageManager from './utils/StorageManager';\\n36: import IdentityAuthClient from './IdentityAuthClient';\\n37: import { crossSigningCallbacks, tryToUnlockSecretStorageWithDehydrationKey } from './SecurityManager';\\n38: import SecurityCustomisations from \\\"./customisations/Security\\\";\\n39: import { SlidingSyncManager } from './SlidingSyncManager';\\n40: import CryptoStoreTooNewDialog from \\\"./components/views/dialogs/CryptoStoreTooNewDialog\\\";\\n41: import { _t } from \\\"./languageHandler\\\";\\n42: \\n43: export interface IMatrixClientCreds {\\n44:     homeserverUrl: string;\\n45:     identityServerUrl?: string;\\n46:     userId: string;\\n47:     deviceId?: string;\\n48:     accessToken: string;\\n49:     guest?: boolean;\\n50:     pickleKey?: string;\\n51:     freshLogin?: boolean;\\n52: }\\n53: \\n54: /**\\n55:  * Holds the current instance of the `MatrixClient` to use across the codebase.\\n56:  * Looking for an `MatrixClient`? Just look for the `MatrixClientPeg` on the peg\\n57:  * board. \\\"Peg\\\" is the literal meaning of something you hang something on. So\\n58:  * you'll find a `MatrixClient` hanging on the `MatrixClientPeg`.\\n59:  */\\n60: export interface IMatrixClientPeg {\\n61:     opts: IStartClientOpts;\\n62: \\n63:     /**\\n64:      * Return the server name of the user's homeserver\\n65:      * Throws an error if unable to deduce the homeserver name\\n66:      * (eg. if the user is not logged in)\\n67:      *\\n68:      * @returns {string} The homeserver name, if present.\\n69:      */\\n70:     getHomeserverName(): string;\\n71: \\n72:     get(): MatrixClient;\\n73:     unset(): void;\\n74:     assign(): Promise<any>;\\n75:     start(): Promise<any>;\\n76: \\n77:     getCredentials(): IMatrixClientCreds;\\n78: \\n79:     /**\\n80:      * If we've registered a user ID we set this to the ID of the\\n81:      * user we've just registered. If they then go & log in, we\\n82:      * can send them to the welcome user (obviously this doesn't\\n83:      * guarantee they'll get a chat with the welcome user).\\n84:      *\\n85:      * @param {string} uid The user ID of the user we've just registered\\n86:      */\\n87:     setJustRegisteredUserId(uid: string | null): void;\\n88: \\n89:     /**\\n90:      * Returns true if the current user has just been registered by this\\n91:      * client as determined by setJustRegisteredUserId()\\n92:      *\\n93:      * @returns {bool} True if user has just been registered\\n94:      */\\n95:     currentUserIsJustRegistered(): boolean;\\n96: \\n97:     /**\\n98:      * If the current user has been registered by this device then this\\n99:      * returns a boolean of whether it was within the last N hours given.\\n100:      */\\n101:     userRegisteredWithinLastHours(hours: number): boolean;\\n102: \\n103:     /**\\n104:      * If the current user has been registered by this device then this\\n105:      * returns a boolean of whether it was after a given timestamp.\\n106:      */\\n107:     userRegisteredAfter(date: Date): boolean;\\n108: \\n109:     /**\\n110:      * Replace this MatrixClientPeg's client with a client instance that has\\n111:      * homeserver / identity server URLs and active credentials\\n112:      *\\n113:      * @param {IMatrixClientCreds} creds The new credentials to use.\\n114:      */\\n115:     replaceUsingCreds(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void;\\n116: }\\n117: \\n118: /**\\n119:  * Wrapper object for handling the js-sdk Matrix Client object in the react-sdk\\n120:  * Handles the creation/initialisation of client objects.\\n121:  * This module provides a singleton instance of this class so the 'current'\\n122:  * Matrix Client object is available easily.\\n123:  */\\n124: class MatrixClientPegClass implements IMatrixClientPeg {\\n125:     // These are the default options used when when the\\n126:     // client is started in 'start'. These can be altered\\n127:     // at any time up to after the 'will_start_client'\\n128:     // event is finished processing.\\n129:     public opts: IStartClientOpts = {\\n130:         initialSyncLimit: 20,\\n131:     };\\n132: \\n133:     private matrixClient: MatrixClient = null;\\n134:     private justRegisteredUserId: string | null = null;\\n135: \\n136:     // the credentials used to init the current client object.\\n137:     // used if we tear it down & recreate it with a different store\\n138:     private currentClientCreds: IMatrixClientCreds;\\n139: \\n140:     public get(): MatrixClient {\\n141:         return this.matrixClient;\\n142:     }\\n143: \\n144:     public unset(): void {\\n145:         this.matrixClient = null;\\n146: \\n147:         MatrixActionCreators.stop();\\n148:     }\\n149: \\n150:     public setJustRegisteredUserId(uid: string | null): void {\\n151:         this.justRegisteredUserId = uid;\\n152:         if (uid) {\\n153:             const registrationTime = Date.now().toString();\\n154:             window.localStorage.setItem(\\\"mx_registration_time\\\", registrationTime);\\n155:         }\\n156:     }\\n157: \\n158:     public currentUserIsJustRegistered(): boolean {\\n159:         return (\\n160:             this.matrixClient &&\\n161:             this.matrixClient.credentials.userId === this.justRegisteredUserId\\n162:         );\\n163:     }\\n164: \\n165:     public userRegisteredWithinLastHours(hours: number): boolean {\\n166:         if (hours <= 0) {\\n167:             return false;\\n168:         }\\n169: \\n170:         try {\\n171:             const registrationTime = parseInt(window.localStorage.getItem(\\\"mx_registration_time\\\"), 10);\\n172:             const diff = Date.now() - registrationTime;\\n173:             return (diff / 36e5) <= hours;\\n174:         } catch (e) {\\n175:             return false;\\n176:         }\\n177:     }\\n178: \\n179:     public userRegisteredAfter(timestamp: Date): boolean {\\n180:         try {\\n181:             const registrationTime = parseInt(window.localStorage.getItem(\\\"mx_registration_time\\\"), 10);\\n182:             return timestamp.getTime() <= registrationTime;\\n183:         } catch (e) {\\n184:             return false;\\n185:         }\\n186:     }\\n187: \\n188:     public replaceUsingCreds(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void {\\n189:         this.currentClientCreds = creds;\\n190:         this.createClient(creds);\\n191:     }\\n192: \\n193:     public async assign(): Promise<any> {\\n194:         for (const dbType of ['indexeddb', 'memory']) {\\n195:             try {\\n196:                 const promise = this.matrixClient.store.startup();\\n197:                 logger.log(\\\"MatrixClientPeg: waiting for MatrixClient store to initialise\\\");\\n198:                 await promise;\\n199:                 break;\\n200:             } catch (err) {\\n201:                 if (dbType === 'indexeddb') {\\n202:                     logger.error('Error starting matrixclient store - falling back to memory store', err);\\n203:                     this.matrixClient.store = new MemoryStore({\\n204:                         localStorage: localStorage,\\n205:                     });\\n206:                 } else {\\n207:                     logger.error('Failed to start memory store!', err);\\n208:                     throw err;\\n209:                 }\\n210:             }\\n211:         }\\n212: \\n213:         // try to initialise e2e on the new client\\n214:         try {\\n215:             // check that we have a version of the js-sdk which includes initCrypto\\n216:             if (!SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"lowBandwidth\\\") && this.matrixClient.initCrypto) {\\n217:                 await this.matrixClient.initCrypto();\\n218:                 this.matrixClient.setCryptoTrustCrossSignedDevices(\\n219:                     !SettingsStore.getValue('e2ee.manuallyVerifyAllSessions'),\\n220:                 );\\n221:                 await tryToUnlockSecretStorageWithDehydrationKey(this.matrixClient);\\n222:                 StorageManager.setCryptoInitialised(true);\\n223:             }\\n224:         } catch (e) {\\n225:             if (e && e.name === 'InvalidCryptoStoreError') {\\n226:                 // The js-sdk found a crypto DB too new for it to use\\n227:                 Modal.createDialog(CryptoStoreTooNewDialog);\\n228:             }\\n229:             // this can happen for a number of reasons, the most likely being\\n230:             // that the olm library was missing. It's not fatal.\\n231:             logger.warn(\\\"Unable to initialise e2e\\\", e);\\n232:         }\\n233: \\n234:         const opts = utils.deepCopy(this.opts);\\n235:         // the react sdk doesn't work without this, so don't allow\\n236:         opts.pendingEventOrdering = PendingEventOrdering.Detached;\\n237:         opts.lazyLoadMembers = true;\\n238:         opts.clientWellKnownPollPeriod = 2 * 60 * 60; // 2 hours\\n239:         opts.experimentalThreadSupport = SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"feature_thread\\\");\\n240: \\n241:         if (SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"feature_sliding_sync\\\")) {\\n242:             const proxyUrl = SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"feature_sliding_sync_proxy_url\\\");\\n243:             if (proxyUrl) {\\n244:                 logger.log(\\\"Activating sliding sync using proxy at \\\", proxyUrl);\\n245:             } else {\\n246:                 logger.log(\\\"Activating sliding sync\\\");\\n247:             }\\n248:             opts.slidingSync = SlidingSyncManager.instance.configure(\\n249:                 this.matrixClient,\\n250:                 proxyUrl || this.matrixClient.baseUrl,\\n251:             );\\n252:             SlidingSyncManager.instance.startSpidering(100, 50); // 100 rooms at a time, 50ms apart\\n253:         }\\n254: \\n255:         // Connect the matrix client to the dispatcher and setting handlers\\n256:         MatrixActionCreators.start(this.matrixClient);\\n257:         MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler.matrixClient = this.matrixClient;\\n258: \\n259:         return opts;\\n260:     }\\n261: \\n262:     public async start(): Promise<any> {\\n263:         const opts = await this.assign();\\n264: \\n265:         logger.log(`MatrixClientPeg: really starting MatrixClient`);\\n266:         await this.get().startClient(opts);\\n267:         logger.log(`MatrixClientPeg: MatrixClient started`);\\n268:     }\\n269: \\n270:     public getCredentials(): IMatrixClientCreds {\\n271:         let copiedCredentials = this.currentClientCreds;\\n272:         if (this.currentClientCreds?.userId !== this.matrixClient?.credentials?.userId) {\\n273:             // cached credentials belong to a different user - don't use them\\n274:             copiedCredentials = null;\\n275:         }\\n276:         return {\\n277:             // Copy the cached credentials before overriding what we can.\\n278:             ...(copiedCredentials ?? {}),\\n279: \\n280:             homeserverUrl: this.matrixClient.baseUrl,\\n281:             identityServerUrl: this.matrixClient.idBaseUrl,\\n282:             userId: this.matrixClient.credentials.userId,\\n283:             deviceId: this.matrixClient.getDeviceId(),\\n284:             accessToken: this.matrixClient.getAccessToken(),\\n285:             guest: this.matrixClient.isGuest(),\\n286:         };\\n287:     }\\n288: \\n289:     public getHomeserverName(): string {\\n290:         const matches = /^@[^:]+:(.+)$/.exec(this.matrixClient.credentials.userId);\\n291:         if (matches === null || matches.length < 1) {\\n292:             throw new Error(\\\"Failed to derive homeserver name from user ID!\\\");\\n293:         }\\n294:         return matches[1];\\n295:     }\\n296: \\n297:     private namesToRoomName(names: string[], count: number): string | undefined {\\n298:         const countWithoutMe = count - 1;\\n299:         if (!names.length) {\\n300:             return _t(\\\"Empty room\\\");\\n301:         }\\n302:         if (names.length === 1 && countWithoutMe <= 1) {\\n303:             return names[0];\\n304:         }\\n305:     }\\n306: \\n307:     private memberNamesToRoomName(names: string[], count: number): string {\\n308:         const name = this.namesToRoomName(names, count);\\n309:         if (name) return name;\\n310: \\n311:         if (names.length === 2 && count === 2) {\\n312:             return _t(\\\"%(user1)s and %(user2)s\\\", {\\n313:                 user1: names[0],\\n314:                 user2: names[1],\\n315:             });\\n316:         }\\n317:         return _t(\\\"%(user)s and %(count)s others\\\", {\\n318:             user: names[0],\\n319:             count: count - 1,\\n320:         });\\n321:     }\\n322: \\n323:     private inviteeNamesToRoomName(names: string[], count: number): string {\\n324:         const name = this.namesToRoomName(names, count);\\n325:         if (name) return name;\\n326: \\n327:         if (names.length === 2 && count === 2) {\\n328:             return _t(\\\"Inviting %(user1)s and %(user2)s\\\", {\\n329:                 user1: names[0],\\n330:                 user2: names[1],\\n331:             });\\n332:         }\\n333:         return _t(\\\"Inviting %(user)s and %(count)s others\\\", {\\n334:             user: names[0],\\n335:             count: count - 1,\\n336:         });\\n337:     }\\n338: \\n339:     private createClient(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void {\\n340:         const opts: ICreateClientOpts = {\\n341:             baseUrl: creds.homeserverUrl,\\n342:             idBaseUrl: creds.identityServerUrl,\\n343:             accessToken: creds.accessToken,\\n344:             userId: creds.userId,\\n345:             deviceId: creds.deviceId,\\n346:             pickleKey: creds.pickleKey,\\n347:             timelineSupport: true,\\n348:             forceTURN: !SettingsStore.getValue('webRtcAllowPeerToPeer'),\\n349:             fallbackICEServerAllowed: !!SettingsStore.getValue('fallbackICEServerAllowed'),\\n350:             // Gather up to 20 ICE candidates when a call arrives: this should be more than we'd\\n351:             // ever normally need, so effectively this should make all the gathering happen when\\n352:             // the call arrives.\\n353:             iceCandidatePoolSize: 20,\\n354:             verificationMethods: [\\n355:                 verificationMethods.SAS,\\n356:                 SHOW_QR_CODE_METHOD,\\n357:                 verificationMethods.RECIPROCATE_QR_CODE,\\n358:             ],\\n359:             identityServer: new IdentityAuthClient(),\\n360:             // These are always installed regardless of the labs flag so that cross-signing features\\n361:             // can toggle on without reloading and also be accessed immediately after login.\\n362:             cryptoCallbacks: { ...crossSigningCallbacks },\\n363:             roomNameGenerator: (_: string, state: RoomNameState) => {\\n364:                 switch (state.type) {\\n365:                     case RoomNameType.Generated:\\n366:                         switch (state.subtype) {\\n367:                             case \\\"Inviting\\\":\\n368:                                 return this.inviteeNamesToRoomName(state.names, state.count);\\n369:                             default:\\n370:                                 return this.memberNamesToRoomName(state.names, state.count);\\n371:                         }\\n372:                     case RoomNameType.EmptyRoom:\\n373:                         if (state.oldName) {\\n374:                             return _t(\\\"Empty room (was %(oldName)s)\\\", {\\n375:                                 oldName: state.oldName,\\n376:                             });\\n377:                         } else {\\n378:                             return _t(\\\"Empty room\\\");\\n379:                         }\\n380:                     default:\\n381:                         return null;\\n382:                 }\\n383:             },\\n384:         };\\n385: \\n386:         if (SecurityCustomisations.getDehydrationKey) {\\n387:             opts.cryptoCallbacks!.getDehydrationKey = SecurityCustomisations.getDehydrationKey;\\n388:         }\\n389: \\n390:         this.matrixClient = createMatrixClient(opts);\\n391: \\n392:         // we're going to add eventlisteners for each matrix event tile, so the\\n393:         // potential number of event listeners is quite high.\\n394:         this.matrixClient.setMaxListeners(500);\\n395: \\n396:         this.matrixClient.setGuest(Boolean(creds.guest));\\n397: \\n398:         const notifTimelineSet = new EventTimelineSet(undefined, {\\n399:             timelineSupport: true,\\n400:             pendingEvents: false,\\n401:         });\\n402:         // XXX: what is our initial pagination token?! it somehow needs to be synchronised with /sync.\\n403:         notifTimelineSet.getLiveTimeline().setPaginationToken(\\\"\\\", EventTimeline.BACKWARDS);\\n404:         this.matrixClient.setNotifTimelineSet(notifTimelineSet);\\n405:     }\\n406: }\\n407: \\n408: /**\\n409:  * Note: You should be using a React context with access to a client rather than\\n410:  * using this, as in a multi-account world this will not exist!\\n411:  */\\n412: export const MatrixClientPeg: IMatrixClientPeg = new MatrixClientPegClass();\\n413: \\n414: if (!window.mxMatrixClientPeg) {\\n415:     window.mxMatrixClientPeg = MatrixClientPeg;\\n416: }\\n417: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/src/MatrixClientPeg.ts\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2015, 2016 OpenMarket Ltd\n3: Copyright 2017 Vector Creations Ltd.\n4: Copyright 2017, 2018, 2019 New Vector Ltd\n5: Copyright 2019, 2020 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n6: \n7: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n8: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n9: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n10: \n11:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n12: \n13: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n14: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n15: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n16: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n17: limitations under the License.\n18: */\n19: \n20: import { ICreateClientOpts, PendingEventOrdering, RoomNameState, RoomNameType } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/matrix';\n21: import { IStartClientOpts, MatrixClient } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/client';\n22: import { MemoryStore } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/store/memory';\n23: import * as utils from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/utils';\n24: import { EventTimeline } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event-timeline';\n25: import { EventTimelineSet } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/models/event-timeline-set';\n26: import { verificationMethods } from 'matrix-js-sdk/src/crypto';\n27: import { SHOW_QR_CODE_METHOD } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/crypto/verification/QRCode\";\n28: import { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\n29: \n30: import createMatrixClient from './utils/createMatrixClient';\n31: import SettingsStore from './settings/SettingsStore';\n32: import MatrixActionCreators from './actions/MatrixActionCreators';\n33: import Modal from './Modal';\n34: import MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler from \"./settings/handlers/MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler\";\n35: import * as StorageManager from './utils/StorageManager';\n36: import IdentityAuthClient from './IdentityAuthClient';\n37: import { crossSigningCallbacks, tryToUnlockSecretStorageWithDehydrationKey } from './SecurityManager';\n38: import SecurityCustomisations from \"./customisations/Security\";\n39: import { SlidingSyncManager } from './SlidingSyncManager';\n40: import CryptoStoreTooNewDialog from \"./components/views/dialogs/CryptoStoreTooNewDialog\";\n41: import { _t } from \"./languageHandler\";\n42: \n43: export interface IMatrixClientCreds {\n44:     homeserverUrl: string;\n45:     identityServerUrl?: string;\n46:     userId: string;\n47:     deviceId?: string;\n48:     accessToken: string;\n49:     guest?: boolean;\n50:     pickleKey?: string;\n51:     freshLogin?: boolean;\n52: }\n53: \n54: /**\n55:  * Holds the current instance of the `MatrixClient` to use across the codebase.\n56:  * Looking for an `MatrixClient`? Just look for the `MatrixClientPeg` on the peg\n57:  * board. \"Peg\" is the literal meaning of something you hang something on. So\n58:  * you'll find a `MatrixClient` hanging on the `MatrixClientPeg`.\n59:  */\n60: export interface IMatrixClientPeg {\n61:     opts: IStartClientOpts;\n62: \n63:     /**\n64:      * Return the server name of the user's homeserver\n65:      * Throws an error if unable to deduce the homeserver name\n66:      * (eg. if the user is not logged in)\n67:      *\n68:      * @returns {string} The homeserver name, if present.\n69:      */\n70:     getHomeserverName(): string;\n71: \n72:     get(): MatrixClient;\n73:     unset(): void;\n74:     assign(): Promise<any>;\n75:     start(): Promise<any>;\n76: \n77:     getCredentials(): IMatrixClientCreds;\n78: \n79:     /**\n80:      * If we've registered a user ID we set this to the ID of the\n81:      * user we've just registered. If they then go & log in, we\n82:      * can send them to the welcome user (obviously this doesn't\n83:      * guarantee they'll get a chat with the welcome user).\n84:      *\n85:      * @param {string} uid The user ID of the user we've just registered\n86:      */\n87:     setJustRegisteredUserId(uid: string | null): void;\n88: \n89:     /**\n90:      * Returns true if the current user has just been registered by this\n91:      * client as determined by setJustRegisteredUserId()\n92:      *\n93:      * @returns {bool} True if user has just been registered\n94:      */\n95:     currentUserIsJustRegistered(): boolean;\n96: \n97:     /**\n98:      * If the current user has been registered by this device then this\n99:      * returns a boolean of whether it was within the last N hours given.\n100:      */\n101:     userRegisteredWithinLastHours(hours: number): boolean;\n102: \n103:     /**\n104:      * If the current user has been registered by this device then this\n105:      * returns a boolean of whether it was after a given timestamp.\n106:      */\n107:     userRegisteredAfter(date: Date): boolean;\n108: \n109:     /**\n110:      * Replace this MatrixClientPeg's client with a client instance that has\n111:      * homeserver / identity server URLs and active credentials\n112:      *\n113:      * @param {IMatrixClientCreds} creds The new credentials to use.\n114:      */\n115:     replaceUsingCreds(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void;\n116: }\n117: \n118: /**\n119:  * Wrapper object for handling the js-sdk Matrix Client object in the react-sdk\n120:  * Handles the creation/initialisation of client objects.\n121:  * This module provides a singleton instance of this class so the 'current'\n122:  * Matrix Client object is available easily.\n123:  */\n124: class MatrixClientPegClass implements IMatrixClientPeg {\n125:     // These are the default options used when when the\n126:     // client is started in 'start'. These can be altered\n127:     // at any time up to after the 'will_start_client'\n128:     // event is finished processing.\n129:     public opts: IStartClientOpts = {\n130:         initialSyncLimit: 20,\n131:     };\n132: \n133:     private matrixClient: MatrixClient = null;\n134:     private justRegisteredUserId: string | null = null;\n135: \n136:     // the credentials used to init the current client object.\n137:     // used if we tear it down & recreate it with a different store\n138:     private currentClientCreds: IMatrixClientCreds;\n139: \n140:     public get(): MatrixClient {\n141:         return this.matrixClient;\n142:     }\n143: \n144:     public unset(): void {\n145:         this.matrixClient = null;\n146: \n147:         MatrixActionCreators.stop();\n148:     }\n149: \n150:     public setJustRegisteredUserId(uid: string | null): void {\n151:         this.justRegisteredUserId = uid;\n152:         if (uid) {\n153:             const registrationTime = Date.now().toString();\n154:             window.localStorage.setItem(\"mx_registration_time\", registrationTime);\n155:         }\n156:     }\n157: \n158:     public currentUserIsJustRegistered(): boolean {\n159:         return (\n160:             this.matrixClient &&\n161:             this.matrixClient.credentials.userId === this.justRegisteredUserId\n162:         );\n163:     }\n164: \n165:     public userRegisteredWithinLastHours(hours: number): boolean {\n166:         if (hours <= 0) {\n167:             return false;\n168:         }\n169: \n170:         try {\n171:             const registrationTime = parseInt(window.localStorage.getItem(\"mx_registration_time\"), 10);\n172:             const diff = Date.now() - registrationTime;\n173:             return (diff / 36e5) <= hours;\n174:         } catch (e) {\n175:             return false;\n176:         }\n177:     }\n178: \n179:     public userRegisteredAfter(timestamp: Date): boolean {\n180:         try {\n181:             const registrationTime = parseInt(window.localStorage.getItem(\"mx_registration_time\"), 10);\n182:             return timestamp.getTime() <= registrationTime;\n183:         } catch (e) {\n184:             return false;\n185:         }\n186:     }\n187: \n188:     public replaceUsingCreds(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void {\n189:         this.currentClientCreds = creds;\n190:         this.createClient(creds);\n191:     }\n192: \n193:     public async assign(): Promise<any> {\n194:         for (const dbType of ['indexeddb', 'memory']) {\n195:             try {\n196:                 const promise = this.matrixClient.store.startup();\n197:                 logger.log(\"MatrixClientPeg: waiting for MatrixClient store to initialise\");\n198:                 await promise;\n199:                 break;\n200:             } catch (err) {\n201:                 if (dbType === 'indexeddb') {\n202:                     logger.error('Error starting matrixclient store -
