falling back to memory store', err);\n203:                     this.matrixClient.store = new MemoryStore({\n204:                         localStorage: localStorage,\n205:                     });\n206:                 } else {\n207:                     logger.error('Failed to start memory store!', err);\n208:                     throw err;\n209:                 }\n210:             }\n211:         }\n212: \n213:         // try to initialise e2e on the new client\n214:         try {\n215:             // check that we have a version of the js-sdk which includes initCrypto\n216:             if (!SettingsStore.getValue(\"lowBandwidth\") && this.matrixClient.initCrypto) {\n217:                 await this.matrixClient.initCrypto();\n218:                 this.matrixClient.setCryptoTrustCrossSignedDevices(\n219:                     !SettingsStore.getValue('e2ee.manuallyVerifyAllSessions'),\n220:                 );\n221:                 await tryToUnlockSecretStorageWithDehydrationKey(this.matrixClient);\n222:                 StorageManager.setCryptoInitialised(true);\n223:             }\n224:         } catch (e) {\n225:             if (e && e.name === 'InvalidCryptoStoreError') {\n226:                 // The js-sdk found a crypto DB too new for it to use\n227:                 Modal.createDialog(CryptoStoreTooNewDialog);\n228:             }\n229:             // this can happen for a number of reasons, the most likely being\n230:             // that the olm library was missing. It's not fatal.\n231:             logger.warn(\"Unable to initialise e2e\", e);\n232:         }\n233: \n234:         const opts = utils.deepCopy(this.opts);\n235:         // the react sdk doesn't work without this, so don't allow\n236:         opts.pendingEventOrdering = PendingEventOrdering.Detached;\n237:         opts.lazyLoadMembers = true;\n238:         opts.clientWellKnownPollPeriod = 2 * 60 * 60; // 2 hours\n239:         opts.experimentalThreadSupport = SettingsStore.getValue(\"feature_thread\");\n240: \n241:         if (SettingsStore.getValue(\"feature_sliding_sync\")) {\n242:             const proxyUrl = SettingsStore.getValue(\"feature_sliding_sync_proxy_url\");\n243:             if (proxyUrl) {\n244:                 logger.log(\"Activating sliding sync using proxy at \", proxyUrl);\n245:             } else {\n246:                 logger.log(\"Activating sliding sync\");\n247:             }\n248:             opts.slidingSync = SlidingSyncManager.instance.configure(\n249:                 this.matrixClient,\n250:                 proxyUrl || this.matrixClient.baseUrl,\n251:             );\n252:             SlidingSyncManager.instance.startSpidering(100, 50); // 100 rooms at a time, 50ms apart\n253:         }\n254: \n255:         // Connect the matrix client to the dispatcher and setting handlers\n256:         MatrixActionCreators.start(this.matrixClient);\n257:         MatrixClientBackedSettingsHandler.matrixClient = this.matrixClient;\n258: \n259:         return opts;\n260:     }\n261: \n262:     public async start(): Promise<any> {\n263:         const opts = await this.assign();\n264: \n265:         logger.log(`MatrixClientPeg: really starting MatrixClient`);\n266:         await this.get().startClient(opts);\n267:         logger.log(`MatrixClientPeg: MatrixClient started`);\n268:     }\n269: \n270:     public getCredentials(): IMatrixClientCreds {\n271:         let copiedCredentials = this.currentClientCreds;\n272:         if (this.currentClientCreds?.userId !== this.matrixClient?.credentials?.userId) {\n273:             // cached credentials belong to a different user - don't use them\n274:             copiedCredentials = null;\n275:         }\n276:         return {\n277:             // Copy the cached credentials before overriding what we can.\n278:             ...(copiedCredentials ?? {}),\n279: \n280:             homeserverUrl: this.matrixClient.baseUrl,\n281:             identityServerUrl: this.matrixClient.idBaseUrl,\n282:             userId: this.matrixClient.credentials.userId,\n283:             deviceId: this.matrixClient.getDeviceId(),\n284:             accessToken: this.matrixClient.getAccessToken(),\n285:             guest: this.matrixClient.isGuest(),\n286:         };\n287:     }\n288: \n289:     public getHomeserverName(): string {\n290:         const matches = /^@[^:]+:(.+)$/.exec(this.matrixClient.credentials.userId);\n291:         if (matches === null || matches.length < 1) {\n292:             throw new Error(\"Failed to derive homeserver name from user ID!\");\n293:         }\n294:         return matches[1];\n295:     }\n296: \n297:     private namesToRoomName(names: string[], count: number): string | undefined {\n298:         const countWithoutMe = count - 1;\n299:         if (!names.length) {\n300:             return _t(\"Empty room\");\n301:         }\n302:         if (names.length === 1 && countWithoutMe <= 1) {\n303:             return names[0];\n304:         }\n305:     }\n306: \n307:     private memberNamesToRoomName(names: string[], count: number): string {\n308:         const name = this.namesToRoomName(names, count);\n309:         if (name) return name;\n310: \n311:         if (names.length === 2 && count === 2) {\n312:             return _t(\"%(user1)s and %(user2)s\", {\n313:                 user1: names[0],\n314:                 user2: names[1],\n315:             });\n316:         }\n317:         return _t(\"%(user)s and %(count)s others\", {\n318:             user: names[0],\n319:             count: count - 1,\n320:         });\n321:     }\n322: \n323:     private inviteeNamesToRoomName(names: string[], count: number): string {\n324:         const name = this.namesToRoomName(names, count);\n325:         if (name) return name;\n326: \n327:         if (names.length === 2 && count === 2) {\n328:             return _t(\"Inviting %(user1)s and %(user2)s\", {\n329:                 user1: names[0],\n330:                 user2: names[1],\n331:             });\n332:         }\n333:         return _t(\"Inviting %(user)s and %(count)s others\", {\n334:             user: names[0],\n335:             count: count - 1,\n336:         });\n337:     }\n338: \n339:     private createClient(creds: IMatrixClientCreds): void {\n340:         const opts: ICreateClientOpts = {\n341:             baseUrl: creds.homeserverUrl,\n342:             idBaseUrl: creds.identityServerUrl,\n343:             accessToken: creds.accessToken,\n344:             userId: creds.userId,\n345:             deviceId: creds.deviceId,\n346:             pickleKey: creds.pickleKey,\n347:             timelineSupport: true,\n348:             forceTURN: !SettingsStore.getValue('webRtcAllowPeerToPeer'),\n349:             fallbackICEServerAllowed: !!SettingsStore.getValue('fallbackICEServerAllowed'),\n350:             // Gather up to 20 ICE candidates when a call arrives: this should be more than we'd\n351:             // ever normally need, so effectively this should make all the gathering happen when\n352:             // the call arrives.\n353:             iceCandidatePoolSize: 20,\n354:             verificationMethods: [\n355:                 verificationMethods.SAS,\n356:                 SHOW_QR_CODE_METHOD,\n357:                 verificationMethods.RECIPROCATE_QR_CODE,\n358:             ],\n359:             identityServer: new IdentityAuthClient(),\n360:             // These are always installed regardless of the labs flag so that cross-signing features\n361:             // can toggle on without reloading and also be accessed immediately after login.\n362:             cryptoCallbacks: { ...crossSigningCallbacks },\n363:             roomNameGenerator: (_: string, state: RoomNameState) => {\n364:                 switch (state.type) {\n365:                     case RoomNameType.Generated:\n366:                         switch (state.subtype) {\n367:                             case \"Inviting\":\n368:                                 return this.inviteeNamesToRoomName(state.names, state.count);\n369:                             default:\n370:                                 return this.memberNamesToRoomName(state.names, state.count);\n371:                         }\n372:                     case RoomNameType.EmptyRoom:\n373:                         if (state.oldName) {\n374:                             return _t(\"Empty room (was %(oldName)s)\", {\n375:                                 oldName: state.oldName,\n376:                             });\n377:                         } else {\n378:                             return _t(\"Empty room\");\n379:                         }\n380:                     default:\n381:                         return null;\n382:                 }\n383:             },\n384:         };\n385: \n386:         if (SecurityCustomisations.getDehydrationKey) {\n387:             opts.cryptoCallbacks!.getDehydrationKey = SecurityCustomisations.getDehydrationKey;\n388:         }\n389: \n390:         this.matrixClient = createMatrixClient(opts);\n391: \n392:         // we're going to add eventlisteners for each matrix event tile, so the\n393:         // potential number of event listeners is quite high.\n394:         this.matrixClient.setMaxListeners(500);\n395: \n396:         this.matrixClient.setGuest(Boolean(creds.guest));\n397: \n398:         const notifTimelineSet = new EventTimelineSet(undefined, {\n399:             timelineSupport: true,\n400:             pendingEvents: false,\n401:         });\n402:         // XXX: what is our initial pagination token?! it somehow needs to be synchronised with /sync.\n403:         notifTimelineSet.getLiveTimeline().setPaginationToken(\"\", EventTimeline.BACKWARDS);\n404:         this.matrixClient.setNotifTimelineSet(notifTimelineSet);\n405:     }\n406: }\n407: \n408: /**\n409:  * Note: You should be using a React context with access to a client rather than\n410:  * using this, as in a multi-account world this will not exist!\n411:  */\n412: export const MatrixClientPeg: IMatrixClientPeg = new MatrixClientPegClass();\n413: \n414: if (!window.mxMatrixClientPeg) {\n415:     window.mxMatrixClientPeg = MatrixClientPeg;\n416: }\n417: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/src/MatrixClientPeg.ts"
  }
}
161. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "path": "src/settings/SettingsStore.ts",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: /*\\n2: Copyright 2017 Travis Ralston\\n3: Copyright 2019, 2020 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\\n4: \\n5: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \\\"License\\\");\\n6: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\\n7: You may obtain a copy of the License at\\n8: \\n9:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\\n10: \\n11: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\\n12: distributed under the License is distributed on an \\\"AS IS\\\" BASIS,\\n13: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\\n14: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\\n15: limitations under the License.\\n16: */\\n17: \\n18: import { logger } from \\\"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\\\";\\n19: import { ReactNode } from \\\"react\\\";\\n20: \\n21: import DeviceSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/DeviceSettingsHandler\\\";\\n22: import RoomDeviceSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/RoomDeviceSettingsHandler\\\";\\n23: import DefaultSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/DefaultSettingsHandler\\\";\\n24: import RoomAccountSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/RoomAccountSettingsHandler\\\";\\n25: import AccountSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/AccountSettingsHandler\\\";\\n26: import RoomSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/RoomSettingsHandler\\\";\\n27: import ConfigSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/ConfigSettingsHandler\\\";\\n28: import { _t } from '../languageHandler';\\n29: import dis from '../dispatcher/dispatcher';\\n30: import { IFeature, ISetting, LabGroup, SETTINGS } from \\\"./Settings\\\";\\n31: import LocalEchoWrapper from \\\"./handlers/LocalEchoWrapper\\\";\\n32: import { CallbackFn as WatchCallbackFn, WatchManager } from \\\"./WatchManager\\\";\\n33: import { SettingLevel } from \\\"./SettingLevel\\\";\\n34: import SettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/SettingsHandler\\\";\\n35: import { SettingUpdatedPayload } from \\\"../dispatcher/payloads/SettingUpdatedPayload\\\";\\n36: import { Action } from \\\"../dispatcher/actions\\\";\\n37: import PlatformSettingsHandler from \\\"./handlers/PlatformSettingsHandler\\\";\\n38: import dispatcher from \\\"../dispatcher/dispatcher\\\";\\n39: import { ActionPayload } from \\\"../dispatcher/payloads\\\";\\n40: import { MatrixClientPeg } from \\\"../MatrixClientPeg\\\";\\n41: \\n42: const defaultWatchManager = new WatchManager();\\n43: \\n44: // Convert the settings to easier to manage objects for the handlers\\n45: const defaultSettings = {};\\n46: const invertedDefaultSettings = {};\\n47: const featureNames = [];\\n48: for (const key of Object.keys(SETTINGS)) {\\n49:     defaultSettings[key] = SETTINGS[key].default;\\n50:     if (SETTINGS[key].isFeature) featureNames.push(key);\\n51:     if (SETTINGS[key].invertedSettingName) {\\n52:         // Invert now so that the rest of the system will invert it back\\n53:         // to what was intended.\\n54:         invertedDefaultSettings[SETTINGS[key].invertedSettingName] = !SETTINGS[key].default;\\n55:     }\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: // Only wrap the handlers with async setters in a local echo wrapper\\n59: const LEVEL_HANDLERS = {\\n60:     [SettingLevel.DEVICE]: new DeviceSettingsHandler(featureNames, defaultWatchManager),\\n61:     [SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE]: new RoomDeviceSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager),\\n62:     [SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT]: new LocalEchoWrapper(\\n63:         new RoomAccountSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager),\\n64:         SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT,\\n65:     ),\\n66:     [SettingLevel.ACCOUNT]: new LocalEchoWrapper(new AccountSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager), SettingLevel.ACCOUNT),\\n67:     [SettingLevel.ROOM]: new LocalEchoWrapper(new RoomSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager), SettingLevel.ROOM),\\n68:     [SettingLevel.PLATFORM]: new LocalEchoWrapper(new PlatformSettingsHandler(), SettingLevel.PLATFORM),\\n69:     [SettingLevel.CONFIG]: new ConfigSettingsHandler(featureNames),\\n70:     [SettingLevel.DEFAULT]: new DefaultSettingsHandler(defaultSettings, invertedDefaultSettings),\\n71: };\\n72: \\n73: export const LEVEL_ORDER = [\\n74:     SettingLevel.DEVICE,\\n75:     SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE,\\n76:     SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT,\\n77:     SettingLevel.ACCOUNT,\\n78:     SettingLevel.ROOM,\\n79:     SettingLevel.CONFIG,\\n80:     SettingLevel.DEFAULT,\\n81: ];\\n82: \\n83: function getLevelOrder(setting: ISetting): SettingLevel[] {\\n84:     // Settings which support only a single setting level are inherently ordered\\n85:     if (setting.supportedLevelsAreOrdered || setting.supportedLevels.length === 1) {\\n86:         // return a copy to prevent callers from modifying the array\\n87:         return [...setting.supportedLevels];\\n88:     }\\n89:     return LEVEL_ORDER;\\n90: }\\n91: \\n92: export type CallbackFn = (\\n93:     settingName: string,\\n94:     roomId: string,\\n95:     atLevel: SettingLevel,\\n96:     newValAtLevel: any,\\n97:     newVal: any,\\n98: ) => void;\\n99: \\n100: interface IHandlerMap {\\n101:     // @ts-ignore - TS wants this to be a string key but we know better\\n102:     [level: SettingLevel]: SettingsHandler;\\n103: }\\n104: \\n105: /**\\n106:  * Controls and manages application settings by providing varying levels at which the\\n107:  * setting value may be specified. The levels are then used to determine what the setting\\n108:  * value should be given a set of circumstances. The levels, in priority order, are:\\n109:  * - SettingLevel.DEVICE         - Values are determined by the current device\\n110:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE    - Values are determined by the current device for a particular room\\n111:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT   - Values are determined by the current account for a particular room\\n112:  * - SettingLevel.ACCOUNT        - Values are determined by the current account\\n113:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM           - Values are determined by a particular room (by the room admins)\\n114:  * - SettingLevel.CONFIG         - Values are determined by the config.json\\n115:  * - SettingLevel.DEFAULT        - Values are determined by the hardcoded defaults\\n116:  *\\n117:  * Each level has a different method to storing the setting value. For implementation\\n118:  * specific details, please see the handlers. The \\\"config\\\" and \\\"default\\\" levels are\\n119:  * both always supported on all platforms. All other settings should be guarded by\\n120:  * isLevelSupported() prior to attempting to set the value.\\n121:  *\\n122:  * Settings can also represent features. Features are significant portions of the\\n123:  * application that warrant a dedicated setting to toggle them on or off. Features are\\n124:  * special-cased to ensure that their values respect the configuration (for example, a\\n125:  * feature may be reported as disabled even though a user has specifically requested it\\n126:  * be enabled).\\n127:  */\\n128: export default class SettingsStore {\\n129:     // We support watching settings for changes, and do this by tracking which callbacks have\\n130:     // been given to us. We end up returning the callbackRef to the caller so they can unsubscribe\\n131:     // at a later point.\\n132:     //\\n133:     // We also maintain a list of monitors which are special watchers: they cause dispatches\\n134:     // when the setting changes. We track which rooms we're monitoring though to ensure we\\n135:     // don't duplicate updates on the bus.\\n136:     private static watchers = new Map<string, WatchCallbackFn>();\\n137:     private static monitors = new Map<string, Map<string, string>>(); // { settingName => { roomId => callbackRef } }\\n138: \\n139:     // Counter used for generation of watcher IDs\\n140:     private static watcherCount = 1;\\n141: \\n142:     /**\\n143:      * Gets all the feature-style setting names.\\n144:      * @returns {string[]} The names of the feature settings.\\n145:      */\\n146:     public static getFeatureSettingNames(): string[] {\\n147:         return Object.keys(SETTINGS).filter(n => SettingsStore.isFeature(n));\\n148:     }\\n149: \\n150:     /**\\n151:      * Watches for changes in a particular setting. This is done without any local echo\\n152:      * wrapping and fires whenever a change is detected in a setting's value, at any level.\\n153:      * Watching is intended to be used in scenarios where the app needs to react to changes\\n154:      * made by other devices. It is otherwise expected that callers will be able to use the\\n155:      * Controller system or track their own changes to settings. Callers should retain the\\n156:      * returned reference to later unsubscribe from updates.\\n157:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to watch\\n158:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to watch for changes in. May be null for 'all'.\\n159:      * @param {function} callbackFn A function to be called when a setting change is\\n160:      * detected. Five arguments can be expected: the setting name, the room ID (may be null),\\n161:      * the level the change happened at, the new value at the given level, and finally the new\\n162:      * value for the setting regardless of level. The callback is responsible for determining\\n163:      * if the change in value is worthwhile enough to react upon.\\n164:      * @returns {string} A reference to the watcher that was employed.\\n165:      */\\n166:     public static watchSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null, callbackFn: CallbackFn): string {\\n167:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n168:         const originalSettingName = settingName;\\n169:         if (!setting) throw new Error(`${settingName} is not a setting`);\\n170: \\n171:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n172:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n173:         }\\n174: \\n175:         const watcherId = `${new Date().getTime()}_${SettingsStore.watcherCount++}_${settingName}_${roomId}`;\\n176: \\n177:         const localizedCallback = (changedInRoomId: string | null, atLevel: SettingLevel, newValAtLevel: any) => {\\n178:             if (!SettingsStore.doesSettingSupportLevel(originalSettingName, atLevel)) {\\n179:                 logger.warn(\\n180:                     `Setting handler notified for an update of an invalid setting level: ` +\\n181:                     `${originalSettingName}@${atLevel} - this likely means a weird setting value ` +\\n182:                     `made it into the level's storage. The notification will be ignored.`,\\n183:                 );\\n184:                 return;\\n185:             }\\n186:             const newValue = SettingsStore.getValue(originalSettingName);\\n187:             const newValueAtLevel = SettingsStore.getValueAt(atLevel, originalSettingName) ?? newValAtLevel;\\n188:             callbackFn(originalSettingName, changedInRoomId, atLevel, newValueAtLevel, newValue);\\n189:         };\\n190: \\n191:         SettingsStore.watchers.set(watcherId, localizedCallback);\\n192:         defaultWatchManager.watchSetting(settingName, roomId, localizedCallback);\\n193: \\n194:         return watcherId;\\n195:     }\\n196: \\n197:     /**\\n198:      * Stops the SettingsStore from watching a setting. This is a no-op if the watcher\\n199:      * provided is not found.\\n200:      * @param {string} watcherReference The watcher reference (received from #watchSetting)\\n201:      * to cancel.\\n202:      */\\n203:     public static unwatchSetting(watcherReference: string) {\\n204:         if (!SettingsStore.watchers.has(watcherReference)) {\\n205:             logger.warn(`Ending non-existent watcher ID ${watcherReference}`);\\n206:             return;\\n207:         }\\n208: \\n209:         defaultWatchManager.unwatchSetting(SettingsStore.watchers.get(watcherReference));\\n210:         SettingsStore.watchers.delete(watcherReference);\\n211:     }\\n212: \\n213:     /**\\n214:      * Sets up a monitor for a setting. This behaves similar to #watchSetting except instead\\n215:      * of making a call to a callback, it forwards all changes to the dispatcher. Callers can\\n216:      * expect to listen for the 'setting_updated' action with an object containing settingName,\\n217:      * roomId, level, newValueAtLevel, and newValue.\\n218:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to monitor.\\n219:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to monitor for changes in. Use null for all rooms.\\n220:      */\\n221:     public static monitorSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null) {\\n222:         roomId = roomId || null; // the thing wants null specifically to work, so appease it.\\n223: \\n224:         if (!this.monitors.has(settingName)) this.monitors.set(settingName, new Map());\\n225: \\n226:         const registerWatcher = () => {\\n227:             this.monitors.get(settingName).set(roomId, SettingsStore.watchSetting(\\n228:                 settingName, roomId, (settingName, inRoomId, level, newValueAtLevel, newValue) => {\\n229:                     dis.dispatch<SettingUpdatedPayload>({\\n230:                         action: Action.SettingUpdated,\\n231:                         settingName,\\n232:                         roomId: inRoomId,\\n233:                         level,\\n234:                         newValueAtLevel,\\n235:                         newValue,\\n236:                     });\\n237:                 },\\n238:             ));\\n239:         };\\n240: \\n241:         const rooms = Array.from(this.monitors.get(settingName).keys());\\n242:         const hasRoom = rooms.find((r) => r === roomId || r === null);\\n243:         if (!hasRoom) {\\n244:             registerWatcher();\\n245:         } else {\\n246:             if (roomId === null) {\\n247:                 // Unregister all existing watchers and register the new one\\n248:                 rooms.forEach(roomId => {\\n249:                     SettingsStore.unwatchSetting(this.monitors.get(settingName).get(roomId));\\n250:                 });\\n251:                 this.monitors.get(settingName).clear();\\n252:                 registerWatcher();\\n253:             } // else a watcher is already registered for the room, so don't bother registering it again\\n254:         }\\n255:     }\\n256: \\n257:     /**\\n258:      * Gets the translated display name for a given setting\\n259:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\\n260:      * @param {SettingLevel} atLevel\\n261:      * The level to get the display name for; Defaults to 'default'.\\n262:      * @return {String} The display name for the setting, or null if not found.\\n263:      */\\n264:     public static getDisplayName(settingName: string, atLevel = SettingLevel.DEFAULT) {\\n265:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName] || !SETTINGS[settingName].displayName) return null;\\n266: \\n267:         let displayName = SETTINGS[settingName].displayName;\\n268:         if (displayName instanceof Object) {\\n269:             if (displayName[atLevel]) displayName = displayName[atLevel];\\n270:             else displayName = displayName[\\\"default\\\"];\\n271:         }\\n272: \\n273:         return _t(displayName as string);\\n274:     }\\n275: \\n276:     /**\\n277:      * Gets the translated description for a given setting\\n278:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\\n279:      * @return {String} The description for the setting, or null if not found.\\n280:      */\\n281:     public static getDescription(settingName: string): string | ReactNode {\\n282:         const description = SETTINGS[settingName]?.description;\\n283:         if (!description) return null;\\n284:         if (typeof description !== 'string') return description();\\n285:         return _t(description);\\n286:     }\\n287: \\n288:     /**\\n289:      * Determines if a setting is also a feature.\\n290:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\\n291:      * @return {boolean} True if the setting is a feature.\\n292:      */\\n293:     public static isFeature(settingName: string) {\\n294:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) return false;\\n295:         return SETTINGS[settingName].isFeature;\\n296:     }\\n297: \\n298:     public static getBetaInfo(settingName: string): ISetting[\\\"betaInfo\\\"] {\\n299:         // consider a beta disabled if the config is explicitly set to false, in which case treat as normal Labs flag\\n300:         if (SettingsStore.isFeature(settingName)\\n301:             && SettingsStore.getValueAt(SettingLevel.CONFIG, settingName, null, true, true) !== false\\n302:         ) {\\n303:             return SETTINGS[settingName]?.betaInfo;\\n304:         }\\n305:     }\\n306: \\n307:     public static getLabGroup(settingName: string): LabGroup {\\n308:         if (SettingsStore.isFeature(settingName)) {\\n309:             return (<IFeature>SETTINGS[settingName]).labsGroup;\\n310:         }\\n311:     }\\n312: \\n313:     /**\\n314:      * Determines if a setting is enabled.\\n315:      * If a setting is disabled then it should be hidden from the user.\\n316:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\\n317:      * @return {boolean} True if the setting is enabled.\\n318:      */\\n319:     public static isEnabled(settingName: string): boolean {\\n320:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) return false;\\n321:         return SETTINGS[settingName].controller ? !SETTINGS[settingName].controller.settingDisabled : true;\\n322:     }\\n323: \\n324:     /**\\n325:      * Gets the value of a setting. The room ID is optional if the setting is not to\\n326:      * be applied to any particular room, otherwise it should be supplied.\\n327:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read the value of.\\n328:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\\n329:      * @param {boolean} excludeDefault True to disable using the default value.\\n330:      * @return {*} The value, or null if not found\\n331:      */\\n332:     public static getValue<T = any>(settingName: string, roomId: string = null, excludeDefault = false): T {\\n333:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n334:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\\n335:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n336:         }\\n337: \\n338:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n339:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\\n340: \\n341:         return SettingsStore.getValueAt(levelOrder[0], settingName, roomId, false, excludeDefault);\\n342:     }\\n343: \\n344:     /**\\n345:      * Gets a setting's value at a particular level, ignoring all levels that are more specific.\\n346:      * @param {SettingLevel|\\\"config\\\"|\\\"default\\\"} level The\\n347:      * level to look at.\\n348:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read.\\n349:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\\n350:      * @param {boolean} explicit If true, this method will not consider other levels, just the one\\n351:      * provided. Defaults to false.\\n352:      * @param {boolean} excludeDefault True to disable using the default value.\\n353:      * @return {*} The value, or null if not found.\\n354:      */\\n355:     public static getValueAt(\\n356:         level: SettingLevel,\\n357:         settingName: string,\\n358:         roomId: string = null,\\n359:         explicit = false,\\n360:         excludeDefault = false,\\n361:     ): any {\\n362:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n363:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n364:         if (!setting) {\\n365:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n366:         }\\n367: \\n368:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\\n369:         if (!levelOrder.includes(SettingLevel.DEFAULT)) levelOrder.push(SettingLevel.DEFAULT); // always include default\\n370: \\n371:         const minIndex = levelOrder.indexOf(level);\\n372:         if (minIndex === -1) throw new Error(`Level \\\"${level}\\\" for setting \\\"${settingName}\\\" is not prioritized`);\\n373: \\n374:         const handlers = SettingsStore.getHandlers(settingName);\\n375: \\n376:         // Check if we need to invert the setting at all. Do this after we get the setting\\n377:         // handlers though, otherwise we'll fail to read the value.\\n378:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n379:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - legacy setting`);\\n380:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n381:         }\\n382: \\n383:         if (explicit) {\\n384:             const handler = handlers[level];\\n385:             if (!handler) {\\n386:                 return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, null, null);\\n387:             }\\n388:             const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\\n389:             return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, value, level);\\n390:         }\\n391: \\n392:         for (let i = minIndex; i < levelOrder.length; i++) {\\n393:             const handler = handlers[levelOrder[i]];\\n394:             if (!handler) continue;\\n395:             if (excludeDefault && levelOrder[i] === \\\"default\\\") continue;\\n396: \\n397:             const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\\n398:             if (value === null || value === undefined) continue;\\n399:             return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, value, levelOrder[i]);\\n400:         }\\n401: \\n402:         return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, null, null);\\n403:     }\\n404: \\n405:     /**\\n406:      * Gets the default value of a setting.\\n407:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read the value of.\\n408:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\\n409:      * @return {*} The default value\\n410:      */\\n411:     public static getDefaultValue(settingName: string): any {\\n412:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n413:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\\n414:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n415:         }\\n416: \\n417:         return SETTINGS[settingName].default;\\n418:     }\\n419: \\n420:     private static getFinalValue(\\n421:         setting: ISetting,\\n422:         level: SettingLevel,\\n423:         roomId: string,\\n424:         calculatedValue: any,\\n425:         calculatedAtLevel: SettingLevel,\\n426:     ): any {\\n427:         let resultingValue = calculatedValue;\\n428: \\n429:         if (setting.controller) {\\n430:             const actualValue = setting.controller.getValueOverride(level, roomId, calculatedValue, calculatedAtLevel);\\n431:             if (actualValue !== undefined && actualValue !== null) resultingValue = actualValue;\\n432:         }\\n433: \\n434:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) resultingValue = !resultingValue;\\n435:         return resultingValue;\\n436:     }\\n437: \\n438:     /* eslint-disable valid-jsdoc */ //https://github.com/eslint/eslint/issues/7307\\n439:     /**\\n440:      * Sets the value for a setting. The room ID is optional if the setting is not being\\n441:      * set for a particular room, otherwise it should be supplied. The value may be null\\n442:      * to indicate that the level should no longer have an override.\\n443:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to change.\\n444:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to change the value in, may be null.\\n445:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\\n446:      * to change the value at.\\n447:      * @param {*} value The new value of the setting, may be null.\\n448:      * @return {Promise} Resolves when the setting has been changed.\\n449:      */\\n450: \\n451:     /* eslint-enable valid-jsdoc */\\n452:     public static async setValue(\\n453:         settingName: string,\\n454:         roomId: string | null,\\n455:         level: SettingLevel,\\n456:         value: any,\\n457:     ): Promise<void> {\\n458:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n459:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n460:         if (!setting) {\\n461:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n462:         }\\n463: \\n464:         const handler = SettingsStore.getHandler(settingName, level);\\n465:         if (!handler) {\\n466:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting \\\" + settingName + \\\" does not have a handler for \\\" + level);\\n467:         }\\n468: \\n469:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\\n470:             // Note: We can't do this when the `level` is \\\"default\\\", however we also\\n471:             // know that the user can't possible change the default value through this\\n472:             // function so we don't bother checking it.\\n473:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - legacy setting`);\\n474:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\\n475:             value = !value;\\n476:         }\\n477: \\n478:         if (!handler.canSetValue(settingName, roomId)) {\\n479:             throw new Error(\\\"User cannot set \\\" + settingName + \\\" at \\\" + level + \\\" in \\\" + roomId);\\n480:         }\\n481: \\n482:         if (setting.controller && !(await setting.controller.beforeChange(level, roomId, value))) {\\n483:             return; // controller says no\\n484:         }\\n485: \\n486:         await handler.setValue(settingName, roomId, value);\\n487: \\n488:         setting.controller?.onChange(level, roomId, value);\\n489:     }\\n490: \\n491:     /**\\n492:      * Determines if the current user is permitted to set the given setting at the given\\n493:      * level for a particular room. The room ID is optional if the setting is not being\\n494:      * set for a particular room, otherwise it should be supplied.\\n495:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to check.\\n496:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to check in, may be null.\\n497:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level to\\n498:      * check at.\\n499:      * @return {boolean} True if the user may set the setting, false otherwise.\\n500:      */\\n501:     public static canSetValue(settingName: string, roomId: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n502:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n503:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\\n504:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n505:         }\\n506: \\n507:         if (!SettingsStore.isEnabled(settingName)) {\\n508:             return false;\\n509:         }\\n510: \\n511:         // When non-beta features are specified in the config.json, we force them as enabled or disabled.\\n512:         if (SettingsStore.isFeature(settingName) && !SETTINGS[settingName]?.betaInfo) {\\n513:             const configVal = SettingsStore.getValueAt(SettingLevel.CONFIG, settingName, roomId, true, true);\\n514:             if (configVal === true || configVal === false) return false;\\n515:         }\\n516: \\n517:         const handler = SettingsStore.getHandler(settingName, level);\\n518:         if (!handler) return false;\\n519:         return handler.canSetValue(settingName, roomId);\\n520:     }\\n521: \\n522:     /**\\n523:      * Determines if the given level is supported on this device.\\n524:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\\n525:      * to check the feasibility of.\\n526:      * @return {boolean} True if the level is supported, false otherwise.\\n527:      */\\n528:     public static isLevelSupported(level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n529:         if (!LEVEL_HANDLERS[level]) return false;\\n530:         return LEVEL_HANDLERS[level].isSupported();\\n531:     }\\n532: \\n533:     /**\\n534:      * Determines if a setting supports a particular level.\\n535:      * @param settingName The setting name.\\n536:      * @param level The level.\\n537:      * @returns True if supported, false otherwise. Note that this will not check to see if\\n538:      * the level itself can be supported by the runtime (ie: you will need to call #isLevelSupported()\\n539:      * on your own).\\n540:      */\\n541:     public static doesSettingSupportLevel(settingName: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\\n542:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n543:         if (!setting) {\\n544:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n545:         }\\n546: \\n547:         return level === SettingLevel.DEFAULT || (setting.supportedLevels.includes(level));\\n548:     }\\n549: \\n550:     /**\\n551:      * Determines the first supported level out of all the levels that can be used for a\\n552:      * specific setting.\\n553:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name.\\n554:      * @return {SettingLevel}\\n555:      */\\n556:     public static firstSupportedLevel(settingName: string): SettingLevel {\\n557:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\\n558:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\\n559:         if (!setting) {\\n560:             throw new Error(\\\"Setting '\\\" + settingName + \\\"' does not appear to be a setting.\\\");\\n561:         }\\n562: \\n563:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\\n564:         if (!levelOrder.includes(SettingLevel.DEFAULT)) levelOrder.push(SettingLevel.DEFAULT); // always include default\\n565: \\n566:         const handlers = SettingsStore.getHandlers(settingName);\\n567: \\n568:         for (const level of levelOrder) {\\n569:             const handler = handlers[level];\\n570:             if (!handler) continue;\\n571:             return level;\\n572:         }\\n573:         return null;\\n574:     }\\n575: \\n576:     /**\\n577:      * Runs or queues any setting migrations needed.\\n578:      */\\n579:     public static runMigrations(): void {\\n580:         // Dev notes: to add your migration, just add a new `migrateMyFeature` function, call it, and\\n581:         // add a comment to note when it can be removed.\\n582: \\n583:         SettingsStore.migrateHiddenReadReceipts(); // Can be removed after October 2022.\\n584:     }\\n585: \\n586:     private static migrateHiddenReadReceipts(): void {\\n587:         if (MatrixClientPeg.get().isGuest()) return; // not worth it\\n588: \\n589:         // We wait for the first sync to ensure that the user's existing account data has loaded, as otherwise\\n590:         // getValue() for an account-level setting like sendReadReceipts will return `null`.\\n591:         const disRef = dispatcher.register((payload: ActionPayload) => {\\n592:             if (payload.action === \\\"MatrixActions.sync\\\") {\\n593:                 dispatcher.unregister(disRef);\\n594: \\n595:                 const rrVal = SettingsStore.getValue(\\\"sendReadReceipts\\\", null, true);\\n596:                 if (typeof rrVal !== \\\"boolean\\\") {\\n597:                     // new setting isn't set - see if the labs flag was. We have to manually reach into the\\n598:                     // handler for this because it isn't a setting anymore (`getValue` will yell at us).\\n599:                     const handler = LEVEL_HANDLERS[SettingLevel.DEVICE] as DeviceSettingsHandler;\\n600:                     const labsVal = handler.readFeature(\\\"feature_hidden_read_receipts\\\");\\n601:                     if (typeof labsVal === \\\"boolean\\\") {\\n602:                         // Inverse of labs flag because negative->positive language switch in setting name\\n603:                         const newVal = !labsVal;\\n604:                         console.log(`Setting sendReadReceipts to ${newVal} because of previously-set labs flag`);\\n605: \\n606:                         // noinspection JSIgnoredPromiseFromCall\\n607:                         SettingsStore.setValue(\\\"sendReadReceipts\\\", null, SettingLevel.ACCOUNT, newVal);\\n608:                     }\\n609:                 }\\n610:             }\\n611:         });\\n612:     }\\n613: \\n614:     /**\\n615:      * Debugging function for reading explicit setting values without going through the\\n616:      * complicated/biased functions in the SettingsStore. This will print information to\\n617:      * the console for analysis. Not intended to be used within the application.\\n618:      * @param {string} realSettingName The setting name to try and read.\\n619:      * @param {string} roomId Optional room ID to test the setting in.\\n620:      */\\n621:     public static debugSetting(realSettingName: string, roomId: string) {\\n622:         logger.log(`--- DEBUG ${realSettingName}`);\\n623: \\n624:         // Note: we intentionally use JSON.stringify here to avoid the console masking the\\n625:         // problem if there's a type representation issue. Also, this way it is guaranteed\\n626:         // to show up in a rageshake if required.\\n627: \\n628:         const def = SETTINGS[realSettingName];\\n629:         logger.log(`--- definition: ${def ? JSON.stringify(def) : '<NOT_FOUND>'}`);\\n630:         logger.log(`--- default level order: ${JSON.stringify(LEVEL_ORDER)}`);\\n631:         logger.log(`--- registered handlers: ${JSON.stringify(Object.keys(LEVEL_HANDLERS))}`);\\n632: \\n633:         const doChecks = (settingName) => {\\n634:             for (const handlerName of Object.keys(LEVEL_HANDLERS)) {\\n635:                 const handler = LEVEL_HANDLERS[handlerName];\\n636: \\n637:                 try {\\n638:                     const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\\n639:                     logger.log(`---     ${handlerName}@${roomId || '<no_room>'} = ${JSON.stringify(value)}`);\\n640:                 } catch (e) {\\n641:                     logger.log(`---     ${handler}@${roomId || '<no_room>'} THREW ERROR: ${e.message}`);\\n642:                     logger.error(e);\\n643:                 }\\n644: \\n645:                 if (roomId) {\\n646:                     try {\\n647:                         const value = handler.getValue(settingName, null);\\n648:                         logger.log(`---     ${handlerName}@<no_room> = ${JSON.stringify(value)}`);\\n649:                     } catch (e) {\\n650:                         logger.log(`---     ${handler}@<no_room> THREW ERROR: ${e.message}`);\\n651:                         logger.error(e);\\n652:                     }\\n653:                 }\\n654:             }\\n655: \\n656:             logger.log(`--- calculating as returned by SettingsStore`);\\n657:             logger.log(`--- these might not match if the setting uses a controller - be warned!`);\\n658: \\n659:             try {\\n660:                 const value = SettingsStore.getValue(settingName, roomId);\\n661:                 logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#generic@${roomId || '<no_room>'}  = ${JSON.stringify(value)}`);\\n662:             } catch (e) {\\n663:                 logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#generic@${roomId || '<no_room>'} THREW ERROR: ${e.message}`);\\n664:                 logger.error(e);\\n665:             }\\n666: \\n667:             if (roomId) {\\n668:                 try {\\n669:                     const value = SettingsStore.getValue(settingName, null);\\n670:                     logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#generic@<no_room>  = ${JSON.stringify(value)}`);\\n671:                 } catch (e) {\\n672:                     logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#generic@$<no_room> THREW ERROR: ${e.message}`);\\n673:                     logger.error(e);\\n674:                 }\\n675:             }\\n676: \\n677:             for (const level of LEVEL_ORDER) {\\n678:                 try {\\n679:                     const value = SettingsStore.getValueAt(level, settingName, roomId);\\n680:                     logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#${level}@${roomId || '<no_room>'} = ${JSON.stringify(value)}`);\\n681:                 } catch (e) {\\n682:                     logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#${level}@${roomId || '<no_room>'} THREW ERROR: ${e.message}`);\\n683:                     logger.error(e);\\n684:                 }\\n685: \\n686:                 if (roomId) {\\n687:                     try {\\n688:                         const value = SettingsStore.getValueAt(level, settingName, null);\\n689:                         logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#${level}@<no_room> = ${JSON.stringify(value)}`);\\n690:                     } catch (e) {\\n691:                         logger.log(`---     SettingsStore#${level}@$<no_room> THREW ERROR: ${e.message}`);\\n692:                         logger.error(e);\\n693:                     }\\n694:                 }\\n695:             }\\n696:         };\\n697: \\n698:         doChecks(realSettingName);\\n699: \\n700:         if (def.invertedSettingName) {\\n701:             logger.log(`--- TESTING INVERTED SETTING NAME`);\\n702:             logger.log(`--- inverted: ${def.invertedSettingName}`);\\n703:             doChecks(def.invertedSettingName);\\n704:         }\\n705: \\n706:         logger.log(`--- END DEBUG`);\\n707:     }\\n708: \\n709:     private static getHandler(settingName: string, level: SettingLevel): SettingsHandler {\\n710:         const handlers = SettingsStore.getHandlers(settingName);\\n711:         if (!handlers[level]) return null;\\n712:         return handlers[level];\\n713:     }\\n714: \\n715:     private static getHandlers(settingName: string): IHandlerMap {\\n716:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) return {};\\n717: \\n718:         const handlers = {};\\n719:         for (const level of SETTINGS[settingName].supportedLevels) {\\n720:             if (!LEVEL_HANDLERS[level]) throw new Error(\\\"Unexpected level \\\" + level);\\n721:             if (SettingsStore.isLevelSupported(level)) handlers[level] = LEVEL_HANDLERS[level];\\n722:         }\\n723: \\n724:         // Always support 'default'\\n725:         if (!handlers['default']) handlers['default'] = LEVEL_HANDLERS['default'];\\n726: \\n727:         return handlers;\\n728:     }\\n729: }\\n730: \\n731: // For debugging purposes\\n732: window.mxSettingsStore = SettingsStore;\\n733: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/element-web--8ebdcab7@ad5bbea8a042bbf168fec65b99f459e75c3f2354/-/blob/src/settings/SettingsStore.ts\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: /*\n2: Copyright 2017 Travis Ralston\n3: Copyright 2019, 2020 The Matrix.org Foundation C.I.C.\n4: \n5: Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n6: you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n7: You may obtain a copy of the License at\n8: \n9:     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n10: \n11: Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n12: distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n13: WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n14: See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n15: limitations under the License.\n16: */\n17: \n18: import { logger } from \"matrix-js-sdk/src/logger\";\n19: import { ReactNode } from \"react\";\n20: \n21: import DeviceSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/DeviceSettingsHandler\";\n22: import RoomDeviceSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/RoomDeviceSettingsHandler\";\n23: import DefaultSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/DefaultSettingsHandler\";\n24: import RoomAccountSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/RoomAccountSettingsHandler\";\n25: import AccountSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/AccountSettingsHandler\";\n26: import RoomSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/RoomSettingsHandler\";\n27: import ConfigSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/ConfigSettingsHandler\";\n28: import { _t } from '../languageHandler';\n29: import dis from '../dispatcher/dispatcher';\n30: import { IFeature, ISetting, LabGroup, SETTINGS } from \"./Settings\";\n31: import LocalEchoWrapper from \"./handlers/LocalEchoWrapper\";\n32: import { CallbackFn as WatchCallbackFn, WatchManager } from \"./WatchManager\";\n33: import { SettingLevel } from \"./SettingLevel\";\n34: import SettingsHandler from \"./handlers/SettingsHandler\";\n35: import { SettingUpdatedPayload } from \"../dispatcher/payloads/SettingUpdatedPayload\";\n36: import { Action } from \"../dispatcher/actions\";\n37: import PlatformSettingsHandler from \"./handlers/PlatformSettingsHandler\";\n38: import dispatcher from \"../dispatcher/dispatcher\";\n39: import { ActionPayload } from \"../dispatcher/payloads\";\n40: import { MatrixClientPeg } from \"../MatrixClientPeg\";\n41: \n42: const defaultWatchManager = new WatchManager();\n43: \n44: // Convert the settings to easier to manage objects for the handlers\n45: const defaultSettings = {};\n46: const invertedDefaultSettings = {};\n47: const featureNames = [];\n48: for (const key of Object.keys(SETTINGS)) {\n49:     defaultSettings[key] = SETTINGS[key].default;\n50:     if (SETTINGS[key].isFeature) featureNames.push(key);\n51:     if (SETTINGS[key].invertedSettingName) {\n52:         // Invert now so that the rest of the system will invert it back\n53:         // to what was intended.\n54:         invertedDefaultSettings[SETTINGS[key].invertedSettingName] = !SETTINGS[key].default;\n55:     }\n56: }\n57: \n58: // Only wrap the handlers with async setters in a local echo wrapper\n59: const LEVEL_HANDLERS = {\n60:     [SettingLevel.DEVICE]: new DeviceSettingsHandler(featureNames, defaultWatchManager),\n61:     [SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE]: new RoomDeviceSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager),\n62:     [SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT]: new LocalEchoWrapper(\n63:         new RoomAccountSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager),\n64:         SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT,\n65:     ),\n66:     [SettingLevel.ACCOUNT]: new LocalEchoWrapper(new AccountSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager), SettingLevel.ACCOUNT),\n67:     [SettingLevel.ROOM]: new LocalEchoWrapper(new RoomSettingsHandler(defaultWatchManager), SettingLevel.ROOM),\n68:     [SettingLevel.PLATFORM]: new LocalEchoWrapper(new PlatformSettingsHandler(), SettingLevel.PLATFORM),\n69:     [SettingLevel.CONFIG]: new ConfigSettingsHandler(featureNames),\n70:     [SettingLevel.DEFAULT]: new DefaultSettingsHandler(defaultSettings, invertedDefaultSettings),\n71: };\n72: \n73: export const LEVEL_ORDER = [\n74:     SettingLevel.DEVICE,\n75:     SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE,\n76:     SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT,\n77:     SettingLevel.ACCOUNT,\n78:     SettingLevel.ROOM,\n79:     SettingLevel.CONFIG,\n80:     SettingLevel.DEFAULT,\n81: ];\n82: \n83: function getLevelOrder(setting: ISetting): SettingLevel[] {\n84:     // Settings which support only a single setting level are inherently ordered\n85:     if (setting.supportedLevelsAreOrdered || setting.supportedLevels.length === 1) {\n86:         // return a copy to prevent callers from modifying the array\n87:         return [...setting.supportedLevels];\n88:     }\n89:     return LEVEL_ORDER;\n90: }\n91: \n92: export type CallbackFn = (\n93:     settingName: string,\n94:     roomId: string,\n95:     atLevel: SettingLevel,\n96:     newValAtLevel: any,\n97:     newVal: any,\n98: ) => void;\n99: \n100: interface IHandlerMap {\n101:     // @ts-ignore - TS wants this to be a string key but we know better\n102:     [level: SettingLevel]: SettingsHandler;\n103: }\n104: \n105: /**\n106:  * Controls and manages application settings by providing varying levels at which the\n107:  * setting value may be specified. The levels are then used to determine what the setting\n108:  * value should be given a set of circumstances. The levels, in priority order, are:\n109:  * - SettingLevel.DEVICE         - Values are determined by the current device\n110:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_DEVICE    - Values are determined by the current device for a particular room\n111:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM_ACCOUNT   - Values are determined by the current account for a particular room\n112:  * - SettingLevel.ACCOUNT        - Values are determined by the current account\n113:  * - SettingLevel.ROOM           - Values are determined by a particular room (by the room admins)\n114:  * - SettingLevel.CONFIG         - Values are determined by the config.json\n115:  * - SettingLevel.DEFAULT        - Values are determined by the hardcoded defaults\n116:  *\n117:  * Each level has a different method to storing the setting value. For implementation\n118:  * specific details, please see the handlers. The \"config\" and \"default\" levels are\n119:  * both always supported on all platforms. All other settings should be guarded by\n120:  * isLevelSupported() prior to attempting to set the value.\n121:  *\n122:  * Settings can also represent features. Features are significant portions of the\n123:  * application that warrant a dedicated setting to toggle them on or off. Features are\n124:  * special-cased to ensure that their values respect the configuration (for example, a\n125:  * feature may be reported as disabled even though a user has specifically requested it\n126:  * be enabled).\n127:  */\n128: export default class SettingsStore {\n129:     // We support watching settings for changes, and do this by tracking which callbacks have\n130:     // been given to us. We end up returning the callbackRef to the caller so they can unsubscribe\n131:     // at a later point.\n132:     //\n133:     // We also maintain a list of monitors which are special watchers: they cause dispatches\n134:     // when the setting changes. We track which rooms we're monitoring though to ensure we\n135:     // don't duplicate updates on the bus.\n136:     private static watchers = new Map<string, WatchCallbackFn>();\n137:     private static monitors = new Map<string, Map<string, string>>(); // { settingName => { roomId => callbackRef } }\n138: \n139:     // Counter used for generation of watcher IDs\n140:     private static watcherCount = 1;\n141: \n142:     /**\n143:      * Gets all the feature-style setting names.\n144:      * @returns {string[]} The names of the feature settings.\n145:      */\n146:     public static getFeatureSettingNames(): string[] {\n147:         return Object.keys(SETTINGS).filter(n => SettingsStore.isFeature(n));\n148:     }\n149: \n150:     /**\n151:      * Watches for changes in a particular setting. This is done without any local echo\n152:      * wrapping and fires whenever a change is detected in a setting's value, at any level.\n153:      * Watching is intended to be used in scenarios where the app needs to react to changes\n154:      * made by other devices. It is otherwise expected that callers will be able to use the\n155:      * Controller system or track their own changes to settings. Callers should retain the\n156:      * returned reference to later unsubscribe from updates.\n157:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to watch\n158:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to watch for changes in. May be null for 'all'.\n159:      * @param {function} callbackFn A function to be called when a setting change is\n160:      * detected. Five arguments can be expected: the setting name, the room ID (may be null),\n161:      * the level the change happened at, the new value at the given level, and finally the new\n162:      * value for the setting regardless of level. The callback is responsible for determining\n163:      * if the change in value is worthwhile enough to react upon.\n164:      * @returns {string} A reference to the watcher that was employed.\n165:      */\n166:     public static watchSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null, callbackFn: CallbackFn): string {\n167:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\n168:         const originalSettingName = settingName;\n169:         if (!setting) throw new Error(`${settingName} is not a setting`);\n170: \n171:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\n172:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\n173:         }\n174: \n175:         const watcherId = `${new Date().getTime()}_${SettingsStore.watcherCount++}_${settingName}_${roomId}`;\n176: \n177:         const localizedCallback = (changedInRoomId: string | null, atLevel: SettingLevel, newValAtLevel: any) => {\n178:             if (!SettingsStore.doesSettingSupportLevel(originalSettingName, atLevel)) {\n179:                 logger.warn(\n180:                     `Setting handler notified for an update of an invalid setting level: ` +\n181:                     `${originalSettingName}@${atLevel} - this likely means a weird setting value ` +\n182:                     `made it into the level's storage. The notification will be ignored.`,\n183:                 );\n184:                 return;\n185:             }\n186:             const newValue = SettingsStore.getValue(originalSettingName);\n187:             const newValueAtLevel = SettingsStore.getValueAt(atLevel, originalSettingName) ?? newValAtLevel;\n188:             callbackFn(originalSettingName, changedInRoomId, atLevel, newValueAtLevel, newValue);\n189:         };\n190: \n191:         SettingsStore.watchers.set(watcherId, localizedCallback);\n192:         defaultWatchManager.watchSetting(settingName, roomId, localizedCallback);\n193: \n194:         return watcherId;\n195:     }\n196: \n197:     /**\n198:      * Stops the SettingsStore from watching a setting. This is a no-op if the watcher\n199:      * provided is not found.\n200:      * @param {string} watcherReference The watcher reference (received from #watchSetting)\n201:      * to cancel.\n202:      */\n203:     public static unwatchSetting(watcherReference: string) {\n204:         if (!SettingsStore.watchers.has(watcherReference)) {\n205:             logger.warn(`Ending non-existent watcher ID ${watcherReference}`);\n206:             return;\n207:         }\n208: \n209:         defaultWatchManager.unwatchSetting(SettingsStore.watchers.get(watcherReference));\n210:         SettingsStore.watchers.delete(watcherReference);\n211:     }\n212: \n213:     /**\n214:      * Sets up a monitor for a setting. This behaves similar to #watchSetting except instead\n215:      * of making a call to a callback, it forwards all changes to the dispatcher. Callers can\n216:      * expect to listen for the 'setting_updated' action with an object containing settingName,\n217:      * roomId, level, newValueAtLevel, and newValue.\n218:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name to monitor.\n219:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to monitor for changes in. Use null for all rooms.\n220:      */\n221:     public static monitorSetting(settingName: string, roomId: string | null) {\n222:         roomId = roomId || null; // the thing wants null specifically to work, so appease it.\n223: \n224:         if (!this.monitors.has(settingName)) this.monitors.set(settingName, new Map());\n225: \n226:         const registerWatcher = () => {\n227:             this.monitors.get(settingName).set(roomId, SettingsStore.watchSetting(\n228:                 settingName, roomId, (settingName, inRoomId, level, newValueAtLevel, newValue) => {\n229:                     dis.dispatch<SettingUpdatedPayload>({\n230:                         action: Action.SettingUpdated,\n231:                         settingName,\n232:                         roomId: inRoomId,\n233:                         level,\n234:                         newValueAtLevel,\n235:                         newValue,\n236:                     });\n237:                 },\n238:             ));\n239:         };\n240: \n241:         const rooms = Array.from(this.monitors.get(settingName).keys());\n242:         const hasRoom = rooms.find((r) => r === roomId || r === null);\n243:         if (!hasRoom) {\n244:             registerWatcher();\n245:         } else {\n246:             if (roomId === null) {\n247:                 // Unregister all existing watchers and register the new one\n248:                 rooms.forEach(roomId => {\n249:                     SettingsStore.unwatchSetting(this.monitors.get(settingName).get(roomId));\n250:                 });\n251:                 this.monitors.get(settingName).clear();\n252:                 registerWatcher();\n253:             } // else a watcher is already registered for the room, so don't bother registering it again\n254:         }\n255:     }\n256: \n257:     /**\n258:      * Gets the translated display name for a given setting\n259:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\n260:      * @param {SettingLevel} atLevel\n261:      * The level to get the display name for; Defaults to 'default'.\n262:      * @return {String} The display name for the setting, or null if not found.\n263:      */\n264:     public static getDisplayName(settingName: string, atLevel = SettingLevel.DEFAULT) {\n265:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName] || !SETTINGS[settingName].displayName) return null;\n266: \n267:         let displayName = SETTINGS[settingName].displayName;\n268:         if (displayName instanceof Object) {\n269:             if (displayName[atLevel]) displayName = displayName[atLevel];\n270:             else displayName = displayName[\"default\"];\n271:         }\n272: \n273:         return _t(displayName as string);\n274:     }\n275: \n276:     /**\n277:      * Gets the translated description for a given setting\n278:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\n279:      * @return {String} The description for the setting, or null if not found.\n280:      */\n281:     public static getDescription(settingName: string): string | ReactNode {\n282:         const description = SETTINGS[settingName]?.description;\n283:         if (!description) return null;\n284:         if (typeof description !== 'string') return description();\n285:         return _t(description);\n286:     }\n287: \n288:     /**\n289:      * Determines if a setting is also a feature.\n290:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\n291:      * @return {boolean} True if the setting is a feature.\n292:      */\n293:     public static isFeature(settingName: string) {\n294:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) return false;\n295:         return SETTINGS[settingName].isFeature;\n296:     }\n297: \n298:     public static getBetaInfo(settingName: string): ISetting[\"betaInfo\"] {\n299:         // consider a beta disabled if the config is explicitly set to false, in which case treat as normal Labs flag\n300:         if (SettingsStore.isFeature(settingName)\n301:             && SettingsStore.getValueAt(SettingLevel.CONFIG, settingName, null, true, true) !== false\n302:         ) {\n303:             return SETTINGS[settingName]?.betaInfo;\n304:         }\n305:     }\n306: \n307:     public static getLabGroup(settingName: string): LabGroup {\n308:         if (SettingsStore.isFeature(settingName)) {\n309:             return (<IFeature>SETTINGS[settingName]).labsGroup;\n310:         }\n311:     }\n312: \n313:     /**\n314:      * Determines if a setting is enabled.\n315:      * If a setting is disabled then it should be hidden from the user.\n316:      * @param {string} settingName The setting to look up.\n317:      * @return {boolean} True if the setting is enabled.\n318:      */\n319:     public static isEnabled(settingName: string): boolean {\n320:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) return false;\n321:         return SETTINGS[settingName].controller ? !SETTINGS[settingName].controller.settingDisabled : true;\n322:     }\n323: \n324:     /**\n325:      * Gets the value of a setting. The room ID is optional if the setting is not to\n326:      * be applied to any particular room, otherwise it should be supplied.\n327:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read the value of.\n328:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\n329:      * @param {boolean} excludeDefault True to disable using the default value.\n330:      * @return {*} The value, or null if not found\n331:      */\n332:     public static getValue<T = any>(settingName: string, roomId: string = null, excludeDefault = false): T {\n333:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\n334:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\n335:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n336:         }\n337: \n338:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\n339:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\n340: \n341:         return SettingsStore.getValueAt(levelOrder[0], settingName, roomId, false, excludeDefault);\n342:     }\n343: \n344:     /**\n345:      * Gets a setting's value at a particular level, ignoring all levels that are more specific.\n346:      * @param {SettingLevel|\"config\"|\"default\"} level The\n347:      * level to look at.\n348:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read.\n349:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\n350:      * @param {boolean} explicit If true, this method will not consider other levels, just the one\n351:      * provided. Defaults to false.\n352:      * @param {boolean} excludeDefault True to disable using the default value.\n353:      * @return {*} The value, or null if not found.\n354:      */\n355:     public static getValueAt(\n356:         level: SettingLevel,\n357:         settingName: string,\n358:         roomId: string = null,\n359:         explicit = false,\n360:         excludeDefault = false,\n361:     ): any {\n362:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\n363:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\n364:         if (!setting) {\n365:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n366:         }\n367: \n368:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\n369:         if (!levelOrder.includes(SettingLevel.DEFAULT)) levelOrder.push(SettingLevel.DEFAULT); // always include default\n370: \n371:         const minIndex = levelOrder.indexOf(level);\n372:         if (minIndex === -1) throw new Error(`Level \"${level}\" for setting \"${settingName}\" is not prioritized`);\n373: \n374:         const handlers = SettingsStore.getHandlers(settingName);\n375: \n376:         // Check if we need to invert the setting at all. Do this after we get the setting\n377:         // handlers though, otherwise we'll fail to read the value.\n378:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\n379:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - legacy setting`);\n380:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\n381:         }\n382: \n383:         if (explicit) {\n384:             const handler = handlers[level];\n385:             if (!handler) {\n386:                 return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, null, null);\n387:             }\n388:             const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\n389:             return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, value, level);\n390:         }\n391: \n392:         for (let i = minIndex; i < levelOrder.length; i++) {\n393:             const handler = handlers[levelOrder[i]];\n394:             if (!handler) continue;\n395:             if (excludeDefault && levelOrder[i] === \"default\") continue;\n396: \n397:             const value = handler.getValue(settingName, roomId);\n398:             if (value === null || value === undefined) continue;\n399:             return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, value, levelOrder[i]);\n400:         }\n401: \n402:         return SettingsStore.getFinalValue(setting, level, roomId, null, null);\n403:     }\n404: \n405:     /**\n406:      * Gets the default value of a setting.\n407:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to read the value of.\n408:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to read the setting value in, may be null.\n409:      * @return {*} The default value\n410:      */\n411:     public static getDefaultValue(settingName: string): any {\n412:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\n413:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\n414:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n415:         }\n416: \n417:         return SETTINGS[settingName].default;\n418:     }\n419: \n420:     private static getFinalValue(\n421:         setting: ISetting,\n422:         level: SettingLevel,\n423:         roomId: string,\n424:         calculatedValue: any,\n425:         calculatedAtLevel: SettingLevel,\n426:     ): any {\n427:         let resultingValue = calculatedValue;\n428: \n429:         if (setting.controller) {\n430:             const actualValue = setting.controller.getValueOverride(level, roomId, calculatedValue, calculatedAtLevel);\n431:             if (actualValue !== undefined && actualValue !== null) resultingValue = actualValue;\n432:         }\n433: \n434:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) resultingValue = !resultingValue;\n435:         return resultingValue;\n436:     }\n437: \n438:     /* eslint-disable valid-jsdoc */ //https://github.com/eslint/eslint/issues/7307\n439:     /**\n440:      * Sets the value for a setting. The room ID is optional if the setting is not being\n441:      * set for a particular room, otherwise it should be supplied. The value may be null\n442:      * to indicate that the level should no longer have an override.\n443:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to change.\n444:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to change the value in, may be null.\n445:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\n446:      * to change the value at.\n447:      * @param {*} value The new value of the setting, may be null.\n448:      * @return {Promise} Resolves when the setting has been changed.\n449:      */\n450: \n451:     /* eslint-enable valid-jsdoc */\n452:     public static async setValue(\n453:         settingName: string,\n454:         roomId: string | null,\n455:         level: SettingLevel,\n456:         value: any,\n457:     ): Promise<void> {\n458:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\n459:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\n460:         if (!setting) {\n461:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n462:         }\n463: \n464:         const handler = SettingsStore.getHandler(settingName, level);\n465:         if (!handler) {\n466:             throw new Error(\"Setting \" + settingName + \" does not have a handler for \" + level);\n467:         }\n468: \n469:         if (setting.invertedSettingName) {\n470:             // Note: We can't do this when the `level` is \"default\", however we also\n471:             // know that the user can't possible change the default value through this\n472:             // function so we don't bother checking it.\n473:             //console.warn(`Inverting ${settingName} to be ${setting.invertedSettingName} - legacy setting`);\n474:             settingName = setting.invertedSettingName;\n475:             value = !value;\n476:         }\n477: \n478:         if (!handler.canSetValue(settingName, roomId)) {\n479:             throw new Error(\"User cannot set \" + settingName + \" at \" + level + \" in \" + roomId);\n480:         }\n481: \n482:         if (setting.controller && !(await setting.controller.beforeChange(level, roomId, value))) {\n483:             return; // controller says no\n484:         }\n485: \n486:         await handler.setValue(settingName, roomId, value);\n487: \n488:         setting.controller?.onChange(level, roomId, value);\n489:     }\n490: \n491:     /**\n492:      * Determines if the current user is permitted to set the given setting at the given\n493:      * level for a particular room. The room ID is optional if the setting is not being\n494:      * set for a particular room, otherwise it should be supplied.\n495:      * @param {string} settingName The name of the setting to check.\n496:      * @param {String} roomId The room ID to check in, may be null.\n497:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level to\n498:      * check at.\n499:      * @return {boolean} True if the user may set the setting, false otherwise.\n500:      */\n501:     public static canSetValue(settingName: string, roomId: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\n502:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\n503:         if (!SETTINGS[settingName]) {\n504:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n505:         }\n506: \n507:         if (!SettingsStore.isEnabled(settingName)) {\n508:             return false;\n509:         }\n510: \n511:         // When non-beta features are specified in the config.json, we force them as enabled or disabled.\n512:         if (SettingsStore.isFeature(settingName) && !SETTINGS[settingName]?.betaInfo) {\n513:             const configVal = SettingsStore.getValueAt(SettingLevel.CONFIG, settingName, roomId, true, true);\n514:             if (configVal === true || configVal === false) return false;\n515:         }\n516: \n517:         const handler = SettingsStore.getHandler(settingName, level);\n518:         if (!handler) return false;\n519:         return handler.canSetValue(settingName, roomId);\n520:     }\n521: \n522:     /**\n523:      * Determines if the given level is supported on this device.\n524:      * @param {SettingLevel} level The level\n525:      * to check the feasibility of.\n526:      * @return {boolean} True if the level is supported, false otherwise.\n527:      */\n528:     public static isLevelSupported(level: SettingLevel): boolean {\n529:         if (!LEVEL_HANDLERS[level]) return false;\n530:         return LEVEL_HANDLERS[level].isSupported();\n531:     }\n532: \n533:     /**\n534:      * Determines if a setting supports a particular level.\n535:      * @param settingName The setting name.\n536:      * @param level The level.\n537:      * @returns True if supported, false otherwise. Note that this will not check to see if\n538:      * the level itself can be supported by the runtime (ie: you will need to call #isLevelSupported()\n539:      * on your own).\n540:      */\n541:     public static doesSettingSupportLevel(settingName: string, level: SettingLevel): boolean {\n542:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\n543:         if (!setting) {\n544:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n545:         }\n546: \n547:         return level === SettingLevel.DEFAULT || (setting.supportedLevels.includes(level));\n548:     }\n549: \n550:     /**\n551:      * Determines the first supported level out of all the levels that can be used for a\n552:      * specific setting.\n553:      * @param {string} settingName The setting name.\n554:      * @return {SettingLevel}\n555:      */\n556:     public static firstSupportedLevel(settingName: string): SettingLevel {\n557:         // Verify that the setting is actually a setting\n558:         const setting = SETTINGS[settingName];\n559:         if (!setting) {\n560:             throw new Error(\"Setting '\" + settingName + \"' does not appear to be a setting.\");\n561:         }\n562: \n563:         const levelOrder = getLevelOrder(setting);\n564:         if (!levelOrder.includes(SettingLevel.DEFAULT)) levelOrder.push(SettingLevel.DEFAULT); // always include default\n565: \n566:         const handlers = SettingsStore.getHandlers(settingName);\n567: \n568:         for (const level of levelOrder) {\n569:             const handler = handlers[level];\n570:             if (!handler) continue;\n571:             return level;\n572:         }\n573:         return null;\n574:     }\n575: \n576:     /**\n577:      * Runs or queues any setting migrations needed.\n578:      */\n579:     public static runMigrations(): void {\n580:         // Dev notes: to add your migration, just add a new `migrateMyFeature` function, call it, and\n581:         // add a comment to note when it can be removed.\n582: \n583:         SettingsStore.migrateHiddenReadReceipts(); // Can be removed after October 2022.\n584:     }\n585: \n586:     private static migrateHiddenReadReceipts(): void {\n587:         if (MatrixClientPeg.get().isGuest()) return; // not worth it\n588: \n589:         // We wait for the first sync to ensure that the user's existing account data has loaded, as otherwise\n590:         // getValue() for an account-level setting like sendReadReceipts will return `null`.\n591:         const disRef = dispatcher.register((payload: ActionPayload) => {\n592:             if (payload.action === \"MatrixActions.sync\") {\n593:                 dispatcher.unregister(disRef);\n594: \n595:                 const rrVal = SettingsStore.getValue(\"sendReadReceipts\", null, true);\n596:                 if (typeof rrVal !== \"boolean\") {\n597:                     // new setting isn't set - see if the labs flag was.
